It finds that user-level `Edit(./docs/**)` allow rules were dead because they resolve relative to `~/.claude/`, removes Bash allowlist entries that enabled arbitrary execution through `find -exec`, `awk system()`, `env bash -c`, and `curl -sS -X POST`, blocks `--dangerously-skip-permissions`, and fixes a statusline that always showed $0 by switching to the documented cost field. The test plan records JSON rendering, shfmt, a statusline sample, and `chezmoi apply` plus `chezmoi verify` on the real machine.

### [Malformed HTTP requests get real status lines in CPython](https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/152980) — Serhiy Storchaka / CPython
A merged CPython PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 fixes an old `http.server` edge case where malformed or unsupported request lines could be answered in bare HTTP/0.9 style, leaving clients with raw HTML and no visible 400 or 505 status. The patch narrows HTTP/0.9 handling to valid two-word GET requests, adds regression coverage in `Lib/test/test_httpservers.py`, and was merged to `main` before Miss Islington opened 3.13, 3.14, and 3.15 backports.

### [Polis traces a Delphi group-label swap to cluster reordering](https://github.com/compdemocracy/polis/pull/2589) — Julien Cornebise / compdemocracy
Polis has an open Fable co-authored fix for a Delphi parity bug where Python re-sorted group clusters by size and reassigned ids, while the legacy Clojure path kept first-encounter order. The PR ties the root cause to a June 11 S3-4 trace where Python `g0` matched Clojure `g1` with identical membership, removes the size re-sort, and pins the invariant with a synthetic smaller-group-first test. It also records the test-gate impact: 9 of 11 D8 finalize-stats variants lifted from xfail, biodiversity cold-start matching Clojure exactly for D9/D10, and residual divergences explicitly reclassified away from the label-swap cause.

### [A static Swiss property dashboard ships with data refresh plumbing](https://github.com/fe19/fe19.github.io/pull/3) — fe19
Fable co-authored a front-end-only Swiss property statistics tool added to an existing static site. The PR builds canton, city, and property-type filters; stat tiles; sortable result tables; Chart.js price-per-square-meter and listing charts; CSV export and re-import; and a bundled snapshot dataset covering all 26 cantons plus major cities. A second commit adds a GitHub Actions workflow and Node refresh script for daily updates from BFS IMPI, so the artifact is not just a hand-entered demo table.

### [A CLI is split into testable Effect layers](https://github.com/peeranat-dan/chanom/pull/8) — peeranat-dan
The `chanom` CLI has an open Fable co-authored refactor that turns a process-heavy command implementation into explicit layers: pure domain helpers, Effect services for prompting, process execution, Git, and package installation, command-specific logic modules, and filesystem utilities over `FileSystem` and `Path`. The branch is large — roughly 3,000 additions across command modules and tests — but the PR description is specific about the architectural boundary: `apply` now returns an updated package object instead of mutating, `cli.ts` owns dispatch and exit-code mapping, and invalid `package.json` input becomes a typed `PkgInvalid` error instead of an unstructured defect.

### [Private preview links fail closed and keep their ports](https://github.com/necorox-com/kotoji/pull/5) — necorox
Kotoji merged a Fable co-authored fix for a preview workflow that was handing users raw private draft URLs even though drafts require a signed grant, and whose backend URL builder dropped nonstandard ports. The patch shares a `PreviewOpenButton`, routes BranchBar private drafts through the grant URL, and changes `urlFor` to inherit scheme and port from `controlBaseUrl`. The test plan is concrete: backend vet/test/build, frontend typecheck/lint/21 Vitest cases, real-browser E2E showing `:18080/?kpt=` opens the draft, and a raw preview URL without the grant returns 404.

### [A one-line Jest change cuts a suite from 144 seconds to 20](https://github.com/disclaimer8/flightfinder/pull/477) — Denys Kolomiiets / flightfinder
In `flightfinder`, a Fable co-authored PR removes `--runInBand` from the server Jest command after checking that each worker process gets its own in-memory SQLite database. The change is only one line, but the measured effect is large: the full suite is reported green in parallel with 8,375 tests passing, local runtime drops from 144 seconds to 20 seconds, and CI should lose roughly three minutes. It is a useful reminder that agentic review can find stale safety constraints as well as application bugs.

### [AsyncIterator hot paths get measured 16–36% wins](https://github.com/jeswr/AsyncIterator/pull/5) — Ruben Taelman / AsyncIterator
Ruben Taelman opened a Fable-generated performance PR for the `asynciterator` library that targets two measured hotspots: per-item `listenerCount('data')` checks in flow mode and promise-executor allocation in ES async iteration. On a noisy two-core Linux host, paired runs showed 200k-item `for await` wall time dropping from 61.3ms to 51.4ms with CPU ratio 0.64, a 1M-item data-flow drain moving from 137.0ms to 114.9ms, and generator wrapping from 62.4ms to 52.4ms. The PR keeps the listener-waiting path and error semantics intact, reports the full suite passing in both scheduler modes with 100% coverage, and explicitly flags the one behavior change around re-attaching `data` listeners.

### [Emergency-stop state machine reaches 30 green scenario oracles](https://github.com/tritinpho/emergency-stop-warning-system/pull/5) — tritinpho / emergency-stop-warning-system
A Fable-authored emergency-stop warning-system PR turns the project’s doc-07 scenario catalogue into executable tests and moves the board from 8 passing, 1 xfail, and 21 todo scenarios to 30 passing. The implementation covers occlusion policy, sensor-mode fallbacks, stale-signal watchdogs, fail-safe sign readback, operator override expiry, congestion suppression, OTA deferral, drift monitoring, and alarm de-duplication. The author notes the code stays in a MicroPython-safe subset for K230 hardware, while leaving K230 YOLO timing spikes, unix-port CI, and hardware handoffs outside this PR.

### [Compacted media context gets a recall digest](https://github.com/pungggi/pi-multimodal-proxy/pull/17) — pungggi / pi-multimodal-proxy
pi-multimodal-proxy has a Fable co-authored PR for a failure mode in long multimodal sessions: compaction kept persisted image and video descriptions in the session, but removed the user messages and fences that the context handler knew how to annotate. The fix detects compacted branches, reinjects a bounded recall digest after the compaction summary, keys it to stable image IDs, and switches to lean budgets after overflow-recovery compactions so the digest itself does not trigger a second overflow. It adds helpers and unit tests for video-description discovery, truncation, digest construction, and prompt-editor autocomplete, while documenting that steering the summarizer through `session_before_compact` was verified not to work in pi 0.79.8 or 0.80.3.

### [MoGen Studio gets a loopback remote-control dashboard](https://github.com/krazyjakee/MoGen/pull/96) — Jake Lee / MoGen
MoGen Studio’s Fable co-authored PR adds an embedded `tiny_http` server that serves a self-contained browser dashboard for the live desktop session. The dashboard mirrors open tabs, active source, diagnostics with line jumps, scene stats, and an orbiting viewport preview, then can push edits, saves, recompiles, and GLB builds back through the normal desktop pipeline so undo history stays coherent. The 1,698-line branch keeps remote access off by default, loopback-only by default, clamps the port, adds tests for state snapshots and settings behavior, and documents the new preferences and security warning.

### [Halo match tracker closes five review-found failure modes](https://github.com/Hysterically/MCC-Halo-3-Custom-Games-Tracker/pull/5) — Hysterically / MCC-Halo-3-Custom-Games-Tracker
A merged Fable co-authored MCC Halo 3 Custom Games Tracker PR fixes the top findings from a code review rather than adding a new feature. The fixes make failed local ingests retry instead of silently losing finished matches, contain Discord startup failures that had killed the whole tracker, recover from deleted cached webhooks, make the destructive clear command dry-run by default, and stop 2v2/FFA games from being scored against retired leaderboard boards. The change is small — 109 additions across five TypeScript files — but each item is tied to a concrete operational failure mode in match posting or leaderboard accounting.

### [A hardware MIDI sequencer grows scale lock, ratchets, and full synth controls](https://github.com/Leeman1982/gm_sam/pull/3) — Leeman1982 / gm_sam
A Fable co-authored `gm_sam` PR expands an Arduino-style SAM2695 MIDI sequencer into a much broader hardware instrument surface. It adds 16-scale lock with root and degree-walking entry, per-step ratchets, per-track direction/divider/transpose/humanize controls, GS SysEx reverb and chorus parameters, keypad performance controls, macro pots with soft pickup, and a reorganized OLED control scheme. The author reports host-side simulated-time engine tests passing for note pairing, ratchets, scale quantization, reverse playback, and dividers across a 1,483-addition branch.

### [Twitch chat performance gets a full hot-path pass](https://github.com/luke-h1/foam/pull/689) — Luke Howsam / foam
foam has an open Fable-authored performance review for a Twitch client that attacks both chat and app-startup hot paths. The PR says badge lookup had been linearly scanning a flattened Chatterino list of about 3,500 entries for nearly every chatter; the fix caches badge maps by array identity, avoids code-point expansion unless surrogate pairs are present, caches global emote and badge fetches for an hour, defers 3,200 emoji objects off the first-paint path, restores React Query structural sharing, batches profile-picture requests, and right-sizes Twitch thumbnails. The eight Fable commits touch 27 files with 696 additions and 230 deletions, and include targeted test updates around the chat-row renderer and channel-load path.

### [Photo measurements become model ground truth](https://github.com/Graphicaljerry/3DRenderapp/pull/5) — Graphicaljerry / 3DRenderapp
3DRenderapp merged a Fable-authored measurement tool for a replacement-part workflow where the user can drag a line over a credit card, coin, AA battery, or custom reference in a photo, then mark the part dimensions in real millimetres. Those measurements are inserted into the prompt as ground truth, while the guided flow also runs web dimension lookup for named products before the vision call. A second Fable commit applied adversarial-review fixes for stale paste handling, image-change resets, duplicate labels after deletes, labels beyond Z, and cancelled pointer drags; the PR reports real headless-browser verification in light and dark mode plus label-logic unit checks.

### [An SRE agent gets durable memory and pre-approval rails](https://github.com/arun-8687/SupportAgent/pull/10) — arun-8687 / SupportAgent
SupportAgent has an open Fable-heavy branch that builds a LangGraph incident-response package around Service Bus intake, Azure Functions, subagent fan-out, root-cause analysis, permission gates, human approval, skill execution, verification, and knowledge capture. Later Fable commits harden the system with alert deduplication, storm suppression, strict production mode, prompt-injection fencing, structured Application Insights telemetry, skill and custom-agent frontmatter, token-budgeted prompts, app-scoped known-error pre-approvals with circuit breakers, and hybrid pgvector plus exact-keyword retrieval. The PR is large, but its commit trail is concrete: tests rise through 24, 60, 93, 133, and 148 passing with one optional pgvector integration skip, and live checks are reported for pre-approved actions and semantic incident recall.

### [Pentest reports get a plain-English web normalizer](https://github.com/amezoii/PT/pull/1) — amezoii / PT
PT has an open Fable-authored full-stack app that turns technical penetration-test reports into client-readable summaries. The Node/Express app adds bcrypt email login, per-user SQLite-backed reports and sessions, upload parsing for PDF, DOCX, TXT/Markdown, XML `.nessus`, Burp, and JSON scanner exports, structured Claude rewriting into plain-language severity, impact, analogies, fixes, glossary, and next steps, plus Playwright PDF output and an on-screen report view. The PR adds 2,854 lines across the server, parsers, report generator, routes, views, and package setup, with the Fable session recorded in the commit trailer.

### [A Matomo SPA reset stops deleting custom dimension 1](https://github.com/felix-berlin/astro-matomo/pull/82) — Felix Scholze / astro-matomo
astro-matomo has an open Fable-authored fix for a subtle analytics data-loss bug in its SPA mode. The `astro:after-swap` handler had always pushed `["deleteCustomDimension", 1]`, so any user-defined custom dimension with id 1 was wiped after the first view transition even though the Matomo SPA guide did not call for that hardcoded reset. The PR removes the unconditional deletion, adds an opt-in `viewTransition.deleteCustomDimensions` setting for page-scoped dimensions, makes `contentElement` optional in the object form, and ties the change directly to issue #62.

### [A copy-paste option bug toggled remote lookups](https://github.com/jeswr/Components.js/pull/5) — Jesse Wright / Components.js
Jesse Wright staged a Fable-generated Components.js fix for a one-line copy-paste bug with outsized behavior. `ComponentsManagerBuilder` assigned `Boolean(options.typeChecking)` to `this.remoteContextLookups` whenever `remoteContextLookups` was defined, so `remoteContextLookups: true` with `typeChecking: false` silently disabled remote lookup, while `remoteContextLookups: false` with default type checking silently enabled the network-access path. The PR changes the assignment and adds a regression test pinning the two options as independent, with the author noting it was generated by Claude Fable 5 for review before upstreaming and the full suite still at 100% coverage.

### [A circuit-simulator cross-check traces a 50 mV solver offset](https://github.com/NyanCAD/Cadnip.jl/pull/208) — Pepijn de Vos / Cadnip.jl
Cadnip has an open Fable-authored fix for a roughly 1e-4 relative-L2 mismatch against VACASK on a diode-voltage-multiplier benchmark. The PR traces the error to DC Newton termination on an absolute residual: weakly conducting floating diode-chain nodes could stop about 50 mV below the driven 50 V level, while VACASK and ngspice landed at 50.000000 V and 50.000058 V. A follow-up Fable commit found a second source of bias, where anchoring Verilog-A internal nodes to ground leaked about 50 pA per node; the branch adds a voltage-update Newton polish, anchors internal gmin to the first terminal instead, and pins the case with a floating-chain regression test.

### [Ruled visa-form pages become structured Q&A tables](https://github.com/instill-ai/models/pull/102) — Ping-Lin Chang / instill-ai
Instill AI has an open Fable co-authored Docling PR for a real filled UK visa application whose visually similar ruled pages were sometimes parsed as proper tables and sometimes as loose text with answer-before-question merges such as “Yu-Wei Given names.” The new `form_qa.py` pass rebuilds two-column form tables from PyMuPDF line geometry, marks question columns as row headers, reclassifies non-TOC `document_index` tables, and stitches wrapped questions across page continuations into a logical form group. The author reports that the visa form now yields 19 tables with 17 chained, 0 `document_index` leftovers, 0 garbled merges, and 0 token loss, while an academic paper fixture is untouched and 14 tests pin the behavior.

### [A fuel-management app closes a fleet-wide export hole](https://github.com/yohan114/Fuel-System-V2/pull/5) — yohan114 / Fuel-System-V2
Fuel-System-V2 has an open Fable-heavy PR that closes several pre-launch security findings before wiring the app into a broader equipment-and-construction portal. The current security gate requires a session on `GET /api/reports/export/xlsx`, which had been reachable through a proxy bypass without handler auth; it also makes the `TEST_ENV` admin bypass unreachable in production, prefers a system-specific `FUEL_AUTH_SECRET`, and removes a hardcoded seed admin password. Later Fable commits add read-only portal endpoints for health, KPI summary, canonical machines/projects, and month-scoped fuel-cost and invoice-income events, while the older commit trail shows substantial fuel analytics and service-record imports with unit tests and type-check gates.

### [Hugo .Render learns nested layout lookup](https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/pull/15091) — Bjørn Erik Pedersen / Hugo
Hugo’s maintainer opened a Fable co-authored fix for `.Render "foo/mylayout"`, making it search `foo` subdirectories along the page layout path instead of only flat layout names. The change is small — 191 additions across `page.go`, the template store, and tests — but it closes a real template-resolution issue and carries a co-author trailer for Joe Mooring as well as Claude Fable 5. The PR is still open, with the behavior pinned in `hugolib/page_test.go` rather than left as a doc-only interpretation.

### [Graph node clicks stop blanking the whole app](https://github.com/samibel/graphi/pull/40) — samibel / Graphi
Graphi used Fable on a UI failure that came from a specific Sigma v3 mismatch: node clicks in compare-off mode converted citation edges to a `dashed` type even though only `line` and `arrow` edge programs were registered, throwing the renderer and unmounting the app to a white screen. The fix keeps citation edges as styled lines, adds a graph error boundary, makes node positions deterministic instead of `Math.random()` on every SSE refresh, labels nodes and edge kinds, and repairs edge-click lookup keys. The PR reports an embedded-UI Playwright run covering load, click, highlight rendering, and no page errors.

### [Graph1x hardens its 1.0 surface with measured allocation wins](https://github.com/lgamorim/graph1x/pull/5) — Luís Amorim / Graph1x
Graph1x has a large Fable co-authored road-to-1.0 PR that stays focused on documentation, interop, API stability, and performance rather than new graph algorithms. The branch adds a DocFX site, reflection-free JSON round-tripping, cancellation-token overloads for 17 long-running entry points, PublicApiAnalyzers over a 476-symbol surface, and AOT compatibility verified by a full-trim consumer. Its benchmarked allocation pass reports BFS path memory down 18% and time down 25%, Dijkstra corner-to-corner allocations down 36%, and connected-components allocations down 25%.

### [Mac brightness sliders resync with real hardware state](https://github.com/VenkateshDas/pulse/pull/43) — Venkatesh Das / Pulse
Pulse used Fable on two concrete macOS brightness bugs: hardware changes from media keys, Control Center, or auto-brightness did not flow back into the app’s slider state, and writing the same value through both user-scale and linear DisplayServices APIs could turn a requested 0.78 into a measured 0.91 user brightness. The fix registers per-display brightness-change notifications, initializes the observer at launch, drops echoes from Pulse’s own writes with a half-second guard, and stops calling `SetLinearBrightness` except at the scale’s shared endpoint. The PR is only 74 additions across four files, but the body explains the Swift 6 isolation constraint and the user-visible drift it repairs.

### [Taylor models land with thesis examples as regression tests](https://github.com/andreapasquale94/tax/pull/31) — Andrea Pasquale / tax
A Claude Fable 5-authored tax PR adds a header-only C++ implementation of Makino-style Taylor models for rigorous remainder bounds, then extends it with a quadratic range bounder, ODE-integration primitives, and Eigen compatibility. The branch is explicit about the math it is pinning: outward-rounded interval arithmetic, domain-checked intrinsics, polynomial-excess folding into remainders, Picard-step helpers, and abstract constants so Eigen’s generated zero and one literals can safely adopt a model domain. The tests include interval and intrinsic containment sweeps plus thesis worked examples from sections 4.15/4.16, Tables 4.2 and 4.3, enclosure functions, a verified double integral, a harmonic-oscillator integration check, and Eigen matrix products.

### [An archaeology outline gets an eighty-six-reference audit](https://github.com/Rui3012/xujianian-network-analysis/pull/1) — Rui3012 / xujianian-network-analysis
A Fable-authored academic review of `Theoretical_Framework_Outline_Rui_Bai_v4.docx` checked all 86 references for bibliographic accuracy and claim support. The audit found no fabricated references but identified six citation errors, a Barton 2014 miscitation as agent-simulation precedent, and three overstatements of Trubitt 2000’s “oscillation” claim. A follow-up commit turns the audit into a revised v5 outline: fixing the bibliography, replacing Barton with Turner & Penn 2002 in Table 1, adding a methods-and-metrics note, adding 17 verified references, and documenting the changes in `docs/V5_changelog.md`.

### [Harmonies scoring is implemented as a pure tested engine](https://github.com/ducktapegirl/game-scorer/pull/1) — ducktapegirl / game-scorer
A Fable-authored first milestone for `game-scorer` builds a reusable TypeScript core for board-game scoring before any UI work. The PR defines generic board state, cells, topologies, token definitions, config schemas, graph helpers, and a Harmonies module with both official board sides — 23 and 25 hexes — plus stacking validation and scoring for trees, mountains, fields, buildings, and water. It includes 76 Vitest cases, including edge cases called out in the PR such as a lone height-1 gray satisfying mountain adjacency and looping or branching rivers measured end-to-end rather than by token count.

### [A Marimo climatology dashboard gets browser tests before deploy](https://github.com/gulfofmaine/climatology_py_dash/pull/82) — Alex Kerney / climatology_py_dash
The Gulf of Maine climatology dashboard added Fable co-authored end-to-end coverage around its Marimo pages rather than relying on imports and unit tests alone. The PR creates a pixi test environment with pytest-playwright, browser smoke tests for all five pages plus sidebar navigation and `/health`, and a GitHub Actions job that runs the suite against the built Docker image before deployment. The same change fixes a Bandit pre-commit exclude regex that had never matched the repo-relative `tests/` path it was supposed to skip.

### [Alpha-gal syndrome gets a hypothesis-adjudicated disease model](https://github.com/monarch-initiative/dismech/pull/5398) — Chris Mungall / Monarch Initiative
Monarch’s dismech project has an open Fable-authored disease-modeling PR for alpha-gal syndrome that goes beyond filling in a template. The branch adds the core causal chain for tick-bite sensitization, anti-alpha-gal IgE, delayed anaphylaxis, and cetuximab hypersensitivity, then records competing mechanistic hypotheses with primary-literature evidence. Follow-up Fable commits run openscientist searches over 51- and 46-paper sets, promoting the tick-salivary model to canonical and deprecating residual blood-meal and microorganism explanations with explicit refuting PMIDs and caveats.

### [A railway contingency map is rebuilt around real track geometry](https://github.com/nh9qxw5shd-oss/ContingencyMap/pull/1) — nh9qxw5shd-oss / ContingencyMap
ContingencyMap has an open Fable-authored rebuild that turns a hardcoded corridor demo into a self-administered full-screen railway map. The app now includes passcode-gated admin CRUD, data-driven scenarios, migrated plans with 138 steps, Supabase RLS, and section drawing that traces railway geometry instead of freehand approximations. A later Fable commit replaces the OpenRailwayMap-dependent tracing path with local Network Rail Track Model assets, ELR and mileage search, and on-demand tiled centre-line snapping.

### [A synth app fixes real MIDI corruption and grows a native audio stack](https://github.com/Ranzlappen/Synth-piano-web/pull/55) — Ranzlappen / Synth-piano-web
Synth-piano-web has an open Fable-authored PR that starts with a concrete hardware bug: the old USB MIDI parser silently corrupted real-device input when messages were fragmented, used running status, included real-time bytes, or crossed SysEx boundaries. The branch rewrites parsing, adds end-to-end pitch bend, fixes a recorder race that dropped notes immediately after record-start, and stores the live metronome BPM instead of hardcoding 120. The same Fable session then expands the audio engine with PolyBLEP band-limited waveforms, stereo FX, Oboe-backed DJ decks, waveform seek strips, EQ, loops, VU meters, and JVM tests around parser, recorder, controller, and waveform behavior.

### [Image-supplier metadata is measured, reviewed, and exported as GS1-style CSV](https://github.com/singhwanderer/v0-image-upload-enhancement/pull/31) — singhwanderer / v0-image-upload-enhancement
v0-image-upload-enhancement has an open Fable co-authored UX and implementation branch aimed at keeping supplier image data intact all the way to retailer handoff. After a code-level audit of the richness funnel, the branch measures image dimensions and DPI from file bytes rather than asking users to type shared values, splits product-wide attributes from per-shot truth fields, warns before discarding pending AI suggestions, validates filenames and vocabulary against the 21-field spec, and exports a real CSV in spec column order. The PR reports Playwright checks for measured 640/480/300 metadata, pending-suggestion review, post-submit AI extraction, and data-richness meter changes.

### [A design pass removes seventy-one broken publication cards](https://github.com/sajor2000/riccc_site_rush/pull/5) — sajor2000 / riccc_site_rush
A merged Fable-authored design pass on riccc_site_rush found that 71 of 224 publication entries were PubMed efetch placeholders with “Metadata unavailable,” no DOI, no authors, and no year. Because those rows grouped under “Unknown,” the publications page opened with the broken cards before real papers. The fix filters unusable metadata in the publication merge and snapshot paths, regenerates the content file to 153 real publications with zero junk rows, sorts any remaining unknown-year group last, and raises contrast on the team-avatar initials fallback.

### [A torrent-to-mpv playback layer lands with race-detector fixes](https://github.com/lexxbyte/shibui/pull/5) — lexxbyte / shibui
shibui merged a Fable-linked Phase 4 PR that adds the app’s torrent engine and detached mpv playback layer. The branch introduces an anacrolix-backed torrent client, file picking by extension, quality, and size, sequential stream copy with backpressure timeouts, cache-path slugging, an mpv spawner, JSON IPC client, graceful quit ordering, and resume helpers. The PR also records reviewer fixes after the worker left player tests truncated: missing graceful/resume tests were completed, a race-detector hit was removed with an atomic timeout setter, lint was updated for Go 1.25, and the reported local gate covered fmt, lint, vet, race tests, vulncheck, build, and CI.

### [MIDI timing and real-time safety checks land in an offline audio harness](https://github.com/Lifted-Truck/Auricle/pull/5) — Julian Smith / Auricle
Auricle’s Fable co-authored PR closes several Phase 0 harness items for an offline audio engine. It adds a framework-free Standard MIDI File reader for format 0 and 1 files, verifies a two-note tempo-map fixture at exact 0, 0.5, 1.0, and 1.5 second boundaries, validates runtime presets with a pinned JSON-schema validator, and adds a macOS dyld interpose leg that counts pthread locks and sleeps inside a guarded real-time scope. A follow-up Fable commit fixed a Windows CI encoding failure, and the PR wires macOS and Windows builds plus the Layer-0 CTest suite into GitHub Actions.

### [A markdown knowledge-base tool drops git subprocesses from walks](https://github.com/zby/commonplace/pull/19) — Zbigniew Łukasiak / commonplace
commonplace used Fable to replace per-path `git check-ignore` calls with a package-owned visibility contract: hidden entries and nested repos stay invisible, build and vendor trees are skipped, and collection walks still exclude reserved `dir-index.md` files. The PR says enumeration on the repo fell from about five seconds of subprocess churn to a plain filesystem walk around 0.02 seconds, while making tracked-but-gitignored notes visible again where the old `--no-index` check had hidden them. The same branch includes Fable review fixes for URL-prefix deduplication, CRLF frontmatter parsing, string-valued tags, snapshot truncation disclosure, relocation helpers, and packaging metadata, with targeted tests added around the failure cases.

### [Home Assistant Hikvision fixes are tied to four open integration issues](https://github.com/ptarjan/home-assistant/pull/8) — Paul Tarjan / Home Assistant fork
A Fable co-authored Home Assistant PR bundles concrete Hikvision integration fixes rather than generic modernization. It deduplicates `(sensor type, channel)` pairs to address duplicate entity IDs, marks entities unavailable while the pyHik event stream is down, avoids camera snapshots from unreachable devices so Hikvision hardware does not enter illegal-access lockout, exposes human/vehicle detection targets, and skips noisy video-loss watchdog events. The patch also bumps pyHik to 0.4.3 and adds Hikvision binary-sensor and camera tests covering the new behavior.

### [Workout cards stop advertising sessions they will not start](https://github.com/Joshfkon/workout-app/pull/383) — Joshfkon / workout-app
workout-app’s Fable co-authored fix follows a subtle product-state mismatch across three commits. After progression switched from calendar weekdays to total-completed-session slots, the mesocycle card and Log launcher could show Upper A while Start actually launched Upper B; later review found stale programs with deleted or renamed exercises and regeneration flows could create the same mismatch. The branch shares the start-path slot-resolution logic with the display path, checks whether stored program exercises still resolve before advertising them, refreshes the card after regeneration, and adds regression tests in `startMesocycleSession.test.ts`.

### [WiFiSentinel closes its Android scan E2E and telemetry-import gaps](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/wifisentinel/pull/132) — Ismael Martinez / WiFiSentinel
WiFiSentinel’s Fable-authored PR turns a documented Android testing gap into an instrumented Compose test: it grants scan permission, taps through the flow, waits for the pipeline, asserts that a result renders, and verifies a Room history row appears. The same change maps phone `latencyMs` into the CLI import schema, makes latency and download subfields individually optional so latency-only scans do not pretend a speed test ran, and updates terminal, HTML, dashboard, and net-engineer outputs for partial measurements. A follow-up review commit seeds permission state from `checkSelfPermission`, replaces a Room polling loop with a suspending collection, adds schema rejection tests for empty measurement shells, and adds an adaptive launcher icon.

### [A BASIC web IDE starts a standalone player and share API path](https://github.com/seanhodges/basically/pull/183) — Sean Hodges / basically
basically used Fable to start a multi-stage standalone player for its browser BASIC environment. The first commit adds dialect-specific short routes such as `/run/:id`, `/load/:id`, and `/chain/:id`, switches the app shell based on `location.pathname`, makes asset URLs safe under nested player paths, and copies `index.html` to `404.html` so GitHub Pages can cold-load deep links. A second Fable commit adds a typed share API client with size and name prechecks, status-specific errors for 404, 410, 429, and 400 responses, malformed-response validation, an unconfigured short-circuit when `VITE_SHARE_API_URL` is absent, and tests around those cases.

### [FFXIV trigger state is seeded from cached plugin events](https://github.com/CateDesu/NyaaTriggers/pull/5) — Cate / NyaaTriggers
NyaaTriggers merged a Fable-authored Windows fix for a timing bug in Final Fantasy XIV overlay setups: connecting after the game and ACT/IINACT were already running left the app without the player's name or current zone until the next zone transition. The PR traces the issue to cached ChangePrimaryPlayer and ChangeZone events that were replayed to new subscribers but only partly consumed, then adds a `primary_player` signal, shared zone-application logic, and safeguards so empty replays cannot overwrite saved names. The result restores You-scoped status triggers and zone-gated timelines for the common startup order, with the change kept to `ws_client.py`, `main_window.py`, docs, and a changelog.

### [Fable writes six reusable Claude Code skills](https://www.iwoszapar.com/p/claude-code-skills-written-by-a-smarter-model) — Iwo Szapar
Iwo Szapar used Fable 5's included-access window to write six Claude Code skills for the model he runs day to day, then blind-tested them on Opus 4.8. He reports 12 wins, 0 losses, and 2 ties under a grader that did not know which output used a skill, and published the prompts, rubric, run protocol, and skill files as a reusable "Skill Distillation Kit." The useful twist is portability: because `SKILL.md` is just markdown procedure, the same files can also be copied into Codex CLI and Gemini CLI rather than staying locked to Claude Code.

### [A badminton tracker ships reflection logging and export in one Fable pass](https://github.com/sirarthur2479/BadmintonApp/pull/5) — sirarthur2479 / BadmintonApp
A merged Fable-authored BadmintonApp PR turns a session logger into a fuller training journal across 4,102 additions and 522 deletions. The commit series follows an explicit red-green-refactor trail through seven tasks: drills move from comma strings to JSON arrays with a v2 migration, sessions gain goals and reflection answers, users can edit old sessions and custom drill tags, goal achievement appears in cards and charts, and sessions can be exported to Markdown with a share sheet. The PR adds focused Flutter tests across models, provider state, database migration, log-session UI, export service and screen, star ratings, session cards, and the new goal chart.

### [sqlite-utils 4.0rc2 ships after thirty-seven Fable prompts](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jul/5/sqlite-utils-fable/) — Simon Willison
Simon Willison used Claude Code with Fable to push `sqlite-utils` from 4.0rc1 toward a stable major release, starting from a final review prompt aimed at catching changes that would be painful after SemVer lock-in. Fable found five release blockers, including a `delete_where()` transaction bug that could leave the connection poisoned so a delete, a later insert, and even a new table disappeared after close. Willison reports 37 prompts, 34 commits, and +1,321/-190 changes across 30 files, with the session estimated at $149.25 unsubsidized; a later GPT-5.5 review found two more SQLite transaction edge cases that Fable then reproduced and fixed.

### [Kubernetes cost groups stop merging across namespaces](https://github.com/samn/gke-cost-analyzer/pull/35) — samn / gke-cost-analyzer
A Fable-authored gke-cost-analyzer PR fixes a cost-accounting bug where pods in different namespaces but with identical labels collapsed into one group, and the recorded namespace depended on whichever pod the Kubernetes API returned first. The branch makes namespace part of the grouping identity, brings the history table and sparkline queries back into agreement, and then follows through on related accounting issues: BigQuery insert-id dedup collisions, native sidecar resource requests, standard-mode capacity math, denominator selection, and SKU matching. The open PR spans command, history, record, setup, and test files rather than a cosmetic report change.

### [An Android updater drops reqwest after event-loop panics](https://github.com/niltonperimneto/neocalc-android/pull/4) — niltonperimneto / neocalc-android
neocalc-android has an open Fable-authored updater rewrite prompted by real crashes from `reqwest`'s hidden Tokio runtime and rustls setup on Android. The new path uses `ureq`, shields every exported FFI function from panics, adds timeouts and exponential-backoff retries, resumes interrupted APK downloads with HTTP Range and `Content-Range` validation, and reports real progress through a UniFFI callback instead of faking 0-to-100%. It also tightens the security model with HTTPS and host allowlists across redirects, mandatory Minisign plus SHA-256 verification, response-size caps, staged `.part` files, and CalVer rollback protection.

### [Bitfinex candle tests pin an awkward field order](https://github.com/GBrooks1970/bfx-ws-screenplay/pull/5) — GBrooks1970 / bfx-ws-screenplay
A merged Fable co-authored Screenplay/Cypress PR adds Bitfinex candles-channel coverage with the kind of detail that prevents well-intentioned regressions. The schema explicitly encodes Bitfinex candles as `[MTS, OPEN, CLOSE, HIGH, LOW, VOLUME]`, with `CLOSE` at index 2 instead of the conventional OHLC slot, and treats the key-only subscription ack as its own confirmation shape. The author reports typecheck and lint clean, three consecutive local 19/19 runs, a PR smoke run, and three dispatched extended-suite runs on the branch.

### [CJK transcript rows stop hard-wrapping at column zero](https://github.com/DylanDDeng/bubble/pull/54) — DylanDDeng / bubble
Bubble has an open Fable-authored terminal-rendering fix for a narrow but user-visible wrapping bug. Transcript rows reserved two columns of horizontal padding, but the text wrapper only subtracted the timeline gutter, so dense CJK text could be handed a budget two cells wider than the real row and force the terminal to wrap the last glyph onto a stray line at column zero. The patch names the padding constant next to the Ink `paddingX` prop and adds an end-to-end regression test that renders CJK list items and paragraphs across a width sweep while asserting every physical row fits the terminal.

### [A nomination form gets a real browser-to-database test](https://github.com/jackvdp/icps-launchpad/pull/5) — jackvdp / icps-launchpad
icps-launchpad has a Fable co-authored Playwright test that exercises a real awards nomination flow rather than mocks. The test starts the local app, fills all five form steps in Chromium, submits through `/api/nominations`, verifies the MongoDB record directly, sends the same Postmark confirmation and admin emails as production, and deletes the record afterward unless `KEEP_E2E_NOMINATION=1` is set. The PR deliberately keeps this out of the Vercel build gate because it needs real credentials and sends real email; the author reports a local full run passing in about 22 seconds.

### [A memory benchmark swings from 17% to 73% with scaffolding](https://x.com/ST4RHaze/status/2073875623995683060) — StarHaze
StarHaze posted a systems-level Fable 5 report around a memory benchmark where the same model scored 73% in one setup and 17% in another. The claimed difference was not the base model but the harness: a plain state file read at session start and updated before stop, long-running sessions instead of five-minute trials, and a separate verifier subagent because models tend to prefer their own existing reasoning when self-grading. The post frames self-improvement as an agent-system property rather than a raw-model property, with Fable doing best when allowed to run for days against persistent state.

### [An SSH tool becomes a fleet dashboard with live server stats](https://x.com/arshadkazmi42/status/2073868384882864399) — Arshad Kazmi
Arshad Kazmi reported using Fable 5 to evolve his `termi` workflow from per-server setup into a centralized server-management platform. The new version lets users add a server with an IP plus SSH key or password, open a terminal instantly, and view an analytics dashboard with fleet-wide CPU, memory, disk, uptime, offline-server, and active-session stats. His screenshots show multiple live servers and a demo video, while the post notes that multiple Claude sessions can run in parallel across machines to continue improving the open-source app.

### [A one-line stock prompt becomes an adversarial research committee](https://x.com/sentisenseapp/status/2073859895326331037) — SentiSense
SentiSense gave Fable 5 a one-line request for “a skill to analyze a stock” and says it produced a five-persona adversarial investment committee rather than a simple checklist. The design has a quality owner, story checker, inverter, macro trader, and forensic short-seller researching independently, feeding a shared evidence ledger, and debating toward a verdict under a “No source, no claim” rule. Their point was not just that the model knew finance vocabulary, but that it built a reusable structured workflow where the evidence ledger constrains the debate.

### [A Bedrock MCP server learns Fable-aware streaming and refusals](https://github.com/Neo2024365/bedrock-openai-experiments-chat/pull/5) — Neo2024365
A Fable-generated PR to `bedrock-openai-experiments-chat` brings the MCP server onto Bedrock’s Converse API for Anthropic models and makes `anthropic.claude-fable-5` the default model it advertises. The change preserves chunk-by-chunk local history writes during `ConverseStream`, falls back to single-shot `Converse` when a model cannot stream, and treats Bedrock’s `refusal` stop reason as a surfaced tool error rather than an empty response or retry. The mock E2E harness now speaks the Converse eventstream format, and the author reports all 13 E2E checks plus package and root typechecks passing.

### [Two playable games in a week, including a 79-check launch audit](https://x.com/brehm_shaun/status/2073874562375418234) — Shaun Brehm
Shaun Brehm posted a short playthrough of two games he says were built with Claude Fable 5 in one week, with no hand-written code from him. `Skywriter` went from idea to playable tycoon in one day, while `Underline` is described as a daily runner backed by a 79-check launch audit. The report is light on implementation internals, but the public playthrough and launch-check detail make it more concrete than a generic “vibe-coded a game” claim.

### [Command & Conquer: Generals runs natively on iPhone and iPad](https://github.com/ammaarreshi/Generals-Mac-iOS-iPad) — Ammaar Reshi / Generals-Mac-iOS-iPad
Ammaar Reshi published a Fable-assisted port of the real 2003 Command & Conquer: Generals — Zero Hour engine to Apple Silicon Macs, iPhone, and iPad, with no emulator and no bundled game assets. The README says campaign, skirmish, and Generals Challenge run through a DirectX 8 → DXVK → Vulkan → MoltenVK → Metal stack, with touch RTS controls for tap-select, drag-box selection, two-finger scrolling, and pinch zoom. The project documents the engineering work behind the iOS build: rerouting writable paths out of a signed app bundle, cross-building DXVK for iPhoneOS, pausing the render loop around iOS lifecycle grabs, and root-causing real-device bugs such as a black minimap alpha fallback and a stuck audio stream that muted EVA voice lines.

### [A SLAM fork review moves from twenty-four findings into navigation fixes](https://github.com/arihantb2/ORB_SLAM3/pull/18) — arihantb2 / ORB_SLAM3
Arihant Bansal opened a Fable co-authored ORB_SLAM3 fork review that starts as a structured static report with 4 critical, 2 high, 5 medium, and 6 low findings, then follows with fix commits instead of stopping at advice. The branch fixes a BRISK feature-cap desynchronization between keypoints and descriptors, MapPoint pool lifetime bugs including premature chunk frees and eager use-after-free, navigation orientation corruption from `Matrix::normalized()` misuse, and CSV/interpolation boundary bugs that silently dropped frames. The PR remains open, but it is already a code-review-to-patch trail across 24 changed files rather than a standalone checklist.

### [A PWA schema stamp bug is fixed before adding GitHub sync](https://github.com/gionin/steward/pull/5) — gionin / steward
steward merged a Fable co-authored fix for a web PWA failure where `storage.js` hardcoded `SCHEMA_VERSION = 2` even though three migrations existed. Every save wrote version 2 back into metadata, so the next `openDatabase` re-ran the v3 migration and crashed on a duplicate `ready_date` column after save and reload. The merged PR derives the version from the migration list, makes the v3 migration idempotent for already-damaged databases, and then layers in GitHub-repo sync for the desktop app and web PWA with the design recorded in the architecture notes.

### [A small agent framework gets a four-part orchestration design](https://github.com/ulvestuen/pi-kit/pull/5) — ulvestuen / pi-kit
pi-kit merged a Fable co-authored orchestration design that keeps the proposed agent system split into four standalone extensions instead of one monolith. The 596-line design adds a fleet runtime for subagents, a planner that emits task DAGs with lykkja-shaped criteria, a critic that independently scores checkpoints, and a thin orchestrator layer over the existing `pi.events` bus and session entries. The plan reuses lykkja as the goal-level quality gate and stopping rule while child sessions handle per-task loops.

### [A feedback agent starts clicking through demos before publishing claims](https://github.com/sbyoun/vibe-feedback-agent/pull/5) — sbyoun / vibe-feedback-agent
vibe-feedback-agent merged a Fable co-authored change that turns its demo pass from a loading check into actual browser exploration. The new first pass connects Playwright MCP, clicks CTAs, and attempts two to four core-flow steps; a verifier pass then replays the draft’s interaction claims in a headless browser before they are trusted. The same PR raises the queue/publish limit from 10 to 50 so normal operation can complete while still acting as a circuit breaker for change-detection storms, and keeps WebFetch only as a fallback when browser access fails.

### [A disk scanner stops counting firmlinked data twice](https://github.com/VenkateshDas/pulse/pull/45) — Venkatesh Das / Pulse
Pulse has a new Fable co-authored storage fix for a macOS-specific accounting bug. Its fast directory scanner recursed into `/System/Volumes/Data` through the `/System` firmlink without applying the existing pruned-path guard, so a 245 GB machine could show 206 GB of user data under “System.” The PR keeps the change to `StorageScanner.swift` and a storage regression test, pruning the firmlinked volume before the scanner double-counts it.

### [A PRD audit reconstructs seven project loops from git history](https://github.com/dwats250/cuttingboard/pull/114) — dwats250 / cuttingboard
cuttingboard has an open Fable-authored audit PR that turns repository history into a diagnosis artifact instead of another implementation pass. The first phase names seven PRD lifecycle loops, measures a 17.2% bookkeeping tax, identifies ledgers as the most-churned files, and records live-status verdicts around the project’s Codex gate history. Follow-up commits add adversarial verification and prescriptions in separate markdown artifacts, leaving the code untouched except for a reconciled `PROJECT_STATE.md`.

### [A science site gets font, CSP, media, and Lighthouse hardening](https://github.com/alexwelcing/lupine-science/pull/5) — alexwelcing / lupine-science
lupine-science merged a Fable-authored performance and hardening pass across its static site and deployment workflow. The PR self-hosts subset Newsreader and Plex Mono fonts at 194 KB total, replaces render-blocking Google Fonts, generates hash-locked CSP and cache headers, cuts the launch film from 3.7 MB to 2.2 MB, and removes an unused 1.3 MB hero asset. Later commits add MOF-5 crystallography content, article builders, JSON-LD and sitemap generation, deterministic-build checks, Lighthouse budgets, and an accessibility fix for invalid `aria-pressed` usage.

### [A fractal calculator becomes an Android APK build](https://github.com/remiverdet76-maker/Dreamt/pull/5) — remiverdet76-maker / Dreamt
Dreamt has an open Fable-authored Android calculator PR spanning 2,106 new lines. The branch builds a “Calc Fractale 991” app with a Casio-style scientific-calculator engine, fraction and π handling, 2D ratio plots, logarithmic and exponential graphing, and 3D visualizations for Platonic solids, torus, Möbius-style surfaces, and related ratio geometry. It also adds the Gradle project, launcher icons, Android manifest, WebView activity, and a GitHub Actions workflow that builds the APK after fixing an invalid YAML apostrophe.

## 2026-07-06

### [Fable finds forty-six valid issues in Opus-written production code](https://x.com/AntonMartyniuk/status/2073708572995641579) — Anton Martyniuk
Anton Martyniuk compared code-review runs on a production notification feature that had been written entirely with Claude Opus 4.8. In Ultracode mode, Fable 5 surfaced 57 issues against the same codebase, while Opus 4.8 found 18; after manual review, he counted 46 of Fable’s findings as legitimate bugs, performance problems, or security issues. The attached report screenshot shows a 737-line Markdown review grouped by severity, making this a concrete model-to-model review comparison rather than a reaction to release notes.

### [Honeycrisp adds Apple Notes tools without osascript](https://github.com/christianpatrick/honeycrisp/pull/5) — Christian Helvin / Honeycrisp
A merged Fable 5 co-authored Honeycrisp PR adds Apple Notes as a full connection: search, folder listing, read, note links, create, and append. The implementation reads NoteStore.sqlite directly, inflates gzipped protobuf note bodies, avoids trash and smart folders, treats locked notes as unreadable, and sends writes through raw Apple events rather than osascript. The branch spans 2,399 additions across service code, permissions, CLI wiring, onboarding, packaging, docs, and tests, including integration coverage for the real store and real writes.

### [ChronoSim examples get byte-identical replay and model-checking gates](https://github.com/adolgert/ChronoSimExamples.jl/pull/5) — Andrew Dolgert / ChronoSimExamples.jl
Andrew Dolgert opened a Fable co-authored PR that exercises ChronoSim’s debugging and verification layer across four real example models rather than toy fixtures. The branch ports validators into named invariants, annotates all 39 `fire!` methods, adds four “why-verb” acceptance scenarios for historical trigger and guard bugs, and wires Quint model checking into CI while retiring a noisy TLA path. The PR reports 41 test-set summaries with zero failures, byte-identical differential trajectories at every commit, and checker tests that pass when the toolchain is present.

### [A tenant-isolated fleet-management app lands in one PR](https://github.com/ludiloqs/Fuhrparkmanagement-/pull/1) — Carsten Tschirner / Fuhrparkmanagement
Carsten Tschirner’s open Fable 5 co-authored PR builds a multi-tenant fleet-management web app with Next.js 16, Prisma, Tailwind, SQLite for development, and PostgreSQL for production. The app includes company self-registration, JWT cookie auth, Admin/Manager/Driver roles, vehicle records with mileage and inspection dates, booking approvals with conflict checks, maintenance history, and dashboard metrics. The body calls out strict `companyId` isolation on the REST API, Zod validation, and seed data for two demo companies to demonstrate tenant separation; the diff is 10,936 additions across schema, migrations, routes, pages, and components.

### [ZapCRM imports Google Maps leads through an Apify prospecting flow](https://github.com/DouglasRomminger/ZapCRM/pull/14) — Douglas Romminger / ZapCRM
ZapCRM has an open Fable 5 co-authored PR for a prospecting module that runs the `compass/crawler-google-places` Apify actor from the backend and imports completed results as CRM contacts. The backend starts runs, polls status, deduplicates contacts by phone, tags leads with the prospecting term, stores category/address/site notes, normalizes Brazilian phone numbers into WhatsApp format, and returns clear 503 errors when `APIFY_TOKEN` is missing. The frontend adds a `/prospeccao` page with compliance copy, polling, import summaries, and lead lists, while the PR reports nine new tests and a 28/28 suite.

### [Eighty-three Fable sessions used mostly for decisions, not drafting](https://note.com/tama_san/n/n0494fb0e94a2) — 小串珠美 / Tama
Tama, an AI coach and consultant, audited five days of Claude Fable 5 use and counted 83 sessions, including 31 on the first day and one YouTube-production rebuild that ran past 800 back-and-forths. The interesting split was not volume but delegation: she says she mostly avoided routine drafting and visualization work, instead using Fable for business strategy after the AI boom, pricing three new consulting offers, deciding which client work to accept, and designing a YouTube publishing system. Her main lesson was to ask Fable to build procedures that lower-tier models could run later — slide-generation scaffolding, smartwatch health-data analysis and notification flows, and production manuals — rather than one-off outputs that would vanish with access to the model.

### [A game-art pipeline spends forty-seven credits against a three-hundred-credit budget](https://x.com/KanaWorks_AI/status/2073947005991420156) — KanaWorks_AI
KanaWorks_AI gave Fable 5 a 300-credit art-production budget and asked it to plan, generate, and replace the assets for a browser deck-building roguelike. The report says Fable avoided one-image-at-a-time generation, batched icons into fixed grids such as 5×4 sheets, and finished 119 art assets plus 11 backgrounds for 47 credits. A follow-up in the thread links the resulting free game, “Card Abyss,” and frames the useful behavior as budget-aware production planning rather than image generation alone.

### [A Ruby CLI review turns nine confirmed bugs into regression tests](https://github.com/git-command/git-commander/pull/5) — Valentino Stoll / git-commander
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored PR to `git-commander` starts with a 414-line repository analysis covering architecture, plugin loading, CI health, and nine empirically confirmed bugs with file and line references. Follow-up commits fix the public registry API dropping command blocks, plugin commands all resolving through the first executor, `--no-` switches losing to truthy defaults, option-set deduplication, stale GitHub password-and-OTP setup, a missing documented help subcommand, and several logging and lookup bugs. The branch also adds regression specs for each fix, upgrades the Ruby dependency floor and lockfile, and modernizes CI across Ruby 3.3–3.5 on Ubuntu and macOS.

### [MCP file-editing tools are jailed behind realpath allowlists](https://github.com/cobiadigital/mcp-for-ssh-control/pull/5) — cobiadigital
A merged Fable 5 co-authored PR to `mcp-for-ssh-control` adds six new MCP tools — directory listing, file read/write/edit, script checking, and script execution — while keeping them disabled unless `ALLOWED_PATHS` is configured. Every requested path is resolved with `realpath` before use so `..` segments and symlinks cannot escape the allowed roots, and `run_script` only dispatches files inside those roots through a fixed interpreter set rather than arbitrary binaries. The patch spans the internal Node service, worker interface, README, and smoke tests that now include path-escape checks.

### [A one-shot 3D traffic view of Powell and Market](https://x.com/KrishivThakuria/status/2073947249743204755) — Krishiv Thakuria
Krishiv Thakuria posted a short demo saying Fable 5 “one shotted” a site that shows live San Francisco traffic in 3D, centered on the Powell & Market cable-car turnaround. The report is compact, but it includes a video of the generated visualization, making the claim more concrete than a text-only reaction. It is a useful small example of Fable being pointed at real-time civic data and producing an interactive 3D web view on the first attempt.

### [A speech-to-text benchmark is built and run by Fable in three days](https://x.com/koedesk/status/2073957504267256124) — koedesk
koedesk published a preregistered benchmark of 13 hosted speech-to-text APIs across 12 languages, with 28,400 raw transcriptions, public scoring code, and a leaderboard generated from the repository rather than hand-edited numbers. In the thread, the team says Fable 5 built and ran almost the entire project — methodology, 13 API adapters, the transcription run, scoring, and a 36-language site — in about three days for roughly $28, with humans at approval gates. The benchmark repo backs up the claim with the frozen preregistration, raw outputs, amendments, scoring scripts, and commit history carrying Claude Fable 5 co-author trailers for the site and methodology updates.

### [Streaming generations survive leaving the editor screen](https://github.com/dsyang/pocket-artifacts/pull/8) — dsyang / pocket-artifacts
pocket-artifacts has an open Fable co-authored PR for a concrete mobile-agent failure: a chat generation was owned by the editor screen, so TCA cancelled the SSE stream when the user navigated away. The new `GenerationService` is an app-lifetime actor with per-artifact single-flight tasks, snapshot-first streams for late subscribers, DB-derived version numbering, partial-content checkpoints on failure or suspend, and a background-task wrapper for iOS’s finite grace window. The PR adds 838 lines across the dependency client, service actor, background-task client, and tests for persistence, no-fence output, cancellation, concurrent artifacts, single-flight refusal, and expiration checkpointing.

### [A transient roster refetch stops collapsing a money app nav bar](https://github.com/ecuaryan/bucket-my-money/pull/266) — ecuaryan / bucket-my-money
bucket-my-money’s Fable-authored v1.8.2 PR traces a bottom-nav jump to a money-move toast that triggered an account Realtime event, refetched the household roster, and briefly replaced a good roster with `[]` on any transient query failure. That made `childCount` hit zero, dropped the Kids/Give tab, moved Buckets to a different nav position, and then snapped back when the next refetch succeeded. The fix retries the unguarded auth-lock query, keeps the last good roster on failed refetches, broadens the shell freeze around absent member state, and adds a provider-level regression test that failed against the old code.

### [An adversarial review finds the missing trust anchor in Eliza’s certification gate](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/14650) — Shaw / elizaOS
Eliza’s open Fable co-authored certification PR follows an adversarial review of an earlier promotion-gate change: 20 of 20 forgery and bypass attempts failed against the crypto, but the public key itself had never been committed because `.gitignore` swallowed `*.pem`. The fix commits the Ed25519 SPKI public key, scopes the ignore exception to that file, records the fingerprint and bootstrap custody note in the certification README, and adds tests that verify existence, shape, and fingerprint so a missing or swapped key fails the suite. The PR reports `bun run --cwd packages/evidence test` at 270 passed and one skipped, including the new anchor tests.

### [A Roblox road-building plugin adds curves, banking, and regional road profiles](https://github.com/gamesonrblx/roblox-redupe/pull/1) — gamesonrblx / roblox-redupe
roblox-redupe has an open Fable co-authored PR adding a second Rojo plugin for building roads inside Roblox Studio. The 3,815-line branch implements straight, quadratic, and cubic curve tools with arc-length parameterized segments, endpoint snapping, tangent-locked continuation, automatic superelevation from design speed and curve radius, transition-based lane widening, and a cross-section editor for drive lanes, sidewalks, medians, shoulders, bike lanes, and parking lanes. It also persists custom road profiles in plugin settings and ships US, UK, EU, JP, and AU presets for drive side, lane widths, marking colors and dash patterns, curb height, max banking, world scale, snapping, and geometry quality.

### [meme-gtd grows an offline-first iOS sync stack](https://github.com/Kumac13/meme-gtd/pull/250) — Kumac13 / meme-gtd
meme-gtd has an open Fable co-authored PR that takes an iOS companion app from server-only reads toward offline-first operation across a long commit series. The branch adds UUID and server-sequence columns, tombstones, idempotency ledgers, sync API endpoints, a GRDB local database, offline memo and comment writes with an outbox, retry backoff, read-only caches for tasks and articles, standalone local storage, and a one-way standalone-to-server migration flow. The PR spans 15,705 additions, carries Fable co-author trailers on more than forty commits, and includes repeated versioned releases through v0.44.0 rather than a single patch.

### [A World Cup predictor connects Monte Carlo simulations to live data and messages](https://github.com/kimonofeelings/github-slideshow/pull/2) — kimonofeelings / github-slideshow

The branch adds zone-based attacker-versus-defender matchups, extra time and penalties, closed-form Poisson validation, team attack and defense scores, stadium, weather, crowd, travel, and referee context, plus live ESPN fixtures, venues, kickoff weather, in-match re-simulation, result grading, and paper trading against Polymarket prices. It also sends summaries through SMS, Twilio, and WhatsApp, raises the default run to 1,000,000 simulations, and wires tests and CI around the engine.

The backend adds case, message, outbound-draft, attachment, and audit-log tables; classifies messages as buyer inquiries, supplier quotes, follow-ups, or general notes; extracts explicit English facts; and generates channel-appropriate drafts with commercial-risk notes for price, lead time, payment terms, and commitments. Later Fable commits add the welcome and conversation UI, 20 PRD acceptance cases, multilingual interface switching, and authentication with a production fail-closed guard.

### [A repository analyzer becomes a single-file skill installer](https://github.com/congtubkbn/cline-agent/pull/5) — congtubkbn / cline-agent

The build script bundles parser, cleaner, and server modules with esbuild, emits `dist/cline-agent-installer.mjs`, installs the app under `~/.cline-agent-analyzer`, and places the skill under `~/.claude/skills` or a project directory while guarding against clobbering unrelated directories. The PR also adds a distribution-specific `SKILL.md`, fixes `serve.mjs` so it resolves web assets relative to its installed location, and documents packaging in the README and a dedicated guide.

### [A Cloudflare dashboard stack ships with deploy-safety gates](https://github.com/arvicco/mimir/pull/5) — Ar Vicco / mimir
mimir's open Phase 4 PR uses Claude Fable 5 as part of a tiered agent workflow for a full Cloudflare publishing layer. The branch adds a Worker API that serves KV envelopes with strict key allowlists, extracts a shared renderer for preview and production pages, builds a production dashboard with live age tickers and a sortable BTCo table, pins ECharts with SRI drift checks, and adds an owner-run `rake deploy` path that refuses CI deployment. The PR reports 298 Ruby runs, 1,517 assertions, 12 node worker tests, byte-identical preview screenshot checks, Playwright interaction checks, and several Gate 4 corrections around Wrangler auth, explicit deployment names, and loop-denied deploy commands.

### [An Apple II emulator stops running twenty-five percent fast](https://github.com/st3fan/ewm/pull/206) — Stefan Arentz / ewm
Stefan Arentz opened a Fable co-authored timing fix for `ewm` after a user noticed games sounded too fast. The PR traces the bug to wrong inherited 6502 cycle counts, missing taken-branch penalties, missing page-cross penalties on read modes, and frame deadlines that were recalculated after rendering. A new ROM WAIT-routine regression test shows the old code counted `WAIT(10)` as 338 cycles instead of the documented 392; after the fix, A values 1, 10, 100, 200, and 255 are cycle-exact, the golden screenshot stays byte-identical, and 51 tests plus fmt and clippy remain green.

### [A game project gets a ten-part Fable review and tooling kit](https://github.com/radiantnode/tensies/pull/57) — Michael Simmons / Tensies
Tensies has an open Fable 5 review PR that is more than a generic audit: it adds ten docs plus runnable examples under `docs/fable5-review/`, covering technical soundness, security, frontend quality, gameplay math, 643-commit project history, collaboration analysis, metrics, and a ranked fix list. The review names two recovery-layer bugs, an unauthenticated `legacy_pid` stat-transfer issue, one markup bug, and a 31-error gap behind a “strict-checked JS” claim, then records follow-up fixes and process advice. Later commits add verified hooks, prompt templates, two Claude Code skills, a statusline, and theme-aware SVG charts generated from git history and transcript scans.

### [A game-theory solver stops returning the feasible hull too early](https://github.com/QuantEcon/QuantEcon.py/pull/848) — Daisuke Oyama / QuantEcon.py
QuantEcon.py has a Fable 5 co-authored fix for a subtle Abreu–Sannikov repeated-game bug in `RepeatedGame.equilibrium_payoffs`. With the default zero threat-point initialization, the algorithm could decide it had converged before threat points were updated, returning the feasible payoff hull for a Prisoner’s Dilemma where the correct equilibrium set has vertices `[[3, 3], [9.75, 3], [9, 9], [3, 9.75]]`. The patch initializes from player-wise minimum payoffs, moves threat-point updates before convergence, raises a clearer error for empty candidate sets, and adds regression tests cross-checked against GameTheory.jl and a 256-bit BigFloat reference computation.

### [Java Playwright tests move into stateless flow and page layers](https://github.com/krll-dev/playwright-e2e/pull/5) — KRLL / playwright-e2e
KRLL merged a Fable 5 co-authored refactor of a Java Playwright end-to-end suite into a layered, stateless test architecture. The change introduces immutable login records, page objects with only locator getters and atomic actions, a navigation component, reusable login and edit flows, and command-style edit actions, then rewrites tests toward Arrange–Act–Assert with no UI logic in the test bodies. It also enables Allure `@Step` output through an AspectJ Surefire agent, adds compiler parameter metadata for clearer step placeholders, and documents layer rules and anti-patterns in `CLAUDE.MD` and the README.

### [Silent Hill texture overrides reach the renderer](https://github.com/SlickAmogus/PsyCross/pull/5) — vjravenlight / PsyCross
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored PsyCross PR connects a Silent Hill decompilation texture-replacement table to the emulator renderer path that had been sitting dormant. The fix calls the host lookup before textured primitive splits, keeps batching keyed by the existing texture id, supplies a weak no-op stub for hosts without overrides, and maps the original TIM pixel size so upscaled replacements sample correctly. It is only 48 new lines across the GPU and render code, but the PR body explains the exact delivery mechanism and cleanup path it needed to preserve.

Astronomical Home has an open Fable 5 branch that merges two independently developed chase-navigation tracks for its Asteroids3D AI. Track A added gap primitives, collision admissibility, and a redesigned sampler; Track B added deterministic obstacle sensing, a terminal cost-to-go field, and a two-AI chase benchmark harness. The integration PR records conflict resolutions file by file, includes baseline and combined benchmark JSONL artifacts, and keeps all eleven commits co-authored by Claude Fable 5 rather than hiding the model’s contribution behind a squash.

The follow-up commits convert that into product work: a truth pass, real-product home-page sections, installation pages, migration and keyboard-reference docs, structured data fixes, live component previews, an accessibility announcement matrix in CI, and a `cascivo theme create` CLI path. ### [Mitos benchmarks copy-on-write density before multi-VM pods](https://github.com/mitos-run/mitos/pull/766) — stubbi / Mitos
Mitos used Claude Fable 5 through Claude Code for a narrow benchmark-infrastructure PR that de-risks same-tenant VM swarms. The workflow gains a dispatch-only `density_forks` input, defaulting to 16 and 64 forks, that runs the metering benchmark on a bare-metal KVM runner and appends per-fork tables for CoW-aware memory, naive memory, savings, and unique memory. The PR explicitly leaves push CI untouched and says the YAML parses, making this a small but concrete example of Fable adding measurement before a larger density change lands.

### [Agent terminal tabs get blocked and done state badges](https://github.com/kt0319/any-console/pull/91) — kt0319 / any-console
any-console has an open Claude Fable 5 co-authored PR that lets job definitions name blocked and done phrases, then polls visible tmux panes to classify each agent session as blocked, done, working, or idle. The state rides the existing status WebSocket and appears as badges on tabs and split panes, while the job editor gets fields for the detection phrases. The 892-line branch adds `api/agent_watch.py`, UI badge components, and tests for the Python watcher, status stream, terminal store, and UI behavior.

### [A VS Code agent extension closes workspace escape paths](https://github.com/getsigit/sigit-vscode/pull/1) — paydii / sigit-vscode
sigit-vscode’s first PR includes a Fable 5 co-authored hardening pass on an ACP-based VS Code extension. The patch scopes agent command, default-agent, registry, and permission settings as machine settings so a repository cannot smuggle them through `.vscode/settings.json`, disables the extension in Restricted Mode, confines file read/write tools to workspace folders, and fixes permission-option matching that could be fooled by agent-chosen labels. It also rejects pending JSON-RPC calls when the agent disconnects, caps the ndjson read buffer at 16 MiB, honors line limits for `read_text_file`, and switches webview CSP nonces from `Math.random` to `crypto.randomBytes`.

### [A generated financial report learns to prove its balances](https://github.com/mcmespana/mcmbank/pull/155) — mcmespana / mcmbank
mcmbank’s Fable 5 co-authored PR fixes a Spanish “memoria económica” generator whose spreadsheet could disagree with the application dashboard. The branch corrects three concrete causes — a balance row that counted chapter I twice, inactive accounts being included in exports, and school-year labels leaking into calendar-year reports — then adds a live “Cuadre del ejercicio” validation panel. The new `ResumenValidacion` compares remnant plus entries minus exits against real account balances, flags uncategorized movements and double-counting through parent and child categories, and updates the review dialog as rows are edited.

### [Leviathan stops sharing Git state across open repositories](https://github.com/hegsie/Leviathan/pull/250) — hegsie / Leviathan
Leviathan’s Fable-authored multi-repo fix starts from four state-corruption bugs that only appear when several repositories are open at once. A global commit-search index mixed repo histories, every tab switch spawned another 500 ms polling thread, background-repo updates had to fake active-tab switches, and ahead/behind badges reflected whichever repo fetched last. The 2,093-line PR keys search indexes, watchers, refreshes, store updates, graph caches, and toolbar notifications by repository path, and adds end-to-end and unit tests for repo tabs, app shell behavior, graph rendering, and toolbar state.

### [A knowledge-vault CLI gets capture, digestion, and timer flows](https://github.com/beep-effect/beep-effect/pull/300) — kriegcloud / beep-effect
beep-effect’s Fable 5 co-authored PR adds a `beep research` command family for maintaining a personal knowledge vault. The new pipeline captures pages with Firecrawl, sifts browser history through DuckDB’s `sqlite_scan`, clones repositories into “repo cards” with GitHub star metadata, pulls Notion pages and databases, runs Cognee datasets, generates digests, and installs a systemd user timer for daily meta-runs. The PR spans 4,620 additions across the CLI command, schemas, service layer, tests, and documentation, and records local proof for advisory feedback, amend, full pre-push, and publish checks.

### [A waitlist form stops hiding failed submissions](https://github.com/hellosurbhi/itskaren-landing/pull/2) — hellosurbhi / itskaren-landing
itskaren-landing has a small but well-scoped Fable 5 co-authored fix for a production waitlist form. The old page set a `localStorage` flag after any submit attempt, hid the form forever, and showed success even when the network request failed; the patch removes the persistence, waits for the request to settle, restores the button on failure, and shows an inline error. The PR reports `node --check` on the extracted script, zero remaining `localStorage` matches, and a live-site diff confirming the bug was present before the fix.

### [Two workspaces share one contact spine without sharing data](https://github.com/serenakg/Design/pull/2) — serenakg / Design
serenakg opened a Fable co-authored foundation PR for a two-workspace contact and campaign app rather than a mockup. The Supabase schema scopes every table by `workspace_id` through a `workspace_access` grant table, adds an approval gate that blocks posts from reaching scheduled or posted states without `approved_by`, and includes consent timestamps plus GDPR export and delete functions for each contact. The Next.js 16 app adds workspace switching, dashboard counts, contact filtering and tagging, exports, settings, auth, and private storage buckets; the PR reports PostgreSQL 16 checks for RLS walls, approval gates, GDPR functions, and privilege-escalation blocks, plus production build, lint, and route smoke tests.

### [An old Chrome rendering bug becomes a browser-support policy](https://github.com/Intentional-Society/is-app/pull/495) — James Baker / Intentional Society
Intentional Society used Fable 5 on a small but concrete production compatibility problem: a member on Chrome 109 saw white-on-white graph labels because the app relied on `paint-order: stroke` for HTML text, which Chrome only supports from version 123. The fix renders a separate stroked halo under plain fill text for the profile mini-map and `/myweb` hover labels, so current browsers look the same while older browsers stay readable. The same PR documents a Baseline-based browser-support policy, names the hard floor in the legacy checker, adds a member-facing `/about` note, and reports local `npm test` plus Playwright checks for the mini-map, hover labels, dark mode, truncation, and links.

### [A risk wiki gets twenty mapped cascade pathways](https://github.com/Dark-Matter-Labs/indy-llm-wiki/pull/27) — indy-ops / Dark Matter Labs
Dark Matter Labs’ indy-llm-wiki has a Fable co-authored synthesis PR that turns the Civilization Options and XCO corpus into a “Cascade Atlas.” The new page maps twenty cascading-risk pathways in a trigger → cascade stages → terminal-risk format, with topology signatures, the optionality component each pathway attacks, an early-warning signal, and the platform meant to interrupt it. The PR also publishes a self-contained responsive HTML version, updates the wiki indexes, and explicitly marks source-grounded material versus synthesis while flagging caveats such as an inconsistency in the planetary-boundaries count.

The PR renders each episode summary and transcript as escaped, self-contained HTML with a per-page table of contents, writes manifest and index pages, uploads public pages to a GCS bucket while keeping the private manifest path, and adds Terraform for bucket IAM and the new Cloud Run environment variable. It spans 1,132 additions across rendering, publishing, config, storage, infrastructure, and tests for HTML rendering plus publish wiring and dry-run behavior.

### [A JavaScript engine narrows several Node benchmark gaps](https://github.com/ThousandBirdsInc/chidori/pull/90) — Colton Pierson / Chidori
Chidori’s Fable co-authored JavaScript-engine PR attacks several measured benchmark gaps against Node rather than a single micro-optimization. The first commit moves dense-array `push`/`pop` and higher-order builtins onto faster paths, taking `array_push_sum` from 325ms to 157ms and `array_hof` from 208ms to 97ms on the author’s benchmark box; later commits add prototype-chain guards after test262 caught regressions, all-number `f64` sort, kernel copy propagation and dead-move elimination, superinstructions, and property slots localized into registers. The commit log reports full unit suites, kernel differential corpora and fuzzing, and selected test262 language, Array, and TypedArray gates clean, with `property_access` dropping from 49ms to 15ms after the final localization pass.

### [Town archers join a game with animation, ballistics, and staging hooks](https://github.com/miskibin/warbell/pull/52) — Michał Skibiński / Warbell
Warbell’s Fable co-authored archer PR is a game-feature implementation with rendering, animation, combat, projectiles, audio, and staging wired together. It adds archer villager bodies with longbows, quivers, hoods, and bracers; a draw-and-loose biped animation whose release frame spawns an arrow aimed at the target’s live chest; ballistic arrows with gravity, hit damage, missed-shaft fadeouts, and projectile unit tests; and bowshot audio gated by earshot. A follow-up commit makes roughly one third of militia spawns bowmen and replaces keep-roof mannequins with real archer bipeds using the same animation and projectile path.

### [Google Apps Script work moves into a bundled TypeScript toolchain](https://github.com/n-drobyshevski/sidekick/pull/5) — n-drobyshevski / sidekick
sidekick merged a Fable co-authored scaffold that turns a Google Apps Script project into a TypeScript, clasp, esbuild, and Vitest workspace. The branch bundles sources into a single IIFE for the GAS V8 runtime, keeps handwritten `doGet`, `api_*`, and trigger delegators in `dist/entry.js`, and inlines client JS and CSS into HtmlService partials at build time. Across 88 files and 18,087 additions, follow-up commits port the domain core with Python golden-fixture parity, add Sheets and Drive storage, implement a UrlFetchApp client with resumable scan jobs, build a six-page SPA shell, and document a plain-text sheet format with UTC pinning.

### [Sentry gaps become an admin observability stack](https://github.com/xdoubleu/tools.xdoubleu.com/pull/313) — xdoubleu / tools.xdoubleu.com
tools.xdoubleu.com merged a Fable co-authored observability pass that starts with three concrete Sentry gaps: `slog.Default()` callers bypassing the Sentry handler, web route-level exceptions missing capture, and watchparty cleanup panics killing the goroutine without a report.
