### [TVDB series status starts flowing into Silo cards](https://github.com/Silo-Server/silo-plugin-tvdb/pull/5) — Quick104 / Silo Server
Silo’s TVDB plugin had already deserialized series statuses such as “Continuing,” “Ended,” and “Upcoming,” but never copied them into the SDK metadata field that the host persists and renders on Show Status cards. A Fable-assisted PR bumps the plugin SDK, maps `series.Status.Name` through `MetadataResult.ShowStatus` into `MetadataItem.status`, and leaves provider spelling normalization to the host. The patch is small — six files and 55 additions — but includes an `httptest` regression that proves the status reaches the metadata result and notes the companion host and TMDB-plugin changes needed for full parity.

### [Fable ties Opus on VulcanBench while costing more and refusing tasks](https://x.com/morganlinton/status/2074499711848034445) — Morgan Linton / VulcanBench
Morgan Linton published a VulcanBench run comparing Claude Fable 5 and Claude Opus 4.8 on 15 real merged pull-request tasks, with low- and high-effort settings for each model. On the 13 common tasks all four configurations tied at 7/13 pass@1, while Fable runs cost about $22–26 and took roughly 99–104 minutes versus Opus at about $7–13 and 37–54 minutes. The hard-tier breakdown was worse for Fable high effort, which exhausted its output budget and wrote no code on the five frontier-hard tasks, and Fable also refused one or two legitimate networking tasks that Opus handled.

### [A compact hook mistook 187k Fable tokens for a full context window](https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC/pull/2468) — girish-kanjiyani7 / ECC
ECC’s strategic-compact hook had a Fable-specific context-window bug: `claude-fable-5` at 187,000 tokens was treated as a 200,000-token model, so the hook reported roughly 93% context use when real use was about 19% of Fable’s one-million-token window. The PR adds known large-window model-family detection for Fable and Mythos, keeps environment overrides first, and anchors substring matching so future suffixes such as a hypothetical mini tier do not inherit the one-million-token budget by accident. Five new tests cover the original Fable repro, Mythos, dated or region-prefixed IDs, override precedence, and unknown-model fallback; the author reports 2,976 tests passing with ESLint clean.

### [A granular synth adds pitch-synchronous grains after the stochastic path failed](https://github.com/Lifted-Truck/Auricle/pull/15) — Julian Smith / Auricle
Auricle’s merged Fable co-authored PR adds a Pitch Lock mode after small, high-density stochastic grains proved unusable noise. The new mode makes grain emission pitch-synchronous — `baseHz·2^(pitch/12)` — latches the read window to fixed buffer content, and uses constant-overlap-add gain so tiny grains form a tone instead of a cloud. The author says autocorrelation verification tracks C2 through C4 within about 15 cents, amplitude ripple has coefficient of variation around 0.03, 37 tests pass, and pluginval strictness 8 passes.

### [A Rust codebase gets a generated knowledge graph with AST and image chunks](https://github.com/jyjeanne/crustly/pull/17) — Jeremy Jeanne / crustly
crustly has an open Fable co-authored PR building a codebase knowledge graph rather than a prose-only architecture note. The branch runs AST extraction over 132 Rust files plus semantic extraction over 50 docs and 7 images, producing a checked-in interactive graph, audit report, and JSON graph with 3,018 nodes, 6,958 edges, and 136 labeled communities. Follow-up commits move the output under `docs/graph/`, add update scripts and git hooks for LLM-free regeneration, and wire a graph viewer command into the TUI.

### [Attachment-only chat turns stop becoming fake dispatch briefs](https://github.com/Dreighto/sully-backend/pull/116) — Dreighto / sully-backend
sully-backend found a small but telling autonomous-dispatch bug: when an operator attached a file, the classifier read the raw Markdown upload link and proposed handing off a job named like `[file.md](./api/…)`. The Fable co-authored fix feeds the classifier plain-English attachment mentions instead of raw links and makes attachment-only turns bypass autonomous dispatch altogether. The patch touches three files and adds three tests for the safer attachment path.

### [Proof Council adds Fable as an Author and Council model](https://github.com/eth-sri/proof-council/pull/2) — Uwe Kreitner / proof-council
Proof Council’s open PR adds `claude-fable-5` as both a long-timeout Author model and a 30-minute Council-seat option for its author-critic workflow. The thread documents Fable-specific API constraints — adaptive thinking only, no sampling parameters, 128k output ceiling, and 30-day retention requirements — then moves beyond config into native Anthropic Files support with `container_upload` and code execution. Two live smokes against the real API generated `answer.tex`, `references.bib`, and `research_notes.tex`, with reported costs of about $0.08 per run and focused unit suites of 27 and 29 tests passing.

### [Next.js evals rerun Fable after judge conversions](https://github.com/vercel/next-evals-oss/pull/93) — gaojude / Vercel
Vercel’s `next-evals-oss` PR reruns `claude-fable-5` after two agent evals moved from brittle regex checks to fixed-Haiku LLM judges. The sync found only `agent-030-app-router-migration-hard` and `agent-034-async-cookies` changed across 44 experiments, plus one renamed eval, so the PR preserves cached results everywhere else. It also tightens stale-result handling from whole experiments to exact experiment/eval pairs, removing Fable’s accepted-stale entry so CI now enforces fresh results for the updated grading pin.

### [Tax benchmark publishes 250 Fable web-search reports](https://github.com/column-tax/tax-calc-bench/pull/89) — Michael Bock / tax-calc-bench
Column Tax’s benchmark PR adds saved Claude Fable 5 web-search outputs for all 50 TY25 tax cases across five thinking levels, producing 500 result files and 250 evaluation reports. The best Fable setting was `ultrathink`, at 34.00% strict accuracy, 44.00% lenient accuracy, 79.77% by line, and 83.34% lenient by line. The run captured web-search queries in 188 of 250 reports, 628 queries total, and the PR reports 80 support tests passing plus ruff and artifact-completeness checks.

### [A Rails imageboard gets a Fable-generated OpenAPI spec](https://github.com/philomena-dev/philomena/pull/707) — Meow / Philomena
Philomena’s open PR adds a 2,014-line OpenAPI specification for its API v1 in a single file. The author says Claude Fable 5 generated it after reading the actual Rails controllers and Derpibooru API docs, while they personally wrote only the top-level description, site links, and small corrections. A follow-up commit applies review updates, making this a concrete documentation-generation case rather than a generic model reaction.

### [Flux Skills turns Fable into an orchestrator](https://x.com/OMNILEV/status/2074302690801357035) — Jacques / OMNILEV
Jacques from Adrective published `flux-skills`, a Claude Code plugin built around using Fable 5 for planning and review rather than routine implementation. The thread describes a Fable orchestrator delegating to cheaper specialized agents, and the repository README spells out the routing: Haiku scouts and verifies, Sonnet or Opus builds, and the top model spends tokens on decisions, synthesis, and review. The skills persist team state in `.claude/TEAM.md`, require evidence from tests, diffs, or logs before accepting subagent work, and add a loop mode for long-horizon execution against a checkable finish line.

### [Winlator runtime bugs fixed across affinity, containers, presets, and Android 15 builds](https://github.com/Hfses/GameNative/pull/1) — Hfses / GameNative
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored GameNative PR fixes a cluster of Winlator-layer runtime failures rather than a surface feature. It replaces saturated CPU-affinity math for core 31, stops release builds from printing every child-process line, copies full container configs instead of hand-preserving only 21 of roughly 60 fields, migrates Box64 presets from a pipe/comma string format to JSON, respects the user's WINEESYNC setting, and aligns remaining native libraries for Android 15's 16 KB pages. Follow-up commits add regression tests for affinity masks, preset storage, container persistence, migration paths, and CI build behavior.

### [Standup generation gets a cross-source work-item knowledge base](https://github.com/pavelpilyak/devrecall/pull/1) — pavelpilyak / devrecall
devrecall used Claude Fable 5 to turn a flat activity log into a structured work-item knowledge base for standup summaries. The PR adds deterministic linking across Jira keys, PR commits, and metadata; an enrichment table with provider-backed one-line digests and controlled-vocabulary tags; a post-sync pipeline that links, enriches, and embeds; and agent tools for retrieving work-item timelines and related activities. The stated failure modes are concrete: related tickets, commits, and PRs were not being grouped into one narrative, while status transitions leaked into prose as fake work bullets.

### [DeployEx engine restarts stop turning into monitor crash loops](https://github.com/thiagoesteves/deployex/pull/242) — thiagoesteves / DeployEx
Thiago Esteves opened a four-PR Claude Fable 5 fix set for DeployEx engine and monitoring edge cases: [initial-boot rollback timeouts](https://github.com/thiagoesteves/deployex/pull/239) no longer corrupt engine state, [cache writes](https://github.com/thiagoesteves/deployex/pull/240) become synchronous so immediate reads do not see stale UI settings, [stderr logs](https://github.com/thiagoesteves/deployex/pull/241) are streamed and retained instead of silently dropped, and [engine restarts](https://github.com/thiagoesteves/deployex/pull/242) tolerate already-running monitors instead of MatchError crash loops. Each PR describes the observed failure path, blast radius, regression risk, and rollback plan, making this a useful example of Fable producing small operational fixes with explicit risk analysis.

### [Cascade adds account auth and user-scoped node queries](https://github.com/Patrickroelofs/cascade/pull/123) — Patrickroelofs / Cascade
Cascade has a Claude Fable 5 co-authored PR that moves the app from public shared state toward user accounts. It adds a Better Auth workspace package with email/password, scrypt, Drizzle auth tables, server and React clients, login and registration pages, session-refreshing middleware, and production cookie-domain handling. More importantly, the app routes now require sessions and every node query or mutation is scoped through a new non-null `nodes.user_id`, with Playwright sign-in setup added for end-to-end coverage.

### [A local anime-data editor gets optimistic-save guards](https://github.com/NathapolDev/AnimeSubThaiOnYoutube/pull/23) — NathapolDev / AnimeSubThaiOnYoutube
AnimeSubThaiOnYoutube used Claude Fable 5 to add a local-only admin editor for hand-maintaining `anime.json`. The tool serves on `127.0.0.1:4321`, separates human-owned fields from read-only pipeline-owned fields, supports raw JSON editing, and saves one entry at a time through the existing `write-data.js` path. The important guard is a per-entry content hash: if the pipeline rewrites an entry between load and save, the editor returns a 409 instead of overwriting newer generated data, and deployment explicitly excludes the admin UI.

### [Haverstack defines a governed commons namespace for shared schemas](https://github.com/haverstack/core/pull/73) — cuibonobo / Haverstack
Haverstack's Claude Fable 5 co-authored Schema Commons PR is a documentation-heavy design artifact rather than code, but it is detailed enough to be useful. It establishes an `org.haverstack` commons namespace, canonical versions of note, bookmark, task, and contact types, and rules for minimal required cores, additive evolution, properties versus perspectives, read-compatible cores, and lightweight proposal governance. Follow-up commits expand the publishing and group clusters, text-type contracts, and naming rationale, staging the material under `docs/commons/` for later extraction into its own repository.

### [PCB agents now report the design-rule damage they create](https://github.com/ecto/vcad/pull/438) — ecto / vcad
vcad has an open Fable 5 co-authored PR prompted by a concrete agent failure: `add_motor_winding` returned only a new `document_version` after shorting a board in three places and creating three islands, so the agent learned about the damage only after a separate DRC run. The fix wraps every copper-mutating MCP tool in before/after design-rule snapshots and returns a `drc_delta` with introduced and resolved counts, categories, worst-first samples, and a `clean` flag. Large boards use a new region-scoped DRC path for geometry while keeping shorts and islands board-global, and the follow-up commit extends the same contract to net-tie tools.

### [Three chat reliability bugs get a Fable patch](https://github.com/Pinewood-Tech-Club/one/pull/5) — AdamEXu / Pinewood-Tech-Club
Pinewood’s chat page PR says it was written by Claude Fable 5 through Claude Code and targets three specific reliability failures. It preserves typed text when `sendMessage` rejects, distinguishes still-loading or transient-auth states from true non-entitlement, and hydrates in-flight tool-call state from durable Convex rows after an SSE reconnect. The author reports `tsc --noEmit` passing and explicitly notes that no full production build or runtime smoke test was run.

### [Nested dict ingestion becomes a planned Fable implementation series](https://github.com/kolodkin/aaiclick/pull/359) — kolodkin / aaiclick
aaiclick’s open PR shows a 17-commit Claude Fable 5 session moving from design spec to implementation for nested data ingestion. The branch flattens singleton nested dicts into plain dotted columns such as `x.y.z`, reconstructs them on `data()`, validates dotted keys and empty dicts, and then routes dict and record ingestion through whole-dataset Arrow schema inference. Follow-up commits handle nulls at every list level, reject `None` inside list-of-dicts, infer all-`None` values as nullable strings for cross-backend use, and consolidate the behavior into the user guide.

### [A no-build Three.js archery game grows through thirteen Fable commits](https://github.com/kolodkin/samples/pull/67) — kolodkin / samples
kolodkin’s samples PR is a 13-commit Claude Fable 5 run that starts with a design spec and TDD plan for a 3D wave-defense archery game, then builds the game in small slices. The branch adds a no-build Three.js scaffold with Playwright boot testing, seeded forest/desert/iceberg stages, first-person bow draw, ballistic arrows with trajectory hints, melee and ranged enemies, elemental arrows, wave spawning, ammo drops, stage progression, localStorage best scores, and a Preact HUD. The pull request spans 25 files and 5,892 added lines, with a README and technical spec at the end of the sequence.

### [A trading bot branch keeps changing after walk-forward checks fail](https://github.com/SivanSoran/logic-task-pass/pull/1) — SivanSoran / logic-task-pass
logic-task-pass has an open Claude Fable 5 co-authored branch that reads like an iterative trading-system lab rather than a single scaffold. It starts with a FundedNext-focused equity-guardian scalper, then shifts after GBPUSD 2024–2026 walk-forward results show breakout-only M30/M12 configurations outperforming mean reversion. Later commits move intraday strategies to H1/H4 swing timeframes because $5-per-lot commission plus spread made buffer-limited scalping uneconomic, add an ONNX-trained probability model with time-ordered train/validation/test splits, and finish with a three-scalper ensemble that self-silences losing systems through a Beta-smoothed EV gate.

### [Expo ArcGIS archives stop failing on Xcode phase ordering](https://github.com/alex-krassavin/expo-arcgis/pull/6) — bhamiltoncx / expo-arcgis
expo-arcgis has a Fable 5 co-authored fix for a release-build failure that only appears when another Expo plugin appends a tail script phase such as Datadog dSYM upload. The PR explains that `addBuildPhase()` placed the ArcGIS Embed Frameworks copy phase after the tail script, creating an Xcode dependency cycle where dSYM upload waited on the finished app and the app waited on the embedded framework. The fix moves Embed Frameworks immediately after Resources, extracts the pbxproj mutation into a tested helper, and reports three Jest tests passing on a real parsed project fixture; a later commit deletes a duplicate ArcGIS xcframework signature to fix archive exit status 70.

### [Twelve scratch scripts find ten sqlite-utils 4.0 blockers and bugs](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jul/7/sqlite-utils-4/#claude-fable-5-helped-a-lot) — Simon Willison
Simon Willison used Fable 5 in Claude Code as a final release reviewer for sqlite-utils 4.0, asking it to inspect the changes since the last 3.x tag, read the changelog and upgrade guide, and write scratch scripts for every new v4 feature. He says GPT-5.5 wrote five scripts and found nothing especially interesting, while Fable wrote 12 scripts, identified four release blockers and ten additional issues, and produced a combined repro script. The reproduced failures included implicit transactions left open after failed writes, rejected write pragmas that still took effect, foreign-key column order mistakes, CSV inserts changing existing column types, and migration stop-before behavior applying too much.

### [Radio-astronomy window functions get a 1.25x axis-scaling bug fixed](https://github.com/HERA-Team/hera_pspec/pull/514) — vincentmackay / HERA-Team
HERA’s hera_pspec PR says parts of the work were done with Claude Fable 5, including vectorization and cleanup, but the result is a domain-specific scientific correction rather than a toy patch. The author found that IDR3.1.1a theory-release window functions used an FT-beam frequency grid at 97.66 kHz while data used 122.07 kHz, silently scaling the k_parallel axis by 1.25x. The branch forces FT beams onto the data’s exact channels, adds extraction/interpolation and out-of-range tests, vectorizes cylindrical binning by about 20x with a 1e-12 parity test against the old loops, and moves FT-beam/window-function production into two CLI commands.

### [Helion kernel dispatch cache moves an H100 benchmark past vLLM on every case](https://github.com/pytorch/helion/pull/3009) — yushangdi / PyTorch
A Fable 5 co-authored PyTorch Helion PR adds a fast dispatch cache to `Kernel.__call__`, avoiding full specialization-key recomputation on repeated calls while preserving the slower bind/autotune path for new argument shapes. The body spells out the safety invariant: the cheap key is strictly finer than the full specialization key, the cache is populated only after a successful slow run, and it is bypassed under TPU compile capture or unsupported argument types. On an H100 per-token group FP8 quant benchmark, the posted before/after table moves Helion from 8 of 24 wins versus vLLM to 24 of 24, with geomean speedup rising from 0.913x to 1.268x and torch_compile wins rising from 19 of 24 to 24 of 24.

### [A bounded-model-checking pass stops dropping assumptions between timesteps](https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/10788) — leizaf / LLVM CIRCT
An LLVM CIRCT pull request marked “Assisted-by: Claude:Fable-5” fixes a verification bug in the VerifToSMT bounded-model-checking loop. The old pop/push structure discarded assertions from earlier timesteps, including `verif.assume` lowered to SMT asserts, so checks at step i could ignore constraints from steps 0 through i-1 and report spurious counterexamples. The patch moves assumptions and definitions into the enclosing assertion level while keeping property negations scoped per step, adds 101 lines across three files, and the circt-tests comparison reported no change to the broader test results.

### [Datasette turns eight modal dialogs into a public web component](https://github.com/simonw/datasette/pull/2820) — Simon Willison / Datasette
Simon Willison's open Datasette PR has a detailed "Fable 5 PR" block and Fable co-author trailers across the commit series. The branch extracts eight separate modal-dialog implementations — create table, alter table, edit row, delete row, set column type, column chooser, mobile actions, and navigation search — into a reusable `<datasette-modal>` web component exposed as a stable plugin API. It removes roughly 1,200 lines of duplicated frame markup, event wiring, and CSS, then adds Playwright coverage, docs, a screenshot-generation script, and follow-up fixes for a mobile-dialog race and declarative cancel buttons.

### [PostHog gets a repair path for lost CDC replication slots](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/69049) — danielcarletti / PostHog
A PostHog data-warehouse PR says it was authored with Claude Code using Claude Fable 5 after debugging a broken CDC source whose UI showed schemas as completed while the replication slot was gone and schedules were paused. The patch adds a `repair_cdc` action that recreates the slot and publication from stored config, resets active schemas to snapshot mode for a full resync, clears broken markers, resumes schedules, and triggers the new snapshots. It also makes the broken state "absorbing" so late batch completions cannot repaint the source healthy, and the CI comments show PostHog's large test/reporting stack running against the branch.

### [A daemon tick stops dying on truncated issue lists](https://github.com/jleechanorg/dark-factory/pull/191) — jleechan2015 / dark-factory
A Claude/Fable-5 dark-factory PR fixes a real `daemon --once` crash from an agent pipeline: `br list --json` silently returned only the first 50 rows, so external-reference deduplication missed an already tracked GitHub issue and tried to create a duplicate bead. The patch moves all three bead-list call sites to unlimited listing, rejects truncated parser output instead of using partial state, and raises GitHub list limits on both CLI and REST fallback paths. The author reports the Rust daemon tests passing with two new regressions for truncated and complete pagination fields, plus clippy and diff checks clean; a later skeptic-gate comment shows 945 tests passing.

### [A first security test baseline lands for a school chat app](https://github.com/Pinewood-Tech-Club/one/pull/7) — AdamEXu / Pinewood-Tech-Club
Pinewood’s PR body opens with a warning that the branch was written by Claude Fable 5 through Claude Code, then lays out the first automated tests for a codebase that previously had none. The 60 passing tests cover security-critical but Convex-independent paths: encryption round-trips and tamper rejection, RS256 JWT header and claim validation, job-lease ownership and stale-steal behavior, scraper section-sync leases, and mobile refresh-token rotation with family revocation on replay. A follow-up commit fixes conftest double-execution drift and strengthens key-binding coverage, making this a test-foundation report rather than a feature scaffold.

### [GraphQL repeated-field validation drops from 5.2 seconds to 0.11](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core/pull/270) — bcmyguest / graphql-core
A graphql-core PR discloses that Claude:fable-5 generated a denial-of-service mitigation for the `OverlappingFieldsCanBeMerged` validation rule. The patch deduplicates structurally identical repeated fields before the O(n²) pairwise conflict pass, while deliberately including printed directives in the fingerprint so `@stream` conflicts are not erased. The author reports a 3,000-field repeated-query validation case dropping from about 5.2 seconds to 0.11 seconds, with 693 validation tests and five benchmark tests passing.

### [LOTUSim sends absolute simulation time to xdyn instead of step duration](https://github.com/naval-group/LOTUSim/pull/35) — cmoron / naval-group
A LOTUSim PR assisted by Claude Fable 5 fixes a co-simulation time bug in the physics-engine interface. The request field `t` was carrying the raw Gazebo step duration in milliseconds, so xdyn treated every state as starting near the same small time and evaluated time-dependent forcings such as waves at a constant wrong timestamp. The one-line fix sends the plugin’s absolute simulation time in seconds instead, and the author reports a websocket tap over a close-hauled run where `t` advanced correctly across 3,708 frames while calm-water trajectories stayed identical.

### [Helion adds a direct Triton launch path with cache-key guards](https://github.com/pytorch/helion/pull/3010) — yushangdi / PyTorch
A stacked PyTorch Helion PR extends the Fable 5 co-authored dispatch-cache work with a `triton_direct_launch` fast path. After a specialization compiles once, repeat launches can call the cached Triton `CompiledKernel` directly, while scalar values, tensor dtypes, alignment bits, captured globals, device, warp/stage choices, and cooperative-launch settings are folded into the cache key so hazards miss back to the slow validating path. The posted H100 group-FP8 benchmark comment reports 22 of 24 wins versus vLLM at 1.253x geomean, 20 of 24 versus Triton at 1.592x, and 24 of 24 versus torch and torch_compile.

### [Certificate issuance attempts regain complete audit trails](https://github.com/letsencrypt/boulder/pull/8846) — beautifulentropy / Let’s Encrypt Boulder
A Claude Fable 5 audit of an earlier Boulder pull request found two paths where issuance audit logs promised a completion line but did not always produce one: renewal-check failures and multi-time-certificate issuance. The resulting patch moves MTC dispatch through the normal inner issuance path, emits `Certificate request - error` on the renewal-check return, and upgrades the MTC success line to an audit log. It is a small one-file change, but it tightens the audit invariant for every certificate issuance attempt rather than just cleaning up comments.

### [A legal AI gateway closes five audit findings](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/pull/289) — SaifAlYounan / LegalQuants
LegalQuants has a five-PR Fable 5 security-audit follow-up for `lq-ai`: [gateway inference auth](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/pull/289), [project ownership on chat creation](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/pull/290), [skill-markdown sanitization](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/pull/291), [startup refusal on the published dev JWT secret](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/pull/292), and [LLM-provider egress guards](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/pull/293). The PR bodies tie each fix to a named audit finding, including an unauthenticated gateway path that could spend provider keys, a cross-tenant KB exfiltration route through `project_id`, a stored-XSS sink in Svelte `{@html}`, and plaintext public-host prompt egress. The local gates are specific as well: focused pytest suites, ruff, mypy, and 17 URL-policy tests where the egress guard is small enough to exercise exhaustively.

### [SPARQ packs benchmark axes onto one self-terminating EC2 box](https://github.com/sparq-org/sparq/pull/1769) — jeswr / SPARQ
SPARQ’s Fable 5 agent built an EC2 benchmark runner that replaces one-box-per-axis benchmarking with a serial, bin-packed run over `fts`, `geo`, `hdt`, `update`, and `parse` on one quiet instance. The PR is careful about operational failure modes: instance-initiated shutdown is `terminate`, first-line and systemd watchdogs cap runtime, the launcher deadline sits below the watchdog, prod/dev instance IDs are refused in cleanup, and result envelopes are compacted for the 64KB serial console while full artifacts are pulled over SSH. A follow-up Fable-tier verification comment checks the invariants, reports shellcheck clean, and records a local `--instance` smoke through the real code path.

### [Agent Design Language proves a Fable provider path with live UTS runs](https://github.com/danielbaustin/agent-design-language/pull/5050) — Daniel B. Austin / Agent Design Language
Agent Design Language added a repeatable hosted-provider acceptance path for `claude-fable-5`, including a reusable output-token-budget wrapper, a Fable UTS runner, refusal-shape normalization, documentation, and tracked proof notes. The live Anthropic run used `max_output_tokens: 1024` and reported zero provider failures after normalization, scoring 10 of 11 on the regular lane and 11 of 11 on the UTS-only lane. The one regular-lane miss is documented rather than hidden: Fable refused an ordinary mutation-shaped inventory update prompt, while the UTS proposal passed because it carried `dry_run_requested: true`.

### [Transit validation reports stop depending on message parsing](https://github.com/ChelseaKR/tods-validate/pull/22) — ChelseaKR / tods-validate
A Fable 5 roadmap batch for `tods-validate` makes its validation output more machine-readable and more honest about privacy limits. The central [FIX-05 PR](https://github.com/ChelseaKR/tods-validate/pull/22) populates structured `Finding.data` across every remaining ERROR-band rule, threads machine-form suggestions into JSON, fixes a `dataclasses.field` name collision that broke package imports, and adds dashboard-style tests that consume offending values without parsing prose. Nearby Fable co-authored PRs add [stamped fleet-wide Markdown reports](https://github.com/ChelseaKR/tods-validate/pull/17) and [vehicle-label pseudonymization plus residual-risk tables](https://github.com/ChelseaKR/tods-validate/pull/28), so the output is both more automatable and clearer about columns still carried through unprotected.

### [TomeSonic hardens account-switch and reader lifecycle edges](https://github.com/AwsomeFox/tomesonic-app/pull/38) — AwsomeFox / TomeSonic
TomeSonic’s Round 7 Fable 5 PR is a 16-commit maintenance sweep across 33 files, starting with account-lifecycle bugs that could leak progress work across logout or account-switch windows. The app now snapshots session identity around `/api/me`, stops playback and clears auto-download mirrors on forced logout, stamps offline progress queue entries with `address::userId`, and ignores refreshes whose config lacks the stored session user ID. Later commits cover PDF unmount flushing, format-switch target resolution, reader and player UI parity, accessibility backfills, confirmation prompts, certificate-aware connection errors, and regression tests for persistence and retry paths.

### [A Rust fuzzer becomes self-contained and differential](https://github.com/jmanchuck/rustsmith/pull/3) — jmanchuck / rustsmith
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored rustsmith PR turns the project from a generator into a more complete compiler-fuzzing harness. The branch removes external dependencies so generated programs and the workspace build offline, adds a seeded xoshiro-based PRNG, emits self-contained Rust files with an inline checksum oracle, and introduces a parallel `fuzz.py` driver that compiles across toolchain and optimization matrices while classifying crashes, timeouts, and output divergences. The commit trail also records Fable fixing its own campaign infrastructure bugs, including an opt-level environment variable that never reached Cargo and nondeterministic type choices that bypassed the seed.

### [Mitos clones sandbox pools so org namespaces can be isolated](https://github.com/mitos-run/mitos/pull/746) — stubbi / Mitos
Mitos used a Claude Fable 5 branch to close the missing piece in its per-organization Kubernetes tenancy plan. The reconciler already created namespaces, quotas, limit ranges, NetworkPolicies, and RBAC, but not the `SandboxPool` each org needed to fork workloads, so hosted deployments still had to rely on a shared namespace. The PR clones a configured reference pool's full spec into each org namespace with `warm.min` overridden to zero, wires the behavior through controller flags and Helm values, documents the threat-model effect, and adds builder plus envtest coverage for clone and skip paths.

### [PyTorch Inductor restores effect ordering after a distributed deadlock](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/189209) — d4l3k / PyTorch
A PyTorch Inductor PR authored with Claude Code and Fable fixes a subtle scheduler bug in `with_effects` lowering. Star dependencies were recorded under `bufN` buffer names while the scheduler looked them up under `opN` operation names, so effect-chain ordering edges silently disappeared; after simple overlap became default, compiled torchcomms graphs could hoist a blocking receive above its paired send and deadlock every rank. The patch changes the keying to operation names, adds a regression test that failed before the fix, and reports an end-to-end 4×H100 torchcomms run where the previously hanging fullgraph send/recv suite passed with 22 tests and 210 subtests.

### [nanoclaw query history learns to stay inside tool-result budgets](https://github.com/jbaruch/nanoclaw-core/pull/78) — jbaruch / nanoclaw-core
A `nanoclaw-core` PR marked `Author-Model: claude-fable-5` fixes a context-recovery helper whose JSON output could exceed the 25 KB tool-result budget it was meant to protect. The new behavior clips oversized row content first, then drops oldest rows with explicit `content_truncated`, `rows_dropped`, and top-level `truncated` markers, and it adds `--offset` paging because the old error message recommended batching that the CLI did not expose. The PR updates the skill contract and reports new tests for paging, row clipping, envelope clipping, and negative offsets, with ruff, pyright, and 35 pytest tests passing.

### [Conference-feed writers stop losing each other's updates](https://github.com/jbaruch/nanoclaw-conferences/pull/41) — jbaruch / nanoclaw-conferences
Another `nanoclaw` PR marked `Author-Model: claude-fable-5` fixes a race in conference CFP state maintenance. The existing temp-file plus `os.replace` discipline prevented partial `cfp-state.json` files, but two writers could still read the same old state and let the later complete write discard the earlier update. The branch adds a persistent sibling `fcntl.flock` lock file around all six read-modify-write scripts, leaves read-only consumers lock-free, maps lock timeouts to the tools' documented exit contract, and reports 300 pytest tests passing with new coverage for lock creation, contention, environment override, and a threaded lost-update regression.

## 2026-07-08

### [Rocq retries failed typeclass searches only after preserving the green path](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22228) — Jason Gross / Rocq
Jason Gross opened a Claude Fable 5-written Rocq PR for a typeclass-resolution regression that broke fiat-crypto on the current Rocq development image. The fix leaves the existing resolution fixpoint alone, but when best-effort resolution fails it retries once with closed goals ordered last, so later goals can instantiate evars without perturbing resolutions that already succeed. The PR records two earlier Fable designs that failed full CI — including stack overflows in downstream projects and goal-count drift — before the final three-file patch, a distilled regression test, a full local test run, and verification that fiat-crypto's `MontgomeryLadder.v` compiles again on both the older offending commit and current master.

### [A SO-101 robot-arm stack reaches millimeter-scale simulated IK](https://github.com/pawntest/vla_play/pull/1) — pawntest / vla_play
A Claude Fable 5-authored `vla_play` PR scaffolds a full SO-101 robot-arm tool rather than a demo script: MuJoCo and mink kinematics, frozen robot/control contracts, a velocity-limited sim backend, safety filtering, a control loop, a Viser 3D view, a real-robot backend, a natural-language agent, a policy runner, CLI commands, docs, and vendored robotstudio SO-101 assets. The author reports that mink differential IK converges to about 1 mm on the 5-DOF arm. The branch spans 56 files and more than 10,000 added lines, with tests for kinematics, safety, simulation, the control loop, the robot backend, the NL agent, and a headless smoke path that was deflaked after the first implementation.

### [An election model audit turns briefing claims into code changes and reports](https://github.com/Hijodeagua/Election-models-by-Tre/pull/15) — Hijodeagua / Election-models-by-Tre
A nine-commit Claude Fable 5 PR for an election-model repository starts by verifying seven findings from a June 2026 data-science briefing, then converts the audit into code, generated data, and a public methodology report. The branch corrects several important interpretations: no `trained_params.json` exists so one finding affects future training rather than shipped numbers, the real unknown-pollster issue is a training/serving ratings skew, and a complement-derivation bias only gates opt-in state-space paths. It touches 38 files across calibration, cross-validation, pollster weighting, Senate simulation, data export, refreshed public JSON, and the methodology page, with later commits merging fresh poll data and recording post-merge control probabilities.

### [A graphics-engine experiment gives Fable a blunt negative grade](https://github.com/adepke/VanguardEngine/pull/61) — Andrew Depke / VanguardEngine
Andrew Depke used Claude Fable 5 to draft a new volumetric temporal accumulation and upscale implementation for VanguardEngine, then posted the result as an open PR specifically to evaluate how the model performed on the hard rendering task. His summary is negative: the output is much blurrier than baseline, has temporal instability and edge shimmering, and still needs substantial refinement after repeated iteration. The PR includes headless render commands plus side-by-side screenshots of the Fable implementation and the baseline, making it a useful failure case rather than a vague complaint.

### [Six karaoke app reliability and security bugs fixed in one pass](https://github.com/bevlee/karaoke/pull/1) — bevlee / karaoke
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored karaoke-app PR fixes six concrete findings across playback, WebSockets, range requests, and cache paths. It stops React StrictMode from spawning zombie reconnect loops, gives consecutive duplicate songs unique queue IDs so playback effects re-run, guards against double-skips from competing end events, clamps the current index after queue edits, rewrites audio range handling to RFC 9110 semantics with stream-error handling, and validates unauthenticated YouTube IDs before using them in filesystem or `yt-dlp` paths. The patch is small — eight files, 134 additions — but each item maps to a named failure mode in the PR body.

### [Calendar markdown stops exploding into phantom event chips](https://github.com/dmarzzz/shape-rotator-os/pull/583) — Mikeishiring / shape-rotator-os
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored shape-rotator-os PR fixes a live calendar bug where organizer descriptions containing blank lines were parsed as new event blocks. The Jul 7 salon had grown roughly 30 phantom chips from five real events because `eventBlock` emitted raw markdown and the renderer split same-day events on the same blank-line delimiter. The localized builder fix flattens internal blank lines, preserves title and time, strips literal markdown markers, keeps the Meet-marker path unchanged, and reports 11 unit tests plus an end-to-end check against the real renderer where the buggy day returns to five correctly titled events.

### [SAM model loads stop redownloading a 350 MB encoder](https://github.com/arjunrajlaboratory/NimbusImage/pull/1219) — arjunrajlab / NimbusImage
NimbusImage used a Claude Fable 5 session to speed up and harden the browser-side Segment Anything tool. The PR caches the roughly 350 MB vit_b ONNX encoder in the browser Cache Storage API, requests the high-performance WebGPU adapter, stops keeping duplicate model bytes alive in the JavaScript heap, rejects non-OK model fetches, and avoids turning transient network errors into a permanently broken session. A follow-up commit upgrades onnxruntime-web from an old 1.19 dev snapshot to 1.27.0 and reports headless Chromium checks on both wasm and WebGPU providers, output parity with Python ONNX Runtime within 3e-7, `pnpm tsc`, lint, build, and 2,123 Vitest tests passing, while clearly leaving a real-hardware WebGPU smoke as the remaining manual check.

### [Flux scheduling fixes a policy-dependent granule match bug](https://github.com/flux-framework/flux-sched/pull/1505) — milroy / Flux Framework
Flux Framework’s scheduler PR is explicitly described as a collaboration with Claude Fable 5 and fixes a resource-matching bug exposed by `first*` match policies. Multi-slot jobspecs over high-capacity resource vertices could under-match under dynamic exploration or over-count under static exploration because the code tested aggregate capacity against per-slot requests instead of packable whole-edge-group shares. The patch adds a shared granule-aware tally, fixes a mislabeled test that had been masking the first-policy path, and adds `t3036` coverage across dynamic and static policies; the discussion also notes a related `ERANGE` report that this change does not claim to fix.

### [Fetch-tooling repairs recover policy research that had been bot-blocked](https://github.com/thecolab-ai/the-for-good-project/pull/810) — adam91holt / The For Good Project
The For Good Project has a cluster of Claude Fable 5 re-research PRs after a new fetch ladder added Jina and stealth-browser paths for previously blocked public sources. One youth-mental-health redo recovered New Zealand Health Survey regional tables and turned a former gap into a 15–24 cross-tab: high or very high psychological distress ranges from Central | Te Ikaroa at 30.6% to Midland | Te Manawa Taki at 15.2%, with unmet need highest where distress is highest. Nearby PRs recover a [five-Act, 142-provision statutory-amendment taxonomy](https://github.com/thecolab-ai/the-for-good-project/pull/795), [British Business Bank Angel CoFund and Regional Angels figures](https://github.com/thecolab-ai/the-for-good-project/pull/796), [Ministry of Transport battery-EV kilometres at 2.16% of light-vehicle travel in 2024](https://github.com/thecolab-ai/the-for-good-project/pull/802), and the [DataGrid-versus-CAFCA 1 GW versus roughly 280 MW dispute](https://github.com/thecolab-ai/the-for-good-project/pull/799), each with validation runs and frontmatter updated to `model: claude-fable-5`.

### [A TypeScript checker clone tightens two tsc-parity edge cases](https://github.com/tsz-org/tsz/pull/15630) — mohsen1 / tsz
Two `tsz` pull requests carry Claude session trailers and read like a careful differential-compatibility pass against TypeScript. [One](https://github.com/tsz-org/tsz/pull/15629) defers cross-evaluator re-entry of in-flight `Application` expansions so mutually recursive conditional-alias graphs do not keep re-walking the same node until depth caps block cache writes; its test matrix covers renamed binders, real errors, divergent aliases, and accumulator trampolines. The next PR generalizes nested literal sources the way `tsc 6.0.2` does — for example rendering a failing `"no"` leaf as `string` when the target cannot hold a singleton — after sweeps showed the leak across tuple, property, array-element, member-return, callback-return, and overload frames.

### [Airflow Databricks auth adds a supplied-token OIDC path](https://github.com/apache/airflow/pull/69272) — kaxil / Apache Airflow
An Apache Airflow Databricks-provider PR adds a `federated_token_provider` connection extra: a trusted callable that mints a short-lived OIDC JWT in-process, which the hook exchanges through Databricks’ RFC 8693 token endpoint instead of relying only on a Kubernetes service-account token from disk. The async path offloads the provider call so deferrable operators do not block the triggerer event loop, whitespace-only returns fail locally, and higher-precedence credentials still win. A Databricks reviewer posted a real-workspace verification note — service-principal federation policy, locally signed RS256 JWT, `_get_token` and `_a_get_token` happy paths, authenticated API call, cache reuse, and invalid-token negative checks — and disclosed that the note was drafted with Claude Code using Fable 5 before human review.

### [PostHog deduplicates server-side distinct ID lookups per request](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog-js/pull/4093) — dustinbyrne / PostHog
PostHog’s Next.js SDK PR says it was authored with Claude Code using Fable 5, with the key approach coming from a verified research pass over Vercel’s Flags SDK. The change memoizes `getDistinctId` resolution in a request-scoped `WeakMap` keyed by Next’s `headers()` instance, so a layout, page, and server components calling `getPostHog()` in the same App Router render do not each repeat an I/O-backed session lookup. It keeps resolver functions independent, deliberately caches rejections so redirects rethrow consistently, leaves the Pages Router path unchanged, updates JSDoc away from recommending React `cache()`, and ships across a 23-file stack that also introduces the `createPostHog` server factory.

### [Kvrocks caps deeply nested Lua replies before they crash workers](https://github.com/apache/kvrocks/pull/3547) — git-hulk / Apache Kvrocks
A Kvrocks PR marked “Assistant-By Claude Fable 5” fixes a server crash path in Redis-compatible Lua scripting. `ReplyToRedisReply()` converted Lua return values into RESP with native C++ recursion after `lua_pcall()` returned, so a self-nested table could overflow a worker thread stack rather than hitting Lua’s own guard. The first Fable patch used `lua_checkstack()`, but a follow-up commit records that this still waited until roughly 8,000 frames and could miss small worker stacks; the branch now caps reply nesting explicitly, surfaces a top-level error, and adds a Go regression test in the scripting suite.

### [Apiome adds live faceted search over MCP catalog metrics](https://github.com/apiome/apiome/pull/4701) — KenSuenobu / Apiome
Apiome’s merged PR says Claude Code using Fable 5 implemented a faceted MCP catalog API and UI over data that previously existed only as rich metrics. The new `GET /v1/mcp/{tenant_slug}/facets` route supports AND-across-facets and OR-within-facet filtering across grade, transport, category, safety, complexity, protocol, health, visibility, and pagination, while returning live bucket counts from the same filtered set. The author reports 4,027 backend pytest tests, 5,160 UI tests, a green root build, and 33 live-dev-DB acceptance checks covering counts, sentinel buckets, empty results, visibility, tenant isolation, and pagination.

### [Nextcloud SSO stops crashing on late service connections](https://github.com/nextcloud/Android-SingleSignOn/pull/1023) — MiMoHo / Nextcloud Android-SingleSignOn
A Nextcloud Android-SingleSignOn PR discloses that it was developed with Claude Code on `claude-fable-5` and fixes a crash reported by host apps such as nextcloud/notes-android. If `NextcloudAPI#close()` ran before an asynchronous bind to `AccountManagerService` completed, the pending `ServiceConnection` stayed registered and later called `onConnected()` on a nulled listener, producing a main-thread `NullPointerException`. The patch tracks requested bindings, unbinds even before establishment, ignores stale connection callbacks, and adds three JVM unit tests for the regular flow, stale callback after close, and close-before-connected unbind path.

### [Airflow streams Kubernetes task logs instead of materializing hundreds of megabytes](https://github.com/apache/airflow/pull/69300) — jason810496 / Apache Airflow
An Apache Airflow KubernetesExecutor PR says Claude Code Fable 5 helped implement streaming logs for running pods, using the new `BaseExecutor.get_streaming_task_log` interface so log lines flow lazily into Airflow’s bounded accumulator. The author reports the benchmark from the parent design: serving a roughly 415 MB running-task log drops peak heap growth from about 2,093.9 MiB to 179.9 MiB, an 11.6× reduction. A related [configuration PR](https://github.com/apache/airflow/pull/69301) adds `running_pod_log_lines` so KubernetesExecutor users can control the tail-line cap instead of inheriting the previous hardcoded value.

### [SpecR exposes confidence and provenance for inferred document hierarchy](https://github.com/wrzonance/SpecR/pull/418) — thewrz / SpecR
SpecR’s Claude Fable 5 co-authored hierarchy-inference branch persists `{ signalUsed, agreed }` provenance per paragraph and derives 0–1 confidence metadata at read time instead of storing a score that would need migration when the formula changes. The PR surfaces `meta.inference` in REST and MCP responses, adds a hierarchy section to onboarding reports, keeps SEC-sourced specs explicit rather than suspect, and proves zero classification drift over a 703-file fixture corpus. The same run also produced a smaller [re-import fix](https://github.com/wrzonance/SpecR/pull/417) for a live-reproduced bug where re-uploading a withdrawn library master rebuilt content inside a tombstoned row and left the fresh parse invisible.

### [Cribbage Trainer deep-links complete analysis state](https://github.com/markafitzgerald1/cribbage-trainer/pull/646) — markafitzgerald1 / Cribbage Trainer
Cribbage Trainer’s Fable 5 co-authored PR turns the analysis URL into a full state contract rather than only a hand parameter. Links can now carry six-card deal order, dealer-or-pone role, selected discards, sort order, and seed, with strict-but-soft parsing that rejects wrong counts, bad cards, duplicates, and discards not in the hand without crashing the app. The branch documents the compatibility surface, ignores local `.claude` worktrees that had doubled Jest runs, and records review-driven fixes for comma decoding, role-only links, popstate races, and stable browser-history behavior; validation reached 71 Playwright tests and 440 Jest tests at 100% coverage.

### [Policy research reruns recover previously blocked New Zealand health sources](https://github.com/thecolab-ai/the-for-good-project/pull/798) — adam91holt / The For Good Project
Two Fable 5 re-research PRs in The For Good Project revisit New Zealand public-policy findings after a fetch-tooling upgrade removed earlier bot-blocked-source caveats. The mental-health [rerun](https://github.com/thecolab-ai/the-for-good-project/pull/798) directly reads Budget 2025 material, the Beehive 111 crisis-response release, RNZ line items, Health NZ service-use data, and Te Hiringa Mahara monitoring, upgrading the “no published national community bed count” finding while saying an OIA is still needed for the precise number. The HPAI [rerun](https://github.com/thecolab-ai/the-for-good-project/pull/797) fetches MPI’s 22-page One Health strategy in full and concludes that the public flagship document still provides no sized wild-bird surveillance design, site list, seasonal frame, detection probability, or escalation trigger.

### [A relay cache gets a global memory budget and live-edge pinning](https://github.com/moq-dev/moq/pull/2110) — kixelated / moq-dev
A moq-dev PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 tackles relay memory growth in `moq-net`: non-latest media groups were cached per track with only a TTL, so memory scaled with track count and would become unbounded if TTLs rose for FETCH. The patch adds a process-wide LRU byte budget where eviction aborts groups, wakes parked readers, pins each track's latest group, and accounts for late frames plus small groups. The commit trail also shows Fable fixing review and CI-discovered edges — repinning refetched latest groups, honoring eviction on the in-place frame-read path, and adding diagnostics for Linux-only eviction-order failures — while later refactors in the same branch are clearly attributed to Opus rather than Fable.

### [A TypeScript checker clone closes literal-display parity gaps](https://github.com/tsz-org/tsz/pull/15633) — mohsen1 / tsz
The `tsz` checker has another Fable 5 compatibility pass aimed at matching `tsc 6.0.2` diagnostics rather than just accepting the same programs. This PR generalizes failing literal sources the way TypeScript does across union, enum-member, tuple, property, array-element, return-type, overload, and top-level relation surfaces, with solver changes for instantiable constraints, lazy applications, conditionals, substitutions, and boolean unit pairs. The body lists each witness gap verified against `tsc --noEmit --strict --pretty false`, and the seven commits carry Claude session trailers through fix and simplification passes.

### [An economics paper adds machine checks after five simulated referee reports](https://github.com/PolicyEngine/llm-econ-beliefs/pull/7) — Max Ghenis / PolicyEngine
PolicyEngine’s `llm-econ-beliefs` paper used a Fable co-authored revision round to reconcile prose with regenerated 17-model tables after five simulated RSOS referee reports. The merged PR re-derives every ranking superlative, including a new place for Claude Fable 5 as the tightest labor-tax model, fixes stale constants and statutory anchors, clarifies panel counts and model budgets, and rerenders the paper. More importantly, it adds `scripts/verify_paper_prose.py` with 27 checks that assert the headline numbers and model-name superlatives against committed tables, alongside 71 passing pytest tests and rendered HTML/PDF spot checks.

### [A Unity XR provider fixes a macOS built-player input trap](https://github.com/DisplayXR/displayxr-unity/pull/214) — dfattal / DisplayXR
DisplayXR merged a Fable 5 co-authored fix for a macOS Unity-player bug where the provider opened a separate weave window while Unity’s real, tiny game window still owned mouse and keyboard input. The patch completes the in-app weave path: built players now attach an input-transparent overlay `NSView` to Unity’s own window, grow it to the display aspect, keep the editor preview path separate, and tear down whichever target was used. The author verified it in a BiRP CubeTest built player with a single correctly titled window, working stereo and focus, user-confirmed input, and a passing MinGW Windows cross-check.

### [Obsidian live sync starts materializing renamed notes again](https://github.com/engram-app/Engram-obsidian/pull/196) — Rasbandit / Engram
Engram’s Obsidian sync PR, co-authored by Claude Fable 5, starts from a deterministic end-to-end failure: after a rename broadcast, a receiving device recorded `received=yes` but never wrote the renamed note to disk because the WebSocket event carried no content change and CRDT enrollment was a no-op for an already-seen note. The fix first flushes the already-synced CRDT document to the new path, then a follow-up commit handles the fresh-boot case by copying trustworthy old-path disk content and ignoring stale backwards relocation events. The PR records red/green e2e evidence for `test_10`, a related echo-suppression fix, and new failing-first unit tests for both remaining rename-family bugs.

### [Harper tests HTTP/2 over a cleartext Unix-socket mirror](https://github.com/HarperFast/harper/pull/1707) — kriszyp / Harper
Harper’s experimental HTTP/2 ingress work was drafted with Claude Fable 5 and paired with a companion [symphony proxy PR](https://github.com/HarperFast/symphony/pull/24). The Harper side adds a flag-gated h2c Unix-domain-socket mirror that reuses the existing request and throttle pipeline, consumes an optional PROXY v1 header before handing the socket to Node’s HTTP/2 server, and drains live sessions with GOAWAY on close. The PR body reports benchmark work behind the design: translating h2 to h1 in the proxy cost roughly 25–30% more CPU end-to-end, while the direct h2c mirror measured 18.2 µs of CPU per request against 10.2 µs for the h1 mirror; the proxy side then dispatches by ALPN while rejecting h2 configurations that would corrupt the connection preface.

### [A profiler-fed Claude pass cuts pytest overhead on tiny tests](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/pull/14687) — Henry Schreiner / pytest
Henry Schreiner profiled pytest on pypa/packaging’s roughly 62,000-test suite with Python 3.15’s sampling profiler, fed the report to Claude, and credited the resulting patch as `Assisted-by: ClaudeCode:claude-fable-5`. The first optimization it found was in `FDCapture.snap()`: when a captured temp file is empty, a single `fstat` can skip the old `seek/read/seek/truncate` cycle. Schreiner reports that this removes about six cycles per test across stdout and stderr capture phases and cuts total runtime for that suite by about 8%.

### [Grails adds latency injection to turn UI flakes into local failures](https://github.com/apache/grails-core/pull/15937) — jdaugherty / Apache Grails
A Grails PR prepared with Claude Code and Claude Fable 5 adds a `grails-testing-support-latency` module for deliberately slowing selected requests in functional tests. The new filter has configurable delay bounds, probability, URL patterns, and seeds, fails fast on invalid configuration, and ships with an example app plus documentation warning that it is testing-only support. The author’s verification covers 10 unit tests for bounds, pass-through, and config validation; two end-to-end tests against the latency example app; a clean code-style run; and a docs build with the new functional-testing guide section rendered.

### [AetherSDR traces a second-pan crash to an old Intel D3D11 swapchain path](https://github.com/aethersdr/AetherSDR/pull/4120) — jensenpat / AetherSDR
AetherSDR’s Claude Fable 5 co-authored fix starts from a reporter’s Windows 10 minidump on an Intel HD Graphics 520 machine, where adding a second panadapter crashed inside `igd10iumd64.dll` while Qt rebuilt a D3D11 swapchain. The patch avoids transient top-level reparenting for panes that stay docked, collapses splitter layout to one resize, and pins `QRhiWidget` color buffers to even-aligned device-pixel sizes so fractional UI scale does not hand odd texture extents to the driver. The author reports a clean RelWithDebInfo build, 76 passing ctests, a live FLEX-8400M macOS/Metal smoke, and a later reporter confirmation that one to four pans worked across UI scale settings without crashing.

### [tsz matches TypeScript’s generator-yield literal widening cases](https://github.com/tsz-org/tsz/pull/15634) — mohsen1 / tsz
The `tsz` checker’s merged Fable-attributed PR pays down the `generatorTypeCheck63.ts` conformance row by matching TypeScript 6.0.3’s treatment of fresh literals yielded from unannotated generators. The body walks through the structural rule, including why `yield 1` under an `IterableIterator<T | undefined, void>` contextual signature should widen to `number`, while `yield 1 as const`, annotated `const` references, and multi-literal unions should keep their narrower forms. Verification includes 14 new targeted tests, an A/B full checker run with no new failures, 114 generator/yield conformance cases, and declaration-emit differentials for the matrix in the PR body.

### [finterm global installs find their bundled docs again](https://github.com/finterm-ai/finterm-cli/pull/12) — jlevy / finterm-cli
A Fable 5 co-authored `finterm-cli` PR fixes a packaging bug that only appeared after global npm install: the executable symlink made the old `/dist` detection miss the bundled documentation, so `skill`, `docs`, `prime`, and `setup` fell back to in-repo placeholders or exited with `SKILL.md not found`. The shared resolver now starts from `import.meta.url`, then tries raw and realpathed script locations before the development fallback. The pack smoke test was extended to run from a neutral working directory and assert real bundled content plus a working `finterm setup`, giving the PR a red-green guard against the exact published 0.2.0/0.2.1 failure.

### [OmniRoute tests context-as-image compression for Fable-only routes](https://github.com/diegosouzapw/OmniRoute/pull/6556) — diegosouzapw / OmniRoute
OmniRoute’s preview PR adds `omniglyph`, a context-as-image compression engine restricted to Claude Fable 5 on the direct Anthropic route. It renders system prompt, tool documentation, and dense history into compact PNG pages and wires the engine through single and stacked compression modes with fail-closed gates for vision support, model, transport, source format, and profitability. The author reports a converted-block reduction of about 10× tokens and 59–70% end-to-end on measured examples, fixes the inflation guard to bill image blocks by dimensions instead of base64 characters, and records 1,249 compression tests passing plus a clean core typecheck and production-file lint after switching from a local file dependency to the published package.

### [Compound Engineering validates solution-document claims before saving them](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin/pull/1073) — tmchow / Every
Every’s Compound Engineering plugin PR, marked with a Fable 5 badge, fixes a failure mode where generated `docs/solutions/` entries could turn conversation summaries into permanent project knowledge without checking the repository. The new flow requires source-line reads before behavior claims, prefers PR numbers over fragile SHAs, runs a bundled mechanical validator for paths, commits, links, scaffold text, and stale-checkout state, and uses a semantic validator in full and headless modes to quote source lines and check merge-state claims against remote truth. The author reports 1,848 Bun tests passing, 31 new validator tests, and behavioral evals improving from 11/17 to 17/17 assertions on planted traps.

### [Highlighter replaces its Lua parser with a Luau lexer](https://github.com/boatbomber/Highlighter/pull/42) — boatbomber / Highlighter
Highlighter’s maintainer says Claude Fable 5 assisted a refactor from the old classic-Lua parser to a new lexer that handles Luau syntax. The branch spans 32 files and includes strict token typing, Jest scaffolding, Rojo/Rokit tooling updates, hot-swapping restoration, documentation, and a benchmark commit. The author’s short evaluation is concrete: the old highlighter “would get messed up” on Luau syntax, while the new lexer is described as both more accurate for Luau and faster.

### [Polars-CV fixes average-precision math and unsafe data imports](https://github.com/heshamdar/polars-cv/pull/27) — heshamdar / polars-cv
A Fable 5 co-authored `polars-cv` branch starts with two evaluation-metric bugs: VOC 11-point AP was overestimated when undetected recall thresholds were dropped from the denominator, and `bootstrap_pr_auc` compared an enveloped point estimate against raw trapezoidal bootstrap replicates. The same eight-commit session also fixes rotate/affine fusion using the wrong shape hints, contiguous-stride declarations after materializing kernels, unchecked blob-header arithmetic plus hostile stride spans, Arrow C-Data imports that ignored array offsets and nulls, `file://` local reads, and aligned buffer deallocation. The PR body and commit messages give worked failure modes rather than only broad “bug fix” summaries.

### [Mallard stops deleting its event history on extension restart](https://github.com/RedPandaMC/Mallard/pull/57) — RedPandaMC / Mallard
Mallard’s Claude Fable 5 co-authored PR fixes a VS Code extension lifecycle bug where `deactivate()` wiped `events.duckdb` and global persisted flags on every window close or extension-host restart. That defeated migrations, ingest watermarks, retention, and any history older than rotated source logs, so the fix makes `deactivate()` a no-op and leaves full deletion to the explicit “Prepare for Uninstall” command. The broader 11-commit branch also separates pure domain code from VS Code and Node dependencies, moves dashboard layout into config, adds attribution provenance, and keeps Codecov reporting every modified coverable line covered.

### [QuantEcon adds repeatable DiscreteDP benchmarks and matching Python guards](https://github.com/QuantEcon/QuantEcon.jl/pull/383) — oyamad / QuantEcon
A Fable 5 co-authored QuantEcon.jl PR adds a BenchmarkTools/PkgBenchmark suite for `DiscreteDP`, covering four fixed-seed model cases and nine operations so future changes can be compared with `PkgBenchmark.judge`. A companion Python [PR](https://github.com/QuantEcon/QuantEcon.py/pull/855) fixes edge cases found while reviewing the module against the Julia implementation: duplicate state-action pairs could silently corrupt a model, trailing no-action states could read out of bounds under Numba, `operator_iteration` returned the wrong shape at zero iterations, and `beta` validation happened too late. Together they show Fable being used both to establish a reproducible performance baseline and to close cross-language correctness gaps.

### [moor keeps raw terminal mode after cmd.exe and Node reset it](https://github.com/walles/moor/pull/444) — walles / moor
A Fable 5 co-authored `moor` PR fixes keyboard breakage caused by other processes sharing the same terminal. On Windows, `cmd.exe` reset console modes while running batch files or ending a pipe; on Unix, a `node ... | moor` pipeline could restore cooked mode after `moor` had gone raw, leaving keystrokes echoed and line-buffered instead of delivered to the pager. The fix reasserts the wanted terminal mode from the read loop at least every 100 ms, preserves unrelated console flags, avoids reasserting during intentional cooked-mode pauses, and adds logging plus docs for the reset path.

### [Elden Ring save picking moves from OS dialogs into game menus](https://github.com/Banon-Labs/er-effects-rs/pull/40) — chozandrias76 / er-effects-rs
An `er-effects-rs` branch with Fable 5 trailers replaces two Windows file-picker dialogs with in-game menus rendered through Elden Ring’s native profile-selection UI. The System→Quit picker becomes a directory browser inside a 10-row game window, while the startup missing-save path boots to the native no-save title menu, auto-opens the picker, validates the chosen save through the old BND4/SteamID pipeline, and reloads through the game’s own return-to-title state. The 27-commit history records reverse-engineering constraints, live no-save test failures, overlay fallbacks, save-source protection, and guard fixes uncovered while making the native flow usable.

### [Outline stops Redis cache clears from blocking the server](https://github.com/outline/outline/pull/12934) — tommoor / Outline
Outline’s Claude Fable 5 co-authored PR replaces a startup cache-clear path that used Redis `KEYS`, which blocks the event loop while scanning the entire keyspace. The new helper walks matching prefixes with incremental `SCAN` and deletes in batches with `UNLINK`, so discovery and memory reclamation avoid stalling other clients on large installations. The change is small — two files, 96 additions, and seven deletions — but includes CacheHelper tests for multi-page prefix clears and documents the failure mode directly in the commit trail.

### [Monad hardens a secondary raptorcast client against cold-start edge cases](https://github.com/category-labs/monad-bft/pull/3157) — xinyuan-dev / Category Labs
Category Labs’ `monad-bft` PR says it was generated using Claude Fable 5 and applies a focused hardening pass to the secondary raptorcast client. It bounds `pending_confirms` and `confirmed_groups` during cold start, checks validator membership on `ConfirmGroup` and `NoConfirm` messages, restores a missing waker call in `RaptorCastSecondary::poll_next`, expires far-future cold-start entries, and rejects overlapping group invitations from the same validator. The branch changes five files with 778 additions and 242 deletions, making the report substantive enough to understand both the protocol risks and the intended fixes.

### [QuantEcon flips eight DiscreteDP broken tests into fixes](https://github.com/QuantEcon/QuantEcon.jl/pull/385) — oyamad / QuantEcon
QuantEcon.jl’s follow-up DiscreteDP branch is co-authored by Claude Fable 5 and turns the `@test_broken` markers from the preceding review into passing regression coverage. The fixes stop `solve(ddp, v_init, ...)` from mutating the caller’s vector, make modified policy iteration respect a supplied `v_init`, reject out-of-range state and action indices instead of silently reattributing sorted pairs, preserve value element types in `s_wise_max`, remove a type-pirated three-dimensional array multiplication method, and tighten beta-one warnings and convergence checks. Four Fable-attributed commits also update the benchmark suite after the internal multiplication change and align the Julia behavior with the companion QuantEcon.py edge-case fixes.

### [ngx-signal-forms closes three pre-1.0 audit findings](https://github.com/ngx-signal-forms/ngx-signal-forms/pull/201) — the-ult / ngx-signal-forms
The `ngx-signal-forms` maintainer is using Fable 5 to turn promoted pre-1.0 audit findings into regression-tested fixes across the toolkit. The Vest-adapter PR fixes two concrete bugs: default `resetOnDestroy` could reset a module-scope suite while a sibling mount still depended on it, and an internal `'root'` sentinel collided with a real Vest field named `root`; the commit says both were covered by failing-first tests. Nearby Fable co-authored PRs also make a missing form context produce the intended dev-mode warning for [`strategy="on-submit"`](https://github.com/ngx-signal-forms/ngx-signal-forms/pull/199) and restore [`@ngx-signal-forms/toolkit/testing`](https://github.com/ngx-signal-forms/ngx-signal-forms/pull/200) as a real secondary entry point, reporting build, lint, and 1,203 toolkit tests passing while noting an unrelated browser-test environment failure on a clean base checkout.

### [Dense dynamic-programming kernels get a 9x solver speedup](https://github.com/QuantEcon/QuantEcon.jl/pull/386) — oyamad / QuantEcon
QuantEcon.jl followed its Fable-assisted DiscreteDP correctness work with a Claude Fable 5 co-authored performance pass over `src/markov/ddp.jl`. The PR rewrites dense `RQ_sigma` as a direct loop fill, adds preallocated Bellman-operator buffers, makes MPFI partial evaluation in-place, and fixes follow-up NaN and mixed-precision argmax edges found while optimizing. The benchmark table reports `RQ_sigma` dropping from 16.6 ms to 0.31 ms, dense `solve_PFI` from 42.1 ms to 4.5 ms, dense `solve_MPFI` from 63.1 ms to 7.2 ms, and VFI allocation from 68 MiB to 2.8 MiB, with solutions remaining bit-identical.

### [CPython fixes a ProcessPoolExecutor worker-replacement hang](https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/152978) — Gregory P. Smith / CPython
A CPython core developer says this `concurrent.futures` fix was "drafted and investigated entirely by Claude Fable 5" from the linked issues. The bug involved `ProcessPoolExecutor` replacement workers being routed through executor attributes that `shutdown(wait=False)` can clear, or through an executor that has already been garbage-collected, leaving submitted work permanently unexecuted after a `max_tasks_per_child` exit. The patch moves replacement decisions into the executor manager thread, turns unrecoverable cases into `BrokenProcessPool` instead of unresolved futures, adds a `_force_shutting_down` guard, and triggered a full CPython buildbot run for review.

### [OpenTelemetry Explorer begins its faceted list-page rebuild](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-ecosystem-explorer/pull/784) — vitorvasc / OpenTelemetry Ecosystem Explorer
The OpenTelemetry Ecosystem Explorer’s Phase 4 list-page redesign starts with a Fable 5 co-authored PR for the three leaf controls that will make up a faceted rail. It adds `CheckboxFacet`, `SearchFacet`, and `SelectFacet` primitives, a debounced-value hook, shared facet styling, and a developer showcase, keeping all display strings supplied by the future consuming panel. The author reports a clean Bun typecheck and lint, 1,146 tests passing including 11 new facet tests, manual light/dark showcase checks, a Netlify deploy preview, and screenshot-bot coverage across desktop, tablet, and mobile pages.

### [Codrift hardens state writes, updater integrity, and local RPC auth](https://github.com/filipecabaco/codrift/pull/5) — filipecabaco / Codrift
A merged Codrift branch co-authored by Claude Fable 5 starts from a severe failure mode: a crash during `initiatives.json` writes could make the next boot load zero initiatives and delete every context directory as “orphaned.” The PR switches persistence to temp-file-plus-rename, backs up corrupt state, and skips pruning when the state file existed but failed to load. The same session fixes release-asset naming and SHA-256 verification, persists agent scrollback into durable transcript logs, distinguishes crashed agents from stopped ones, and adds loopback-token auth plus symlink-resolution guards so local RPC calls cannot write through project symlinks to arbitrary host paths.

### [A geotechnical PFN study moves from synthetic tests to Haneda boreholes](https://github.com/TsumiNa/geo_pfn/pull/5) — TsumiNa / geo_pfn
A merged `geo_pfn` PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 adds the project’s first real-data study on the Tokyo Haneda borehole pilot dataset: 3,521 specimens across 240 boreholes, predicting undrained shear strength. It documents the dataset, builds a leakage-safe borehole GroupKFold harness with feature ablations and five imputation strategies, and commits aggregate results from a 95-minute five-fold run on MPS. The reported results are concrete: TabPFN v2 beats miniPFN by 2.7–4.1 paired points on binned classification, the best zero-finetune regression recipe reaches about 14.0 kN/m² RMSE and R² 0.79, KNN imputation hurts, and missing-indicator columns help.

### [Mux traces missing AI spend across aborted turns and sub-agents](https://github.com/coder/mux/pull/3693) — ThomasK33 / Coder Mux
A Coder Mux PR generated with `anthropic:claude-fable-5` tackles a real cost-accounting gap: provider-console spend was about $1.5k per day while the dashboard reported much less. The investigation found six paths where billed tokens never reached analytics, including aborted or preempted streams committed without usage metadata, failed streams, slow `totalUsage` reads, stale zero-dollar pricing rows, deleted sub-agent child workspaces, and headless consumers such as `/btw` and memory jobs. The validation section reports a sandboxed `MUX_ROOT` dogfood run where seeded interrupted, archived-child, and control turns produced exact dashboard totals of $6.00 + $0.75 + $8.25 = $15.00, and the PR itself records a Fable run cost of $78.78.

### [An AMX bf16 matrix kernel reaches 2.4 TFLOPS on a four-core box](https://github.com/tboerstad/agentic_matmul/pull/107) — tboerstad / agentic_matmul
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored `agentic_matmul` PR implements an Intel AMX bf16 tile kernel in Mojo for aligned matrix shapes, with VNNI-packed B tiles and f32 accumulator tiles kept in registers through the K sweep. On the author’s 2.10 GHz Granite Rapids four-core machine, all 16 tested shapes beat `linalg`; `sq2048` rises from 296 to 2,438 GFLOPS in the main benchmark and 2,610 GFLOPS after switching from inline assembly to LLVM AMX intrinsics. The write-up also records negative results from the same session — a proposed prefill C-traffic/pack-overlap idea lost in measured tables, and a suspected decode gap did not reproduce on that boot.

### [A Rust policy engine cuts a shell-parse hot path by 20x](https://github.com/watany-dev/ptuf/pull/153) — watany-dev / ptuf
A `ptuf` PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 starts from `divan` and callgrind profiling of `Engine::decide`, then replaces byte-at-a-time shell-word accumulation with span copies and memchr-based scanning. It also removes allocations from the sensitive-token gate, reuses already parsed Bash facts instead of parsing twice, and skips a regex sweep when a megabyte command has no relevant sink. The median `perf/decide_shell_parse/1048576` benchmark drops from 6.386 ms to 316.6 µs, while property tests for parser behavior, classify-prefilter equivalence, and bypass cases are reported unchanged.

### [SciPy audits a SuperLU C glue file for error-path correctness](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/25585) — Ralf Gommers / SciPy
SciPy maintainer Ralf Gommers used Claude Fable 5 to audit `_superlu_utils.c` after applying a small hand fix from an issue. The resulting PR adds missing checks for `PyDict_New`, `PyDict_SetItemString`, and `PyLong_FromVoidPtr`, replaces deprecated `PyErr_Fetch` / `PyErr_Restore` usage, moves a legacy `PyTypeObject` initializer to designated fields, and tidies tabs in the same file. Gommers explicitly says the Fable suggestions were reviewed and tested by hand, and that the affected paths are mostly exit or memory-exhaustion handling — minor in normal operation, but exactly the kind of code meant to survive failure.

### [A second NumPy type-stub audit already yields fixes](https://gist.github.com/jorenham/9d55eb8ed68d85007d0aa8b0fb1b8813) — jorenham
jorenham published a second Fable 5 audit of NumPy’s Python type stubs, checked against NumPy main at `3b1e425eb3`, a local `2.6.0.dev0` runtime build, and basedpyright 1.39.8. The audit lists concrete runtime/stub mismatches with reproducing snippets and backport notes; two fixes are already open: [`np.bincount` with weights returns `float64`, not `intp`](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/31911), and [`np.strings.partition` / `rpartition` return a 3-tuple of arrays rather than one array](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/31912). Both PRs say the human wrote the code while Claude Fable 5 found the bugs, making this a clean example of audit-first model use rather than unattended patch generation.

### [Flink adds a Source V2 exactly-once failure harness](https://github.com/apache/flink/pull/28686) — Martijn Visser / Apache Flink
Apache Flink’s FLINK-40098 PR says it was generated by Claude Fable 5 and tackles a testing migration problem rather than an app feature. Roughly 15 checkpointing integration tests still depended on a legacy `SourceFunction` pattern that fails once after a completed checkpoint and resumes from restored position, while no Source V2 utility offered the same semantics. The new `FailingCheckpointedSource` holds emission until a snapshot arms failure, re-arms after aborted checkpoints, avoids double-assigning splits on partial failover, and migrates `EventTimeAllWindowCheckpointingITCase` as the first consumer across a 952-line test-utils patch.

### [Short audio clips stop going empty in batched ASR](https://github.com/mudler/parakeet.cpp/pull/49) — richiejp / parakeet.cpp
A parakeet.cpp PR assisted by `claude-fable-5` traces a silent ASR failure to local-attention masking inside heterogeneous-length batches. On streaming models with short attention windows, a heavily padded short clip could produce an all-`-inf` mask row, turn softmax into NaNs, poison later layers, and return an empty transcript; full attention and single-clip paths were unaffected, which made the bug easy to miss. The one-file C++ fix leaves fully masked padded query rows finite before the existing output mask zeroes them, with verification on nemotron-3.5 and parakeet_realtime fixtures showing the previously empty 7.4-second clip now byte-matches its solo decode across tested batch spreads.

### [Windows heartbeat IPC lands in an Elixir-to-Tauri bridge](https://github.com/filipecabaco/ex_tauri/pull/12) — Filipe Cabaco
Filipe Cabaco’s ex_tauri PR, co-authored by Claude Fable 5, closes a Windows shutdown gap where Unix-style sidecar signaling did not apply.
