The branch adds a TCP heartbeat transport that writes an OS-assigned port file, teaches the Rust template to connect and reconnect on Windows, and stops the heartbeat before quit so the sidecar exits cleanly by timeout. The same 44-file pass fixes duplicate LiveView hook registration after reconnect, byte-index corruption when multibyte characters preceded hook insertion points, silent `cargo install` failures, CLI binary resolution on Windows, and adds tests for the TCP transport and template heartbeat behavior.

### [Physlib proof golfing removes thousands of Lean lines](https://github.com/leanprover-community/physlib/pull/1364) — Vilin97 / Physlib
A Physlib draft PR discloses a large formal-proof golfing run that used Claude Fable 5 and Claude Opus 4.8 subagents, one max-reasoning agent per file. The reported final stats are about 1,561 proofs golfed across 129 Lean files, with proof bodies shrinking by 6,743 net lines while statements and definitions were left unchanged. The process included per-file `lake env lean` verification, independent re-verification, a signature audit showing declaration headers byte-identical to master, and a full `lake build` of 9,121 jobs after roughly 42 hours and about 21 million output tokens.

### [Stale context-window reports stop truncating Fable sessions](https://github.com/zts212653/clowder-ai/pull/1120) — Ryann-Leee / clowder-ai
A clowder-ai PR generated with Claude Code fixes a Fable-specific accounting bug documented from production snapshots. Claude CLI versions at or below 2.1.177 reported `claude-fable-5` as a 200,000-token context model, so clowder’s budget chain sealed four Fable variants around 190k tokens even though two recorded turns had already consumed 303,416 and 307,529 input tokens successfully. The fix applies known minimum floors — one million tokens for `claude-fable-5` and `[1m]` suffixed models — without lowering newer CLI reports, and adds eight tests for exact floors, prefix handling, stale correction, fallback interaction, plus build and Biome checks.

### [LiteLLM stops strict-tool calls from breaking on Bedrock Fable](https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/pull/32455) — seanturner83 / LiteLLM
A LiteLLM PR documents a concrete integration failure for Fable 5 on Amazon Bedrock Converse: SDKs such as `openai-agents` stamp `strict` on every tool, while Bedrock's Anthropic-compatible validator rejects `tools.0.custom.strict`, so the first tool-calling request to any affected Fable 5 profile returned a 400. The fix adds `bedrock_converse_supports_strict_tools: false` to all four Fable 5 inference-profile entries, mirrors the data into the backup cost map, and extends the regression suite so those keys drop `toolSpec.strict` before sending the request. Codecov reported every modified coverable line covered, and Greptile's review caught only a stale docstring plus a few missing regional variants in one end-to-end test.

### [A Fable-chaired review matrix closes a prompt-injection exfil path](https://github.com/Atom-oh/claude-code-usage-dashboard/pull/5) — Atom-oh
Atom-oh's `claude-code-usage-dashboard` PR is itself the result of an AI review workflow chaired by Claude Fable 5, with a lens-by-model panel reviewing the patch in public. The fix removes Kiro's `fs_read` tool grant for untrusted PR diffs after identifying a path where diff-borne prompt injection could make a privileged `pull_request_target` job read local files and leak them into a public review comment. The same branch makes panel coverage depend on real exit statuses and vendor diversity rather than non-empty stdout, then the Fable-chaired review comment walks through rejected false positives, residual risks such as argv exposure and truncation, and suggested runtime canaries.

### [A Starknet replay service grows an OS-runner stack](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14714) — ron-starkware / StarkWare
StarkWare has an eleven-PR Fable 5 co-authored Graphite stack for `echonet`, a sequencer replay service: [14711](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14711), [14712](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14712), [14713](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14713), [14714](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14714), [14715](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14715), [14716](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14716), [14717](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14717), [14718](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14718), [14719](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14719), [14720](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14720), and [14721](https://github.com/starkware-libs/sequencer/pull/14721). The stack fixes a Kubernetes PVC multi-attach rollout deadlock, stores multi-megabyte cende blobs as raw bytes to avoid 30–50 MB parsed-dict heap spikes, builds Starknet OS CLI inputs from replayed blobs, fetches missing classes from the feeder gateway with a PVC cache, and runs the OS over each replayed block through bounded worker subprocesses. Later PRs add outcome counters, recent-failure reporting, CSV-exportable UI sections, and pod sizing for an eight-worker OS-runner workload, making this a systems integration report rather than a small codegen patch.

### [Roboflow pipelines remote video frames 8x to 12x faster](https://github.com/roboflow/inference/pull/2623) — balthazur / Roboflow
A Roboflow draft PR written by Claude Fable 5 turns remote video-workflow execution into a generic stream-lookahead scheduler. The branch keeps up to N long-latency model or external-API calls in flight while stateful trackers and counters still emit frames in order; the benchmark table reports tracking at 3.52 FPS sequential versus 43.8 FPS at depth 16, two-model workflows at 2.14 to 22.8 FPS, and SAM3 segmentation at 1.51 to 12.7 FPS. The author also records tracker-ID parity with zero mismatched frames, 59 adopted blocks, and several adversarial-review fixes for swallowed tail-frame errors, lost execution context, usage-duration accounting, and hung-request shutdown.

### [Iceberg Python starts writing table-format v3 metadata](https://github.com/apache/iceberg-python/pull/3624) — moomindani / Apache Iceberg
Two Apache Iceberg Python PRs written by Claude Fable 5 add the lower-level pieces needed for format version 3 writes. The [upgrade PR](https://github.com/apache/iceberg-python/pull/3623) lets `upgrade_table_version(3)` create `TableMetadataV3` with `next-row-id=0`, while the manifest-writer PR adds V3 manifest and manifest-list writers that preserve or assign `first_row_id` according to the spec and expose `next_row_id` for the commit path. The tests cover V3 data-file fields that would otherwise serialize as null, first-row-id assignment over unassigned, preserved, delete, and second-unassigned manifests, and validation that `first_row_id` is required.

### [Flink makes a flaky TLS test assert the actual handshake boundary](https://github.com/apache/flink/pull/28676) — Martijn Visser / Apache Flink
A Flink PR generated by Claude Fable 5 narrows `RestClientITCase#testHttpsConnectionWithDefaultCerts` to the behavior it was meant to prove: that the JDK default truststore completes a TLS handshake. GitHub Actions sometimes returned plain-text 429s from the public endpoint, so the old JSON-body assertion failed even though TLS had already succeeded; the new test treats `RestClientException` after an HTTP response as success for the handshake case while still failing on real `SSLException` certificate errors. The author verified the normal endpoint, a valid-TLS non-JSON endpoint, and `expired.badssl.com`, and the Flink bot reported the Azure run green.

### [LLVM vector lowering stops crashing on untraceable masks](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/207299) — marquisburg / LLVM
An LLVM contributor disclosed Claude Fable 5 assistance on a small but concrete MLIR crash fix. The vector narrowing transform traced masks backward assuming it would always find `create_mask`, `constant_mask`, or `arith.constant`; a block-argument mask could instead leave a null defining op and crash, while other unsupported defining ops could loop forever. The patch bails out cleanly and adds negative maskedstore and maskedload tests so the unsupported cases remain in the original form rather than taking the crashing rewrite path.

### [A fraud benchmark shows the same refusals by a different mechanism](https://x.com/Lhmisme/status/2074845208726921231) — Lhmisme
Lhmisme reports a VATA adversarial run against Claude Fable 5 with 75 trials each across insurance-refund fraud, payroll diversion, and executive wire transfer, using a fixed Grok 4.3 attacker and preserving raw logs behind a Sepolia anchor. The measured attacker success rate was 0% in all three domains, matching the prior Claude Opus 4.8 baseline, but the path changed: Fable returned hard API-level `stop_reason: "refusal"` before full reasoning in 61/75 insurance trials, 68/75 payroll trials, and 75/75 executive-wire trials. The monotonic refusal rates make this a useful safety-mechanism report rather than a generic guardrail complaint.

### [A Godot physics fork gets replayable Box3D debugging and game-driven fixes](https://github.com/bearlikelion/godot-box3d/pull/5) — 01-ws-ops / godot-box3d
The godot-box3d maintainer says all fork updates in this PR were handled through Claude Fable 5, then validated by dropping the fork into an upcoming game project. The patch fixes real physics-integration failures — reverse-facing trimesh collisions, Godot-style one-way collision masks, stale shape data after `set_faces`, slope and character-crowd motion bugs, default-area gravity queries, and MSVC build breakage — while adding scaled transforms, pair exceptions, cone-twist joints, cylinders, and a `Box3DRecording` singleton for deterministic record/replay debugging. A comment thread also records a post-fix regression found during character-slide testing, which makes the report useful as ongoing field use rather than a polished one-shot demo.

### [GraphQL field validation gets a denial-of-service guard](https://github.com/bcmyguest/graphql-core/pull/1) — bcmyguest / graphql-core
A PR assisted by `Claude:claude-fable-5` ports a GraphQL validation hardening fix for repeated fields with the same response name. The old `OverlappingFieldsCanBeMerged` rule could perform quadratic pairwise comparisons on queries like `{ hello hello hello ... }`, making thousands of duplicate fields a CPU denial-of-service path. The branch deduplicates structurally identical fields before comparison, adds a comparison budget, and includes long-running regression coverage across five commits rather than relying on a prose mitigation.

### [AQL measures interpreter spawn overhead before redesigning engine reuse](https://github.com/aql-lang/aql/pull/233) — rjrodger / aql
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored AQL design-and-implementation PR audits every production `New(r).Run` sub-engine spawn site and asks which ones can safely run on the main tape using mark/move cells. The finding is not just architectural: the PR reports fresh engine-plus-tape allocation around 92 µs and 189 KB per callback versus about 9 µs and 112 B for reuse, then recommends a VM-island pattern for hot per-element sites while preserving sub-engines where they serve as error traps or hook seams. The 21-commit branch follows the audit with pooled sub-engines, unified named and unnamed argument semantics, coverage programs, and a linked Claude session for reproducibility.

### [PyTorch split groups preserve caller rank order](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/189090) — d4l3k / PyTorch
A PyTorch distributed PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 fixes `dist.split_group` silently sorting subgroup rank lists, which made callers unable to control rank assignment inside a subgroup and blocked delegation from `new_group(sort_ranks=False)`. The author explains the compatibility tradeoff, checks public code search for external users relying on unsorted inputs being sorted, and verifies that NCCL and Gloo split semantics care about consistent ordering rather than sorted ordering. The test plan includes a new reversed-rank unit test plus an end-to-end four-rank NCCL scratch script exercising `all_reduce`, `broadcast`, and the raw pybind `pg.split_group` path.

### [Airflow adds Azure AI Foundry hosted-agent operators](https://github.com/apache/airflow/pull/68799) — Alejandro Morgante / Apache Airflow
An Apache Airflow provider PR discloses that Codex GPT-5 and Claude Code Fable 5 co-authored the implementation of Azure AI Foundry hosted-agent operators. The branch adds create, update, run, and delete operators for immutable hosted-agent versions, including deferrable polling until a version becomes active, OpenAI-compatible `responses` invocation, an SDK-pipeline fallback for the not-yet-exposed `invocations` protocol, and connection UI extras for endpoint, project, and auth configuration. It is a large provider integration — 15 files, about 3,000 additions, dependency and checksum updates, docs, examples, and tests for sync and async hook behavior, operator waits, triggers, and serialization paths.

### [Myotis adds Android idle sleep with wake-on-RPC](https://github.com/biafra23/myotis/pull/161) — biafra23 / Myotis
Myotis's Claude Fable 5 co-authored PR makes the Android Ethereum light-client stack battery-friendlier by entering a pseudo-sleep state after a configurable idle window. In that state discv4/discv5, RLPx, libp2p, beacon finality polling, snap maintenance, and head warming stop so the radio can sleep, while JSON-RPC keeps listening and the verified light-client store, sync roots, peer caches, and DNS pool stay warm in memory. A first verified read triggers a single-flight resume with a bounded 90-second hold; follow-up commits add RPC access logging, sleep metrics, daily catch-up race fixes, and wake-gate robustness after Copilot/Gemini review.

### [A motif-mining skill gets a real-data dropout benchmark](https://github.com/ssharpe42/template/pull/2) — ssharpe42
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored PR builds a full sequence-motif-discovery skill for labeled event streams, with a six-phase operator playbook, a portable pattern DSL, a class-aware PrefixSpan miner, a verifier using Fisher tests plus BH-FDR correction, leakage scanning, and synthetic smoke data. The commit trail then adds time windows, itemset events, random-view censoring, parallel mining and verification, and deterministic rule-set selection. Its real-data check on the KDD Cup 2015 MOOC dropout task reports five readable rules reaching 0.817 test AUC versus 0.851 for a count-feature logistic-regression baseline, after cleanup removed course-end and recency leakage.

### [Firefox’s hidden H.264 padding bug gets a client workaround](https://github.com/selkies-project/selkies/pull/272) — rmounce / Selkies
A Selkies draft PR captures a Fable-accelerated debugging run for a Firefox/WebCodecs video artifact: full-frame H.264 streams encoded as 1920×1088 were being drawn into a nominal 1920×1080 canvas with the macroblock padding included, producing a green bar on VA-API/NVENC streams and a subtle vertical resample even when x264 hid the bar by edge-replicating padding. The author reports reproducing the issue on Intel/VA-API and NVIDIA/NVENC, trying source-rect draws, `visibleRect`, synthetic-frame probes, and coded-size gates before landing on a Firefox-only overscan workaround that moves the padded rows off-canvas. In the review thread, rmounce says promotional Claude Fable 5 access accelerated the testing and proposed fixes, while a maintainer says the workaround is the least computationally expensive one found so far.

### [Mux stops interrupted sub-agent workspaces from resurfacing](https://github.com/coder/mux/pull/3694) — ThomasK33 / Coder Mux
Coder Mux followed its Fable-generated spend-accounting fix with another `anthropic:claude-fable-5` PR aimed at “garbage” sub-agent workspaces lingering in the sidebar. The root causes were a lost-update race in `config.json` writes that could resurrect just-removed child workspaces, plus interrupted workflow-owned children that were never eligible for cleanup after a run ended. The patch serializes config mutations through queued transforms, archives interrupted workflow descendants deepest-first with lossy-untracked-file guards, runs a startup self-healing sweep, and reports verification from a pre-fix failing resurrection test, a 1,200-workspace / 17-probe production-scale race with zero resurrections, static checks, and 469 targeted tests passing.

### [Containerd removes a prebuilt image from a whiteout integration test](https://github.com/containerd/containerd/pull/13735) — AkihiroSuda / containerd
A containerd PR assisted by Claude Fable 5 makes `TestIssue13030` build its whiteout fixture image locally instead of pulling `ghcr.io/containerd/whiteout-test`. The test now generates layers with `archive.WriteDiff`, serves them from a localhost HTTP registry through a new `testutil.ServeImage` helper, and exercises the normal `client.Pull` plus unpack path. That removes the need to publish and track a separate multi-architecture test image, deleting the Dockerfile, Makefile, and image-list plumbing added for the prebuilt fixture.

### [Our World in Data teaches chart axes about daily, weekly, and quarterly calendars](https://github.com/owid/owid-grapher/pull/6721) — sophiamersmann / Our World in Data
Three Fable 5 co-authored PRs in the OWID Grapher stack extend calendar-aware tick generation beyond monthly charts: [daily](https://github.com/owid/owid-grapher/pull/6721), [weekly](https://github.com/owid/owid-grapher/pull/6722), and [quarterly](https://github.com/owid/owid-grapher/pull/6723) axes now choose interval-specific tick positions and labels instead of leaning on generic day-number spacing. Continuous weekly axes step on Mondays, quarterly axes stay on quarter starts, and band axes thin to the finest evenly spaced label set that fits. Each stack member includes staging-preview and screenshot-bot links, making the report useful for seeing Fable applied to visual edge cases rather than only backend code.

### [Mergify adds an all-scopes barrier for queue-wide CI changes](https://github.com/Mergifyio/gha-mergify-ci/pull/279) — jd / Mergify
A Mergify GitHub Action PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 adds an `all_scopes` input for pull requests that should block parallel merge-queue batching across every scope. The feature is aimed at build-system or workflow changes where enumerating affected scopes is brittle: `action: scopes` and `action: scopes-upload` now send `--all` through `mergify ci scopes-send`, allow an empty concrete scope list, and document the barrier semantics in the README. The PR ties the change to a specific customer request, `MRGFY-7885`, and includes a CI exercise against the latest Mergify CLI because the pinned setup action predated the flag.

### [A Pico 2 groovebox session finds the real blank-screen bug after several wrong turns](https://github.com/Leeman1982/gm_sam/pull/4) — Leeman1982 / gm_sam
A long Fable 5 co-authored `gm_sam` branch replaces an earlier sequencer with a 16-track SAM2695 groovebox for Raspberry Pi Pico 2, including polymeter patterns, per-step probability and ratchets, a serial-MIDI driver for reverb, chorus, NRPNs, EQ, rhythm overrides, and a dual-core UI/engine split. The commit trail is especially useful as a hardware-debug report: Fable tried and then corrected display-driver hypotheses before tracing the blank screen to core1 racing core0 through display and flash initialization, then moved multicore launch to the end of setup to match the user’s working sketch. Later commits add on-screen input diagnostics and document confirmed RP2350 display timing, showing an iterative lab-style debugging process rather than a clean one-shot generation.

### [Memeget copies actual video files instead of clipboard still frames](https://github.com/ayooooo123/Memeget/pull/27) — ayooooo123 / Memeget
Memeget’s Fable 5 co-authored mobile PR fixes a concrete native-platform mismatch: Expo Clipboard could only copy text and images, so a video copy action degraded to a still frame. The new Android native module stages the file in a cache directory behind a `FileProvider`, puts a `content://` URI with the video MIME type on `ClipData`, and falls back to the old frame-copy behavior when the native module is unavailable or fails. A temporary workflow-trigger commit in the same PR records the practical build constraint: GitHub integration could not dispatch the APK workflow, so the branch was wired into the push trigger to get device-test artifacts.

### [A skill-indexing tool fixes nine agent-readiness defects](https://github.com/AlecBhamani1/skillmap/pull/5) — AlecBhamani1
AlecBhamani1 says Claude Fable 5 made the fixes and Sonnet 5 agents orchestrated them across Skillmap, a tool for discovering and ranking agent skills. The merged PR repairs nine concrete defects: plural stemming now handles “files” and “failures,” `--project-only` no longer falls back to global roots outside a project, project-only filtering happens before truncation, project skills shadow global ones, EOF frontmatter parses correctly, trigger extraction works, the `uv tool run --from graphifyy` fallback matches the actual CLI, shebangs are tokenized, and the generated documentation was refreshed after the code changed. The body includes a live verification note that a “worktrees” scope query now matches the “worktree” concept.

### [Supabase Realtime for C# stops leaking broadcast handlers](https://github.com/lan-party-tech/realtime-csharp/pull/1) — seanboehnke / LAN Party Tech
LAN Party Tech’s C# Realtime patch, co-authored by Claude Fable 5, fixes two operational problems found during a RoomNet migration from FishNet to Supabase Realtime. Broadcasts sent with acknowledgements disabled no longer allocate tracked `Push` objects that can never receive replies, and `presence_state` is treated as the authoritative roster so members who left during a websocket outage do not linger forever. Follow-up commits make current Supabase servers send the initial roster by setting `presence.enabled`, send `phx_leave` with the join reference Phoenix expects, and report a two-client live Supabase harness where late joiners receive the roster and unsubscribe presence clears immediately; the companion [supabase-csharp bump](https://github.com/lan-party-tech/supabase-csharp/pull/1) carries those fixes downstream.

### [Transit-distance lookups stop scanning D1 on every request](https://github.com/FreiFahren/FreiFahren/pull/807) — johan-t / FreiFahren
FreiFahren’s Fable 5 co-authored PR targets a hot endpoint rather than a visible UI change: `GET /v0/transit/distance` was the only transit path still scanning the stations, line-stations, and lines tables in Cloudflare D1 for every request. The patch caches the raw graph inputs under a city-scoped key with the same reseed purge tag the service already uses, then rebuilds the in-memory graph per request because JavaScript `Map`s do not survive JSON serialization. A review follow-up avoids fabricating topology with manual database inserts and instead proves the cache path by seeding the Cache API with stations that do not exist in D1, so a 200 before purge and 404 after purge demonstrate the intended boundary.

### [Bayescycle restores 133 lost visualization-package tests](https://github.com/StefanSko/bayescycle/pull/54) — StefanSko / bayescycle
A bayescycle PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 restores test coverage that was dropped during a monorepo migration. The branch ports 133 archived tests into `bayesite-idata` and `bayesite-viz`, including protocol, dense-data, CLI, golden-artifact, architecture, IO, and output suites, adds a test-only path dependency on the sibling `bayeswire` corpus, and changes the CI job from a placeholder to real `ty check` plus `pytest` coverage. The author reports `bayesite-idata` at 56 passed, `bayesite-viz` at 77 passed, format/lint/type checks green in both packages, and 13 root guard tests passing, with a new root README documenting the package trust surfaces and CLI path.

### [A legal-work benchmark gives Fable the lead but only 14.2%](https://artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/harvey-lab-aa) — Artificial Analysis
Artificial Analysis launched Harvey LAB-AA, a benchmark of 120 real legal tasks graded by strict all-pass rubrics, and reports Claude Fable 5 with adaptive reasoning, max effort, and Opus 4.8 fallback as the top-scoring configuration. The headline number is still small: Fable reaches 14.2% all-pass, ahead of Claude Opus 4.8 and GLM-5.2 at 7.5% each, while many tested models score zero under the same rule. The benchmark is useful because it separates partial rubric success from fully acceptable work products, showing both a Fable lead and how far the category remains from dependable legal automation.

### [DoorDash routes code review through Kimi first and Fable for depth](https://careersatdoordash.com/blog/how-we-learned-to-trust-our-ai-code-reviewer-at-doordash/) — DoorDash Engineering
DoorDash’s DashBench write-up describes a production-style code-review benchmark built from 105 historical pull requests with known ground-truth issues. Their best tested setup used Kimi K2.6 as the scout and Claude Fable 5 as the deeper reviewer, reaching 65.2% weighted recall and 75.3% weighted F1, compared with the existing Sonnet 4.6 plus Opus 4.8 production reviewer at 53.6% weighted recall and $3.91 per PR. The result is a concrete Fable deployment pattern rather than a single-model leaderboard claim: a cheaper first pass decides what deserves the more expensive Fable review step.

### [Plumber wires Fable into a read-only PR review workflow](https://github.com/getplumber/plumber/pull/321) — Thomas Boni / Plumber
Plumber’s Fable 5 co-authored CI PR adds advisory-only Claude reviews for security, bug risk, test coverage, and false-positive/false-negative analysis of Plumber controls. The workflow treats PR content as untrusted input: review agents get only read tools plus `git diff/log/show`, hold no GitHub write permission, return JSON artifacts, and leave all posting to a separate `github-script` job with `pull-requests: write`. A second commit changes the output from one sticky comment into deduplicated, resolvable review threads keyed by finding fingerprint, while the live Fable review on the PR caught concrete follow-ups such as immutable artifact names on reruns and the need to test the comment-merging script.

### [A microdrop heater stops claiming the wrong Pico board](https://github.com/Blue-Ocean-Technologies-Inc/heater-microdrop-plugin-py/pull/7) — Vigne-hub / Blue Ocean Technologies
A Fable 5 co-authored heater-controller PR fixes a hardware-identification bug caused by two Raspberry Pi Pico boards sharing the same USB VID:PID. The old monitor could attach to the fluorescence LED board by mistake; the new path probes each candidate serial port with Microdrop’s `whoami` helper and only claims devices whose `device_id` contains `heater`, while keeping a warned fallback for older firmware that cannot identify itself. It is a two-file patch, but the failure mode is concrete lab automation plumbing rather than a generic refactor.

### [Rocq receives a batch of small Ltac2 standard-library utilities](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22233) — Jason Gross / Rocq
Jason Gross opened a series of small Rocq PRs described as written by Claude Fable 5 via Claude Code and extracted from a larger Ltac2 utility library: [index-returning list search](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22233), [take/drop while](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22234), [shared prefixes](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22235), [relaxed firstn/skipn](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22236), [option-traverse](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22237), [string search](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22238), [split/trim](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22239), [prefix and character replacement](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22240), [basic message printers](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22241), and [integer min/max](https://github.com/rocq-prover/rocq/pull/22242). Each PR is small — typically three or four files with a changelog note, implementation, and focused Ltac2 tests — but the cluster shows Fable being used for maintainable proof-assistant standard-library plumbing rather than a demo app.

### [RocksDB async commits stop starving the libuv threadpool](https://github.com/HarperFast/rocksdb-js/pull/694) — kriszyp / HarperFast
A HarperFast rocksdb-js PR generated by Claude Fable 5 and reviewed by Kris replaces per-commit libuv async work with a dedicated per-database commit thread. The write-up ties the change to real degraded-mode incidents where slow commits pinned the default four libuv slots, then reports A/B results: during a heavy write burst, fs.stat p50 fell from 15.1 ms to 0.12 ms, p99 from 223.7 ms to 2.6 ms, and commit throughput stayed slightly higher. A second strained 46 GB database run still favored the single-lane default, while a cross-model review drove lifecycle fixes for env teardown, transaction-handle cleanup, and destroy/close ordering.

### [A ClojureScript calculator fixes borrowed-REPL panics and frozen outputs](https://github.com/csm/infinite-calculators/pull/5) — csm / infinite-calculators
The infinite-calculators PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 documents three bugs reproduced from real use: DOM event handlers could re-enter the main Repl while an eval still held a RefCell borrow, a panic could leave Replicant stuck in rendering mode forever, and Clojure ratios such as 9/50 produced invalid JSON compute replies. The fix serializes all Repl entry through one promise chain, replaces idle polling with event-driven effect draining, promotes non-integer numbers through doubles, and preserves raw typed input so mid-edit renders no longer rewrite fields. The author says the changes are covered by new Playwright probes plus the existing end-to-end suite.

### [Mux queues user messages behind internal workspace turns instead of erroring](https://github.com/coder/mux/pull/3696) — ammar-agent / Coder Mux
Coder Mux has another `anthropic:claude-fable-5` PR on a hot-path agent UX failure: sending a normal message while an internal workspace-turn follow-up or skill invocation was queued produced a hard "Cannot queue additional messages" error. The branch replaces the single merged message batch with FIFO queue entries, keeps plain follow-ups mergeable, seals special sends so metadata and callbacks stay attached to the right dispatch, and adds targeted cancellation for one queued workspace turn without dropping unrelated user messages. Validation reports a new regression test, 4,133 Bun service tests passing, and `make static-check` green after Codex review requested the targeted-cancel fix.

### [Bag of Words teaches codegen to recover from its own sandbox violations](https://github.com/bagofwords1/bagofwords/pull/577) — yochze / Bag of Words
Bag of Words has two Fable 5 co-authored fixes aimed at feedback loops in a data-analysis app. The sandbox PR turns safe pre-execution AST violations into retryable feedback, injects the failed code and validator-derived rules into the next generation prompt, and verifies the change end-to-end on a messy-header XLSX that previously got stuck on forbidden `getattr()` calls. A neighboring [usage-tab PR](https://github.com/bagofwords1/bagofwords/pull/576) fixes counters that stayed at zero for uncapped users, forces fresh quota reads when the profile tab opens, and records live-stack validation with scripted LLM calls, real SQL, and before/after Playwright screenshots.

### [OpenNMS adds mutual TLS across three collector paths](https://github.com/OpenNMS/opennms/pull/8622) — marshallmassengill / OpenNMS
OpenNMS has a Fable 5-assisted PR for mutual TLS support in its XML, Prometheus, and HTTP collectors, motivated by Incus/LXC and Prometheus endpoints that require client certificates. The branch adds keystore and truststore service parameters, secure-credentials-vault interpolation, validated hostname handling, and shared SSL-context helpers across the collector stack. The commit trail is especially useful because an adversarial review tightened redirect behavior so a client certificate cannot follow redirects to a different scheme or port, and the tests use JDK HTTPS servers with required client auth, PKCS12 and JKS stores, hostname-mismatch cases, and duplicate-client coverage.

### [AXI5-Lite rules turned into Lean proofs and a synthesizable subordinate](https://github.com/tibrewalrachit/sparkle/pull/2) — tibrewalrachit / Sparkle
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored Sparkle PR extracts rules from ARM IHI 0022 Issue L and formalizes an AXI5-Lite interface in Lean. The first layer adds 36 theorems with zero `sorry`s for valid-ready handshakes, write and read dependencies, response encoding, Lite-only response restrictions, aligned-transfer bounds, trace-level safety, and bounded cooperative liveness. Later commits connect the spec FSMs to RTL gate forms and Signal DSL loop bodies, synthesize a SystemVerilog subordinate, and add a Veil/SMT cross-verification subproject with negative tests for broken BVALID and gate mutations.

### [A chess engine stops laundering repetition draws through its transposition table](https://github.com/SupplyChef/Chess/pull/139) — SupplyChef
SupplyChef’s Fable 5 co-authored chess-engine fix starts from a reproduced blunder: the engine saw a shuffle endgame as +0cp by repetition, played a quiet move, and dropped to -1063cp one move later. The root cause was path-dependent repetition scoring leaking into exact transposition-table entries when a tainted child participated in the max but was not the chosen best move; a fabricated-history reproduction made a queen-winning `Qxd5` position store as exact 0 at depth 17. The patch tracks whether any child carried a repetition-tainted score, demotes or skips unsafe TT stores, changes the root tie-break toward certain repetition draws, and stamps TT entries by search generation so stale history-dependent entries become replaceable.

### [A digital-logic simulator fixes a save-data-loss chain and hardens loaders](https://github.com/anadon/JLS/pull/88) — anadon / JLS
A Fable 5 co-authored JLS branch starts with three concrete data-loss bugs: opening a circuit forgot its directory so Save targeted the filesystem root, failed writes cleared the dirty flag before the write succeeded, and a queued checkpoint could resurrect stale pre-save content after a save deleted `file.jls~`. The same PR grew into a larger simulator-hardening pass across 57 files, covering saved-value parsing, string unescaping, constructor selection, headless CLI failures, untrusted archive size caps, simulator threading, CodeQL, JaCoCo, SBOM generation, and repository health files. The commit history records regression tests for dirty-flag ordering, checkpoint interleavings, bit-set round trips, string escapes, parameter validation, untrusted-file parsing, and version identity rather than just a broad rewrite.

### [A 22-agent UI audit produces verified defects and a Playwright rig](https://github.com/merogith/battle/pull/375) — merogith / battle
Battle used Claude Fable 5 to open a full UI, UX, and accessibility audit of every screen, driven by a 22-agent workflow. The audit covers nine visual screen clusters at up to seven viewports in real headless Chromium, a new-player journey playtest, a CSS-system census, and keyboard/screen-reader checks; every P0 and P1 finding was adversarially re-verified through real entry paths. The resulting plan lists 3 P0s, about 28 P1s, 1,597 inline styles, 859 unique hex colors, and roughly 94 button classes, while the PR also commits a reusable Playwright audit rig and machine-readable findings with measurements and verifier verdicts.

### [Airflow cancels deferred Redshift statements when tasks are killed](https://github.com/apache/airflow/pull/69631) — steveahnahn / Apache Airflow
An Apache Airflow PR generated with Claude Code Fable 5 closes a deferrable-operator gap in `RedshiftDataOperator`. When a deferred task was marked failed, cleared, or marked success, the operator's `on_kill` path no longer ran and Redshift statements could keep billing RPU-hours or occupying WLM slots until they finished on their own. The fix adds best-effort cancellation to `RedshiftDataTrigger`, threads a default-on `cancel_on_kill` flag from the operator into the trigger, and adds tests for cancellation, opt-out, missing statement IDs, swallowed cancellation failures, serialization, and defer wiring.

### [Harper warns when component env files try to shape top-level config](https://github.com/HarperFast/harper/pull/1580) — kylebernhardy / Harper
Harper's Fable 5 co-authored PR started from a real configuration trap: `HARPER_DEFAULT_CONFIG`, `HARPER_CONFIG`, and `HARPER_SET_CONFIG` delivered through a component `.env` silently no-oped because top-level config had already been composed and memoized. After review, the branch deliberately pivoted from honoring those variables to warning about them, preserving the rule that top-level config controls components rather than the reverse. The final patch adds a boot pre-pass and loadEnv warning path that name the variable, file, and supported channels, plus 10 pre-pass unit tests, a loadEnv warning test, and an integration test proving `/mcp` stays unmounted when config shaping is attempted only through `.env`.

### [Schemabot refuses multi-statement DDL classification](https://github.com/block/schemabot/pull/646) — aparajon / Block
Block's Schemabot merged a small Fable 5 co-authored safety fix for `ClassifyStatement`. The helper previously returned the first result when given compound SQL such as `ALTER TABLE t1 ...; DROP TABLE t2`, meaning a destructive statement could hide behind the benign classification of the first statement even though current live callers already split inputs. The patch makes multi-statement input an error that points callers to `SplitStatements`, adds tests for the behavior, and follows up by truncating error previews on rune boundaries so non-ASCII identifiers cannot produce invalid UTF-8.

### [Observ replaces noisy benchmark gating with interleaved A/B runs](https://github.com/fork-tongue/observ/pull/181) — Korijn / observ
Observ had a benchmark CI guard that compared one master run against one PR run and failed on mean slowdowns over 5%, but the Fable co-authored diagnosis found that identical code already drifted past that line in 25 of 56 benchmarks, with swings up to 40%. The fix disables GC during timed rounds, pins `PYTHONHASHSEED`, runs master and PR benchmarks interleaved as A/B/A/B/A/B, and only fails when the fastest PR median is more than 10% slower than the slowest master median. Local validation showed master-vs-master worst-case “consistent slowdown” at 0.9%, while an injected 50 ns `proxy()` overhead still failed exactly the four affected read benchmarks.

### [One user plugin stops paging Engram on-call](https://github.com/engram-app/Engram/pull/965) — Rasbandit / Engram
Engram traced a production alert-noise incident to client plugin logs being re-emitted by the backend at `:error`, so a single broken CRDT channel-join retry loop could trip the `>20-error/5m` Loki alert and page on-call. The Fable co-authored fix caps re-emitted client severity at `:warning` while preserving the original value as `client_severity` metadata, and the same investigation confirmed a suspected checkpoint `NoResultsError` storm was already fixed on main. The PR reports a new regression test, 3,524 Elixir tests with zero failures, and clean format, Credo, and Sobelow checks.

### [Neuralscape makes its benchmark harness drive the real systems](https://github.com/ehfazrezwan/neuralscape/pull/187) — ehfazrezwan / neuralscape
A Neuralscape Phase III PR co-authored by Fable fixes benchmark wiring discovered while running the live ICEBench matrix, rather than just editing benchmark scripts in the abstract. It makes `ns_ice` run native index and snapshot CLIs on the host under `/usr/bin/time -v`, fixes REST parameter names and competitor adapter class names, switches `ns_graphify` to the verified graphify surface, and adds `blast_radius` query generation where Track-Q previously logged “Unknown operation.” The live validation note says `ns_ice` indexed Click end-to-end with 1,867 symbols and 7,278 edges in 163 seconds at 403 MB RSS, with all five REST operations returning, before the small-py four-system matrix was run on a quiesced box.

### [Mathlib gets Fable-assisted Hellinger affinity proofs](https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/pull/41517) — sadasant / mathlib4
Daniel Rodriguez opened a mathlib PR defining Hellinger affinity for two measures as an `ENNReal` lintegral against `μ + ν`, with symmetry, dominating-measure invariance, probability-measure bounds, computation rules, and a zero-affinity iff mutual singularity theorem. The disclosure says the definitions, proofs, and PR description were developed in a public repository with Claude Fable 5 via Claude Code from a human-reviewed design and statement plan, as groundwork for a formalization of Kakutani’s dichotomy for infinite products of probability measures. The author reports Lean checked the proofs and that `#print axioms` on the main results shows only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.

### [An Ethereum Safe reproduction is proven on a fork before bootstrap](https://github.com/S01-Issuer/st0x.deploy/pull/232) — hardyjosh / S01 Issuer
A Fable co-authored st0x.deploy stack moves an Ethereum bootstrap from runbook prose into typed scripts and fork-proven invariants. The related [chain-principals refactor](https://github.com/S01-Issuer/st0x.deploy/pull/228), [V4 authoriser bootstrap script](https://github.com/S01-Issuer/st0x.deploy/pull/229), and [cross-chain parity CI](https://github.com/S01-Issuer/st0x.deploy/pull/230) make Base and Ethereum deployment data chain-parametric, gate missing pins with typed preflights, and keep parity checks scheduled. The headline PR pins the Base Safe’s v1.3.0 genesis creation parameters and runs the real Ethereum v1.3.0 factory on a fork, proving the produced proxy is byte-for-byte the same token-owner Safe address before a separate post-deploy policy replay upgrades it to current ownership.

### [vLLM brings NVFP4 KV cache to Ampere and Hopper GPUs](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/46963) — lesj0610 / vLLM
A vLLM PR says Codex and Claude Fable 5 were used during implementation and PR preparation for a FlashInfer NVFP4 paged-KV path on pre-SM100 GPUs. The branch lets Ampere and Hopper systems use NVFP4 KV cache instead of the Blackwell-only path, adds a BF16 FA2 prefill fallback, mixed-head layout checks, and cached FlashInfer KV views. The reported RTX 3090 benchmarks show about 3x KV-cache capacity — for example Qwen3.6-27B from 291,999 to 884,736 tokens — with 23 NVFP4 unit tests, static checks, and a `/v1/completions` runtime smoke returning 200 OK.

### [llama.cpp adds Hy3 with measured MTP speculation](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/25395) — satindergrewal / llama.cpp
A llama.cpp contributor disclosed that Anthropic Claude Fable 5 wrote most of a Hy3 support PR under their direction, with the human reviewer testing builds on real hardware and taking responsibility for the contribution. The branch registers Tencent's 299B MoE Hy3 architecture, exports the appended MTP block from the converter, and wires the next-token-prediction head into the existing `--spec-type draft-mtp` path. On an RTX 5090, the PR reports 5.81–6.31 tok/s without speculation versus 7.97 tok/s with `n_max=3` and `p_min=0.75`, while candidly noting that the default `p_min=0` is slower and that a 598GB-weight perplexity comparison was not run.

### [CommonTools separates membership labels from member contents](https://github.com/commontoolsinc/labs/pull/4391) — mathpirate / CommonTools
CommonTools has a two-PR Fable 5 report in its CFC flow-label runner. The first PR says Claude Fable 5 reconciled the analysis for a `filter`/`flatMap` bug where result containers inherited every input member's content confidentiality instead of only the membership information that the predicate actually observed. A follow-up [array-shrink fix](https://github.com/commontoolsinc/labs/pull/4556), credited to Fable for analysis, fix, and tests, clears stale per-slot link labels so a regrown list cannot re-import a departed member's label; its verification includes a red-first test and a 136-file, 1,044-step runner blast-radius suite with zero failures.

### [ORC hardens malformed-file validation across C++ and Java readers](https://github.com/apache/orc/pull/2679) — dongjoon-hyun / Apache ORC
Apache ORC has a compact Fable-generated hardening batch aimed at malformed ORC inputs rather than feature work. The C++ reader [now validates type trees](https://github.com/apache/orc/pull/2679) when creating a reader from a serialized file tail, preventing out-of-bounds reads or unbounded recursion; the Java side adds checks for [negative decoded dictionary lengths](https://github.com/apache/orc/pull/2680), [stream offset+length overflow](https://github.com/apache/orc/pull/2681), [invalid union tags](https://github.com/apache/orc/pull/2682), and [bad stripe offset/index/data/footer lengths](https://github.com/apache/orc/pull/2683). Each PR carries a `Generated-by: Claude Fable 5` disclosure and adds a focused regression test or CI-backed test case.

### [A threat dashboard closes audit gaps against Postgres and AI-tool surfaces](https://github.com/ChristianPresley/threat-dashboard/pull/5) — ChristianPresley
A co-authored Fable 5 PR to a threat-dashboard app describes a four-agent audit across panels, the data layer, command handling, and the AI subsystem, followed by implementation of every confirmed gap. The patch adds store mutations for feed, case, false-positive, and retention operations; fixes Postgres parity failures such as enrichment lookups no-oping under binary search; persists audit logs through restarts; and expands the dashboard, keyboard, help, MCP, and AI-tool surfaces. Its validation section reports deterministic mock seeding, browser command journeys, and a Postgres end-to-end run where `pgload` and a tour produced enrichment mutations, CI stamps, and 32 audit entries that reloaded after restart.

## 2026-07-09

### [Iceberg Python learns V3 tolerance and explicit manifest rewrites](https://github.com/apache/iceberg-python/pull/3630) — moomindani / Apache Iceberg
Two Claude Fable 5-written Iceberg Python PRs continue the table-format v3 work with reader tolerance and maintenance APIs. The first makes PyIceberg load tables whose partition or sort fields use multi-argument `source-ids`, treating them as unknown transforms instead of failing, while also preserving unknown transform names on rewrite. The companion [rewrite_manifests API](https://github.com/apache/iceberg-python/pull/3631) merges small data manifests into target-sized groups, preserves sequence numbers, commits a replace snapshot only when useful, and adds Spark interop coverage for the data, history, file counts, and manifest counts.

### [Syncthing trims hot-path allocation and lookup overhead](https://github.com/gaby/syncthing/pull/48) — gaby / Syncthing
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored Syncthing PR targets small costs on paths hit for every socket read, block, file, event, and case-insensitive filesystem lookup. The changes cache Prometheus counters per connection or puller state, avoid repeated `FileInfo` copies, make `Vector.String` byte-identical without reflection, reuse file-batch backing arrays, replace formatted block keys with structs, and fix a defeated duplicate-suppression path in case folding. Follow-up review commits adjusted event metric laziness, block-reuse semantics, comments, and test puller metrics rather than leaving the first optimization pass unchallenged.

### [Memeget rebuilds on-device meme tagging around few-shot teaching](https://github.com/ayooooo123/Memeget/pull/28) — ayooooo123 / Memeget
Memeget’s Fable 5 co-authored Android branch switches the default “max” vision tier to Gemma 4 E2B via react-native-executorch, then reworks teaching so one positive example can add CLIP-similar lookalikes and re-tag the library in one pass. Later commits speed indexing by querying known files once, overlapping copy/thumbnail/transcode work with model inference, and running CLIP embedding and OCR concurrently. The few-shot learner also moves away from random negatives, excluding near-positive background vectors and using hard negatives from other labels to avoid teaching a common class to reject itself.

### [PostHog shrinks an events-json migration’s raw-SQL surface](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/69539) — andyzzhao / PostHog
Two human-directed Claude Fable 5 PRs at PostHog reduce migration risk around session and survey maintenance code. The survey PR moves `stop_surveys_reached_target` and adaptive sampling from hand-built ClickHouse SQL to HogQL, fixing a real gap where the adaptive-sampling count had no `team_id` filter and could scan every team’s events for a survey id. A related [sessions cleanup](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/69477) deletes a 2024 backfill command after repo searches, US/EU ops logs, and query-log archive checks found no remaining executions, including a negative validation over 18 million log lines.

### [Switchboard makes triage a dispatch precondition](https://github.com/colin-prologue/Switchboard/pull/53) — switchboard-agent / colin-prologue
Switchboard’s Fable co-authored workflow PR turns status transitions into a committed YAML table and makes `gate:triage-passed` a marker the orchestrator must see before claiming a `todo`. The dispatch path now refuses unstamped work without writing labels, while triage PASS and NEEDS-WORK update the marker alongside status labels; tests grep for duplicated transition literals and cover poll and retry refusal paths. The branch reports 174 orchestrator tests before merge reconciliation and 255 tests after integrating the claim-visibility work, then a Codex review found and Fable fixed a direct `--entry todo` path that would otherwise have created unstamped tasks.

### [Memory Lane stops treating branch-like tokens as secrets](https://github.com/ribbons-digital/memory-lane/pull/163) — ribbons-digital / Memory Lane
Memory Lane used Fable 5 for diagnosis and implementation review on a secret-detector bug where high-entropy branch names and feature-flag-like tokens could be rejected as secrets. The fix requires generic entropy hits to appear in secret-bearing context while preserving explicit prefixes, private keys, and assignment-style secret detection, and adds metadata-only `skippedSecret` debug counts without logging raw candidate text. The PR reproduces the issue with `release/JIRA-2024-blueGreenRollout-phase3`, runs focused core, Claude, Codex, CLI, build, and full test gates, and documents a failed external runner fallback instead of hiding it.

### [A seven-specialist diagnostic board gains datasets and evaluation](https://github.com/Wachirawut2023/AegisMed/pull/1) — Wachirawut2023 / AegisMed
AegisMed’s Fable 5 co-authored PR builds a hackathon medical-diagnosis app around parallel specialist agents and a synthesis agent that ranks differential diagnoses and flags rare diseases. The branch includes demo mode, a single-page UI, Docker packaging, a dataset builder for rare-disease cases, an evaluation harness with known-diagnosis cases, evidence retrieval with citation indexing, smart specialist routing to reduce model calls, and a fine-tuning pipeline for the board-chair model. The commit history shows the project moving from a basic multi-agent demo into an evaluable system with intake questions and curated demo cases rather than just a prompt wrapper.

### [Geist consolidates its agent core and fixes latent route bugs](https://github.com/DavidDworetzky/Geist/pull/277) — DavidDworetzky / Geist
A Fable 5 co-authored Geist PR removes duplicated agent implementations by moving the OODA tick loop, adapter dispatch, chat-history hydration, generation-parameter resolution, and subprocess lifecycle into `BaseAgent`. The same branch fixes a cluster of latent runtime bugs: explicit generation parameters were being overwritten by defaults, `temperature=0` was treated as unset, a chat-history endpoint recursed into itself and returned an unawaited coroutine, mutable defaults leaked state across `AgentContext` instances, newline splitting used a literal backslash-n, phase-in/out called a nonexistent lookup, and enum handling made `/agent/complete_text_new` fail. Legacy GPT4/Llama tests were ported to route-level OnlineAgent and LocalAgent tests with a stub runner, turning the refactor into a regression sweep rather than a pure cleanup.

### [A legacy PHP timeclock gets prepared statements and PHP 8 repairs](https://github.com/BoatrightTBC/timeclock/pull/5) — rjonesbsink / PHP Timeclock
A Fable 5 co-authored modernization branch for PHP Timeclock and Punchclock converts roughly 260 SQL queries across about 40 files to prepared-statement helpers, replacing scattered `mysqli_connect` blocks with a shared strict-mode `lib/db.php`. It also moves password storage from DES `crypt($pw, 'xy')` to `password_hash()` with transparent rehash on legacy login, stops tracking a credential-bearing config file, and documents that an old committed password should be treated as compromised if it was ever real. The PHP 8.3 pass removes fatal `get_magic_quotes_gpc()` and `ereg_replace()` calls, fixes a PHP4-style constructor that left `empfullname` null, and catches a mid-PR include-order regression that caused `Cannot redeclare _tc_bind_param()` on some request paths.

### [Windows workflow launches recover from hidden carriage returns](https://github.com/cfg-is/cfgms/pull/2456) — jrdnr / cfgms
A small Fable 5 co-authored cfgms PR documents a concrete Windows-only failure: Claude named-workflow invocations rejected checked-out `.claude/workflows/*.js` files because `core.autocrlf=true` had smudged them to CRLF, producing `\r` bytes that the Workflow permission layer treated as hidden control characters. The fix adds `*.js text eol=lf` and `*.py text eol=lf` to `.gitattributes`, with no index renormalization because the stored files were already LF. The author validated the change on the Windows self-dispatch host: the files moved from `w/crlf` to `w/lf`, `story-review.js` contained zero carriage returns, the same `Workflow({scriptPath: ...})` launch succeeded, and `make test` passed.

### [An iOS finance-app audit turns into calculators, CI, and import fixes](https://github.com/ragunawan/EggSpend/pull/1) — ragunawan / EggSpend
EggSpend’s first Fable 5 co-authored PR starts as a full engineering and product audit, then commits the working loop needed to act on it: six subagent role files, a loop log, a GitHub Actions test workflow, and a prioritized backlog. Subsequent commits implement several audit-derived fixes in the Swift app, including a unified `NetWorthCalculator`, a liability-sign correction, account-linked transaction filtering for net-worth reconstruction, and CSV amount-sign/type-inference repairs. The changed file list spans models, persistence, import, calculators, budget alerts, dashboard and metrics views, so the public trail shows Fable moving from repo analysis into implementation rather than stopping at a roadmap.

### [Prompt Fidelity ships as an installable Claude skill](https://github.com/Barneyjm/prompt-fidelity/pull/5) — Barneyjm
A Fable 5 co-authored PR packages Prompt Fidelity as both a Claude skill and a Claude Code plugin-marketplace entry. The skill teaches the model to split a tool-backed answer into verified and inferred constraints, estimate survival rates, compute a fidelity score with a bundled stdlib-only Python script, and call out which parts of an answer are auditable versus judgment calls. A follow-up commit fixes a singular/plural constraint-count bug in the generated report, which is a useful reminder that even meta-evaluation tooling needs its own output checks.

### [Rhythm-game recommendations find near-miss goals](https://github.com/signoiidx/bokuraway/pull/15) — signoiidx / bokuraway
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored bokuraway PR adds an “あと一歩” recommendations tab for IIDX charts where a goal is close enough to be worth practicing. The pure `src/nudge.ts` module scores next-lamp targets from BP rates, full-combo opportunities from low BP counts, and A/AA/AAA grade boundaries within 1.0%, then returns separate `nudges`, `toHard`, and `toClear` lists for the renderer. Three Claude-authored commits refine the feature from general near-miss goals to HARD/EASY clear focus and then grade goals, with unit tests in `tests/nudge.test.mjs` plus an end-to-end test path rather than only UI wiring.

### [A phone task app grows from scaffold to signed APK workflow](https://github.com/FunkeePanda/Claude_taskapp/pull/1) — FunkeePanda
FunkeePanda’s Fable 5 co-authored PR starts with a Capacitor 7 skeleton for a phone-first focus app and then continues through a long sequence of Claude commits adding CRUD tasks, natural-language quick add, focus scoring, nag reminders, custom intervals, a calendar, nested subtasks, category colors, backup and docs. The branch commits both the Android platform and a vanilla-JS web app that can run on device, GitHub Pages, or headless CI, with workflows for a signed rolling APK release and Pages preview. The useful public trail is the breadth of follow-up commits after the initial scaffold, including fixes to reminder plugin registration and install-over-existing builds that preserve data.

### [LeaveDesk SG combines a demo app with a Supabase approval backend](https://github.com/fluffyland/the-unspoken-gift/pull/1) — fluffyland
A Fable 5 co-authored LeaveDesk SG PR builds a leave-management demo and a more production-shaped Supabase foundation in one branch. The single-page app models employee, manager, and HR roles; balances, one- and two-level approvals, return-and-resubmit flows, Singapore holiday calculations, simulated email, all-staff calendars, team-conflict acknowledgements, HR employee management, and mobile layout. The PR also adds seven Supabase tables, RLS policies, a balance view, status-machine procedures, a Resend notification function, seed data, and records a 13-step headless-browser smoke test plus a later fix for a UUID-vs-bigint RLS policy bug.

### [UK legal rules are batch-encoded with manifests and validation gates](https://github.com/TheAxiomFoundation/rulespec-uk/pull/129) — MaxGhenis / The Axiom Foundation
The Axiom Foundation’s rulespec-uk PR records a Fable 5 co-authored bulk encoding run for UK legal material. It updates eight cited modules in the PR body — including regulations and statutes such as UKSI 2004/692 schedule 1 and UKPGA 1994/23 sections 2 and 29A — while the changed files include signed apply manifests, YAML rule encodings, test YAML, and a regenerated coverage-pending index. The author describes it as merge-train-style consolidation with one reverse-index regeneration, one validation run, and a local gate of eight green modules and zero needs-fixtures, with the required `validate / validate` check as the authority.

### [A vault-sync deployment collapses into one OCI image](https://github.com/aliasunder/vault-cortex/pull/291) — aliasunder / vault-cortex
A vault-cortex PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 rewrites the project’s Docker packaging into local and remote build targets, replacing a two-service Docker Compose deployment with one OCI image that supervises Obsidian Sync and the MCP server under s6. The author reports arm64 and amd64 builds, local `/healthz` and MCP initialize smokes, and remote-target testing against a real Obsidian Sync account. That live verification found a broken upstream fork merge where fresh-vault setup passed a duplicated `sync-setup` token, a bug fixed in the absorbed rootfs copy rather than only documented as deployment work.

### [Cachew streams 5–10 GB snapshots without a full temp file](https://github.com/block/cachew/pull/386) — Alec Thomas / Block
Block’s cachew PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 adds `ParallelGetStream`, which fetches ranged chunks concurrently and reorders them into a sequential stream through an unlinked spill file. The goal is practical: very large git snapshots, described as 5–10 GB, can pipe directly into extraction with flat memory instead of first materializing a full-size temporary file. The patch includes Linux and macOS hole-punching so consumed spill regions are reclaimed, a cgo layout guard for Darwin structs, git-restore pipeline changes, and tests for both the streaming downloader and error-reporting behavior.

### [Tessio adds team email routing and CSAT surveys](https://github.com/tessio-ai/tessio/pull/8) — jonmoubayed / Tessio
Tessio has two Claude Fable 5 co-authored feature PRs that push a support-ticketing app beyond UI polish. The [team-email PR](https://github.com/tessio-ai/tessio/pull/8) adds per-team sender identities, inbound recipient parsing, API validation, settings UI, and IMAP routing so messages to `it@example.com` or `hr@example.com` land with the right team. A companion [CSAT PR](https://github.com/tessio-ai/tessio/pull/7) adds per-org satisfaction settings, survey emails after resolved or closed tickets, requester rating links, staff-visible responses, and reporting measures; both PRs include Cloudflare preview deployments and integration-test surfaces across API, worker, and web code.

### [Knockoff ships a Safari userscript and safer marketplace filters](https://github.com/Shpigford/knockoff/pull/64) — pseudovidecoder / Knockoff
Knockoff’s Fable-authored Safari userscript PR bundles a week of marketplace-extension hardening into one branch: a Userscripts build with a GM-storage shim, settings sheet, and smoke test, plus several smaller fixes already split as focused PRs. Those focused PRs cover [keeping the cached curated blocklist](https://github.com/Shpigford/knockoff/pull/59) when `/flagged` temporarily fails, [diacritic-aware dedupe](https://github.com/Shpigford/knockoff/pull/60) matching the detector’s normalization, [pseudo-brand seller-name warnings](https://github.com/Shpigford/knockoff/pull/61), [filtered-brand panel actions](https://github.com/Shpigford/knockoff/pull/62), and [settings export/import](https://github.com/Shpigford/knockoff/pull/63). The linked PR bodies report 93–101 Node tests, Safari resource syncs, manual export/import checks, and concrete Amazon search demos rather than only describing feature intent.

### [Mainframe fixes a context meter that treated 1M as 200k](https://github.com/qlan-ro/mainframe/pull/423) — doruchiulan / Mainframe
Mainframe’s Claude Fable 5 co-authored PR fixes a misleading context meter in a Claude-backed chat app. The diagnosis found that live `get_context_usage` data was correct, but the UI’s fallback estimate divided by a catalog guess of 200k tokens even when the CLI reported about 967k usable tokens for 1M-context Claude sessions. The patch persists the CLI-reported total and max tokens, preserves `[1m]` resolution for `claude-fable-5`, prevents subagent usage from polluting parent estimates, and adds red-to-green coverage across adapter enrichment, probe-models, event handling, session-bar state, service integration, and migrations.

### [OpenClaw exposes the full Fable effort ladder through Claude CLI](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/102355) — ryuhaneul / OpenClaw
OpenClaw’s Fable co-authored PR fixes a provider-policy mismatch where Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 users on the `claude-cli` backend could only select `off` in `/think`, even though Claude Code already accepts `--effort` for those models. The author verified `claude --model claude-fable-5 --effort low|high|xhigh|max -p ...` returning `pong`, and an invalid `banana` effort producing the expected warning with the valid values. The code change is small — four files — but it makes subscription-auth Fable sessions expose the same low, medium, high, xhigh, and max effort ladder as the native Anthropic route, backed by provider-policy and runtime tests.

### [Gitea re-derives incremental migration sync in four reviewable PRs](https://github.com/davidlovas/gitea/pull/4) — davidlovas / Gitea
David Lovas is reworking Gitea’s migrated-repository sync in a Fable 5-assisted stack split into [interface primitives](https://github.com/davidlovas/gitea/pull/1), [GitHub incremental listing](https://github.com/davidlovas/gitea/pull/2), [issue and pull-request upserts](https://github.com/davidlovas/gitea/pull/3), and the headline [SyncRepository orchestration](https://github.com/davidlovas/gitea/pull/4). The series avoids the Search API’s 1,000-result cap, keys issue and PR updates by `(repo_id, index)`, rebuilds uploader caches from an earlier migration, and derives a watermark from preserved remote timestamps without a schema change. Lovas says he reviewed every line and tested the full series end-to-end on a live self-hosted Gitea instance mirroring about 30 private GitHub repos plus the 1,393-PR public `auth0/auth0-cli` repo, with the final sync updating entities in place and avoiding the duplicate-key collision that motivated the rework.

### [A ten-subsystem HPC-agent audit turns into dozens of fixes](https://github.com/jamesdchen/hpc-copilot/pull/9) — jamesdchen / hpc-copilot
hpc-copilot used Claude Fable 5 for a ten-subsystem parallel audit that produced 40 findings across result corruption, state loss, CI, tooling, and cluster-runtime tiers, each recorded with file-and-line references and a concrete failure scenario. The same PR then commits ten Fable-backed follow-up changes across 98 files, including journal settling, dead-worker scoping, sidecar lifecycle repairs, service-env dispatch self-containment, stale-metrics quarantine, weighted reductions, transport preclean safety, scheduler accounting, and several memory and aggregate-runner fixes. The commit log is useful because it shows the audit moving from a written bug sweep into implementation slices with named failure modes, rather than stopping at a checklist.

### [Harper OAuth adds strict Ed25519 client assertions](https://github.com/HarperFast/oauth/pull/165) — heskew / Harper
Harper’s Fable 5 co-authored OAuth PR implements the RFC 7523 client-assertion primitives for headless MCP agents before wiring them into the grant flow. The verifier accepts only public OKP/Ed25519 JWKs, pins `alg: EdDSA`, rejects `crit`, malformed base64url, private-key material, ambiguous `kid`s, long or replayed `jti`s, and multi-audience tokens, and verifies the signature before inspecting claims. The replay store hashes a length-prefixed `(client_id, jti)` pair, a test caught the naive delimiter-collision version, and the PR reports 35 new tests with 841 total tests green plus follow-up fixes from Gemini and Claude review.

### [Calendrome makes time-entry date ranges agree](https://github.com/mklute101/calendrome/pull/94) — mklute101 / Calendrome
Calendrome’s Fable 5 co-authored fix starts from a subtle SQLite boundary mismatch: `get_timesheet_summary` compared `DATE(te.start_at)` to full ISO timestamps, so entries on the first selected day disappeared, while `list_pending_review` had the mirror-image last-day problem when given a plain date. The patch adds a shared day-range helper used by timesheet summary, export, pending review, week layout, and harvest push, collapsing timestamp bounds to UTC days and comparing inclusive day buckets. The branch also removes an old harvest-push workaround, updates tool schemas and skills to document the accepted formats, and adds range-parity regression tests.

### [A telemetry opt-out stops printing first-run notices](https://github.com/anandgupta42/receipts/pull/200) — anandgupta42 / receipts
Receipts used Claude Fable 5 on a privacy-footgun fix: a user who set `AIRECEIPTS_TELEMETRY=off` or `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` before first run still saw the five-line telemetry notice on stderr, making the opt-out look ignored. The fix makes the notice path consult the existing telemetry kill-switch resolver before printing or writing the “shown” flag, while still showing the full disclosure on the first run where telemetry is actually enabled. The PR says the new red-then-green tests failed before the change and passed after it, with the full gate reporting 134 files and 1,737 tests green plus TypeScript, ESLint, golden, determinism, spec-lint, hygiene, and Codex acceptance checks.

### [Spark confines Netty stream serving to registered directories](https://github.com/apache/spark/pull/57151) — dongjoon-hyun / Apache Spark
Apache Spark has a Fable 5-generated core hardening PR for `NettyStreamManager` in Spark 4.3.0. The change makes the stream manager serve only files under the registered directory, aligning it with the canonicalization behavior of `addFile` and `addJar` instead of trusting a path outside that boundary. The PR is small — two files, 74 additions, one deletion — but it adds a `NettyStreamManagerSuite` case and reports CI passing with the new test.

### [QuantEcon.jl ports DiscreteDP converters from Python](https://github.com/QuantEcon/QuantEcon.jl/pull/389) — oyamad / QuantEcon
QuantEcon.jl has a Fable 5 co-authored PR synchronizing several `DiscreteDP` features from QuantEcon.py: storing sorted state indices, exposing `num_sa_pairs`, and adding `to_sa_pair_form` / `to_product_form` converters. The PR is tied to benchmark guidance that sparse state-action form can be up to about 30x faster on low-fill models, so the converters are the migration path from natural product-form definitions to the faster representation. Review follow-up removed an unnecessary dense `Matrix(ddp.Q)` materialization in the product-form conversion, and the author reports 318 `test_ddp` cases plus the full package suite passing.

### [Zcash adds an Orchard-to-Ironwood migration engine](https://github.com/zcash/librustzcash/pull/2572) — Chlup / Zcash
A large librustzcash PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 adds `zcash_pool_migration`, a network-free engine for migrating mobile-wallet funds from the Orchard pool to the Ironwood pool. The branch plans self-funding power-of-ten denominations, builds and signs migration transactions as PCZTs, supports software and external-signer flows, schedules transfers by block height, and persists state in five `ext_ironwood_migration_*` tables. Its public trail is unusually detailed: design and TDD-plan commits, 95 unit tests, four seeded-wallet end-to-end tests that mine and reconcile real transactions, workspace-wide `cargo test --all-features`, clippy `-D warnings`, fmt, and doc builds.

### [cibuildwheel compares an unedited Fable refactor plan](https://github.com/pypa/cibuildwheel/pull/2907) — Henry Schreiner / PyPA
Henry Schreiner opened a cibuildwheel PR explicitly as an alternative to an Opus 4.8 attempt, this time using Claude Fable 5 and leaving the generated plan unedited. The branch extracts duplicated per-wheel build loops into a shared runner, then ports Pyodide, macOS, Windows, iOS, Android, and Linux one by one while documenting behavior differences such as stricter multi-wheel repair errors, Android test-environment handling, and a latent Linux temp-dir bug. Schreiner says it is not mergeable as-is but may become reviewable after iteration; maintainer feedback in the thread says the central `run_builds` logic reads well while raising concerns about hidden state and reader context.

### [Silo Server halves a production vector index](https://github.com/Silo-Server/silo-server/pull/351) — RXWatcher / Silo Server
Silo Server used Claude Code with Fable 5 to add a binary-quantized Meilisearch embedding option for catalog search, defaulting it on only for fresh installs so existing deployments do not silently close their sync gate and freeze search. The author benchmarked it on a 607.9k-document production catalog with 3,072-dimensional embeddings: on-disk index size dropped from 18G to 8.3G, pure semantic median latency went from 9.0ms to 4.0ms, hybrid search stayed within the keyword-control jitter band at 7.5ms versus 8.0ms, and rebuild time stayed around 41–47 minutes. Tests pin the schema-hash behavior, and the PR reports `go build`, `go vet`, catalog tests, web ESLint, Prettier, and local-path verification passing.

### [SolveSpace gets NURBS boolean fixes from a layman’s Fable session](https://github.com/solvespace/solvespace/pull/1729) — BoykoNeov / SolveSpace
BoykoNeov describes a SolveSpace NURBS-boolean PR as “100% LLMs work” from a contributor who calls himself a very basic programmer and a layman in this code. The Fable 5 co-authored commits find an OpenMP use-after-free in exact-curve handling, replace an uninitialized-output path in tangent edge-on-edge classification, add a volume-based regression for tangent shell edges, and handle knife-edge cases where more than two surfaces meet. Maintainers reacted strongly in the thread — “This is astonishing” and “The LLM is truly impressive” — while also checking many sketches for regressions and identifying which related issues the branch does and does not fix.

### [Texera cuts a 400-file browser freeze to about a second](https://github.com/apache/texera/pull/6285) — kunwp1 / Apache Texera
Apache Texera has a Claude Code / Claude Fable 5 PR for a frontend freeze in dataset-version browsing. The root cause was concrete: every file in a selected version rendered as a full Angular tree-node with virtualization disabled, and each row re-read layout during change detection. On a local `ng serve` stack with Chrome Long Tasks API, the author measured a 400-file version going from 24.5 seconds before the page became usable to 1.3 seconds, main-thread blocking from 22.2 seconds to 0.2 seconds, and DOM rows from 400 to 28. The PR also links a recorded test artifact and follows up with fit, throttle, comment, fixture, and coverage commits.

### [Silo Android runs a phone QA sweep across playback and navigation](https://github.com/Silo-Server/silo-android/pull/49) — RXWatcher / Silo Server
Silo Android’s Claude Fable 5 phone QA PR covers about 37 items across onboarding, Home, detail pages, the player, People, Library browse, For You, Calendar, and downloads. The write-up calls out specific root causes: duplicate shared-element keys caused poster flicker, subtitle overrides fingerprinted the mounted track list but restored against catalog tracks, Material 3 post-fling velocity hid player sheets, HDR black-screen failures evaded stall detection because audio kept progressing, and a title layout starved portrait-player controls. The branch spans 55 files with focused tests for HDR engine routing, subtitle fingerprints, and ordinal translation, plus reported `:androidApp:assembleDebug`, `:androidTvApp:assembleDebug`, and full unit-test-suite passes.

### [Tunga fixes three i18n extraction bugs plus scan noise](https://github.com/vardirhq/tunga/pull/5) — MadsenDev / Tunga
Tunga’s merged Fable co-authored PR fixes an i18n extraction/apply loop that could silently rewrite code to the wrong locale key when collision suffixes were involved. It also makes inline handler bodies visible to both scanner and codemod, preserves significant JSX whitespace so text like `297 GB used` does not fuse into `297 GBused`, and applies the low-confidence filter consistently so extraction stops writing orphaned keys. The same branch adds CSS/SVG candidate filtering, word-boundary key truncation, a review manifest, denylist and ignore-directive support, and an interactive scan checklist.

### [Yellow Core makes setup drift machine-checkable](https://github.com/KingInYellows/yellow-plugins/pull/625) — KingInYellow18 / yellow-plugins
A Fable 5 co-authored yellow-core PR turns several setup-documentation drifts into validator errors instead of checklist prose. The branch removes a stale ToolSearch probe, fixes a missing dashboard row, distinguishes READY, PARTIAL, and NEEDS SETUP for `yellow-devin`, surfaces plugin dependency relationships, and makes the validator derive command mappings from markdown plus real command files. It adds `ERROR-SETUP-001` through `ERROR-SETUP-007`, CRLF-tolerant frontmatter parsing, and 11 integration fixtures that reintroduce historical drift classes and assert the corresponding error codes.

### [Sporive stops a password setup failure from looking successful](https://github.com/sho29saka31/Sporive/pull/23) — sho29saka31 / Sporive
Three linked Sporive PRs co-authored by Claude Fable 5 trace a real authentication failure from diagnosis through recovery UX. The first removes a dangerous fallback where Supabase `current_password_required` caused `/signup/set-password` to mark `password_set` true without actually saving the entered password; the [second](https://github.com/sho29saka31/Sporive/pull/24) adds an account-settings password form that reauthenticates existing-password users and shares password validation; the [third](https://github.com/sho29saka31/Sporive/pull/25) adds a reset-email recovery path after two affected accounts were found. Each PR reports TypeScript, lint, and build passing while explicitly noting that Supabase API behavior still needed preview-environment confirmation.

### [A server template records live deployment lessons as pipeline checks](https://github.com/uppertoe/server-instance-template/pull/27) — uppertoe
A Fable 5 co-authored PR to `server-instance-template` folds lessons from a hands-off live deployment back into the reusable scaffold. The update adds state and weekly freshness checks for out-of-repo realities that otherwise go stale silently: Mend app settings, publish gates, rebuild schedules, cron workflows disabled after 60 days of repository inactivity, and Renovate going quiet. It also splits first-party digest updates into their own Renovate group, documents deploy-signature verification hosts, and carries template fixes for running-image vulnerability scans, signed-deploy trust, AIDE rebaselining, and CI compliance convergence.

### [Three wrong-metric ML tests separate prediction from decisions](https://alphasignalai.substack.com/p/claude-fable-5s-hardest-test-was) — AlphaSignal AI
AlphaSignal ran Claude Fable 5 on three private ML debugging repos built around misleading success metrics. In the first, Fable gave up perfect validation after finding post-outcome leakage; in the second, it ignored a plausible drift story and traced a missed Enterprise Plus segment to a plan-name schema mismatch; in the third, it replaced raw churn targeting with an uplift model and expected-net-value ranking. The hardest case was only a partial pass — 68.8% of offered accounts were still negative-value on the private potential-outcomes holdout — and an Opus 4.8 rerun made slightly more simulated money while Fable found more true uplift.

### [RevenueCat benchmarks config endpoints through live SDK paths](https://github.com/RevenueCat/purchases-ios/pull/7174) — facumenzella / RevenueCat
RevenueCat’s Fable 5-assisted PR adds a macOS `SDKConfigBenchmark` suite that measures legacy offerings delivery against a new config-endpoint path through the same manager-level SDK calls used by `Purchases.updateAllCaches`.
