It ships deterministic simulated transports, live runs against a pinned stress-test project, JSONL percentile output, a comparison gate that rejects invalid or mismatched rows, 65 unit tests, and methodology docs. The author records a six-pass Codex review loop around Fable’s implementation, with headline findings that config cold LTE runs are about 10x slower when all workflows are prefetch-gated, warm config delivery is at parity with legacy, and the kill switch costs about one extra API round trip per launch.

### [cmux makes a 20-attempt SSH retry loop actually retry](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux/pull/7711) — austinywang / cmux
A cmux PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 fixes a remote SSH PTY reconnect path that advertised 20 attempts but stopped after the first retry because bridge-establishment failures exited with fatal code 1 instead of retryable 255. The branch starts with a red regression test for transient `workspace.remote.pty_bridge` failures, then adds a typed exit-code taxonomy, structured v2 error-code classification, a bounded pre-ready bridge wait so silent bridges do not hang forever, and state-preserving cleanup for wrapper-retryable failures. Later review-driven commits add tests for session-lost messages and `pty_attach_end` behavior, showing the Fable loop moving from reproducer to protocol-level cleanup rather than a one-line status-code patch.

### [Sporive routes Supabase recovery links through PKCE callback handling](https://github.com/sho29saka31/Sporive/pull/26) — sho29saka31 / Sporive
A follow-up Sporive PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 fixes another real account-recovery edge case: logged-in users opening a password-reset link were redirected to `/home?code=...` before the recovery code could be exchanged. The diagnosis ties the failure to Supabase SSR’s PKCE query-parameter flow and middleware that redirected authenticated users away from `/login`; the fix routes password reset, magic-link, and email-change flows through the existing `/auth/callback` code-exchange endpoint. The PR reports `npx tsc --noEmit`, lint, and a 21-route production build passing, while explicitly noting that live Supabase email-link behavior still needs preview-environment confirmation.

### [PyTorch defers NVSHMEM frees until a collective allocation boundary](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/189343) — crcrpar / PyTorch
A PyTorch PR authored with Claude Opus 4.8 and Fable addresses a distributed-systems footgun in symmetric memory: `nvshmem_free` is collective, but tensor storage destruction is local and unordered. Instead of freeing from the storage deleter, the patch assigns collective allocation ids, queues local frees, and drains only the ids all ranks agree on at the next collective allocation boundary, avoiding divergent free order and deadlock. The trade-off is explicit in the PR: memory from free-heavy phases stays resident until a later collective allocation, but the free path becomes collective-safe without forcing every tensor destructor into a rank-synchronized operation.

### [AutomationBench-AA puts Fable near the top on guarded SaaS workflows](https://artificialanalysis.ai/articles/announcing-zapier-automationbench-aa) — Artificial Analysis
Artificial Analysis published an independent AutomationBench-AA leaderboard for 657 workflow tasks across 40 simulated SaaS environments, including Gmail, Sheets, Slack, Salesforce, Zendesk, Jira, and HubSpot. Its headline metric counts objectives completed without guardrail violations, where Claude Fable 5 scored 48.6%, essentially tied with Claude Opus 4.8 at 48.5% and ahead of Gemini 3.5 Flash at 42.6% and GPT-5.5 xhigh at 42.1%. The write-up also notes that Anthropic’s classifier fell back from Fable to Opus on about 18% of tasks, making the result a useful harness-level benchmark rather than a clean single-model score.

### [Nethermind compacts flat-state snapshots with sorted pooled content](https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/pull/12353) — asdacap / Nethermind
Nethermind’s Fable 5 co-authored flat-DB PR replaces `ConcurrentDictionary`-based snapshot compaction with sorted, immutable, pooled content built by k-way loser-tree merges. The commit trail starts with a benchmark, removes an O(self-destructs × accumulated-size) scan, then adds sorted merge dictionaries, base-snapshot conversion, pooled reuse, and review fixes for exception cleanup and zero-source merges. The PR reports 576 `Nethermind.State.Flat` tests passing and a compaction benchmark where the 32-snapshot sorted case runs in 382 ms with 4.4 KB allocated, versus roughly 1,088 ms and 600 MB for the original `ConcurrentDictionary` compactor.

### [TapHome’s Home Assistant integration moves from YAML to tested UI config](https://github.com/martindybal/taphome-homeassistant/pull/207) — martindybal
A long Fable 5 co-authored TapHome Home Assistant branch converts the integration to config entries, guided options flows, reauth/reconfigure handling, device repair flows, and an external `taphome-sdk` package. The commits show the work moving from teardown primitives and auth errors through a mock TapHome core server, offline config-flow tests, platform tests for cover/climate/select, Home Assistant device-registry naming, YAML import, documentation rewrites, and CI fixes. The PR spans 108 files with more than 15,000 additions, but the useful signal is the sequence of concrete integration changes and test scaffolding rather than a one-shot generated port.

### [Dust gives agents live database tools without letting publish mutate schemas](https://github.com/dust-tt/dust/pull/28720) — id13 / Dust
Dust has a three-PR Fable 5 series around sandbox-function databases: [queries can now run SELECT plus gated DML while refusing DDL](https://github.com/dust-tt/dust/pull/28718), [stored schema manifests and compatibility diffs are removed in favor of live reconcile gates](https://github.com/dust-tt/dust/pull/28719), and the headline PR exposes `db_list`, `db_schema`, `db_query`, and `db_reconcile` tools to agents. The commits are careful about first-keyword SQL gating before prepare, BEGIN IMMEDIATE writes with schema-version rechecks, additive-only reconcile, WAL-inclusive size reporting, spill-capped query results, and model-correctable error mapping. Follow-up review commits make `db_reconcile` the only apply path and stop publish from claiming it can create or mutate live databases.

### [Dataviz rewrites graph aggregation as a resumable diff pipeline](https://github.com/rkrumins/dataviz/pull/133) — rkrumins
A single Fable 5 co-authored Dataviz PR replaces three legacy FalkorDB `:AGGREGATED` materialization strategies with an EXTRACT → COMPUTE → RECONCILE → APPLY pipeline. The new design avoids O(E²) ordered scans by partitioning fixed ID ranges, computes ancestor rollups in worker memory, writes only guarded diffs, persists a v3 cursor from the first checkpoint, and makes each phase resumable so a failed run cannot wipe good edges. It also adds Redis-backed admission control, FalkorDB timeout and memory settings, progress-aware job watchdogs, UI phase labels, and tests for semantics, overflow weights, cancel/resume, no-op reruns, guarded deletes, cursor contracts, and admission control.

### [RoSys turns leaking page repeaters into explicit client-scoped lifetimes](https://github.com/zauberzeug/rosys/pull/429) — falkoschindler / RoSys
RoSys used Fable 5 on a design-heavy fix for page-owned `on_repeat` handlers that otherwise keep their owner objects alive forever through the global task registry. The PR evolves through reviewer pushback: an initial `scope` API gains startup-stop fixes, handler release on client deletion, a CameraProjector lifetime escape hatch, removal of the tempting but unsafe `auto` inference, and finally a smaller `client_scoped=True` flag. The thread includes runnable counterexamples, analysis of `gc.disable()`, weak-reference cleanup limits, and why the chosen fix tears down behavior deterministically even when object graphs may wait for pressure-triggered collection.

### [nntrainer speeds an int8 GEMM kernel on Galaxy S26+](https://github.com/nntrainer/nntrainer/pull/4065) — jaemini-shin / nntrainer
nntrainer’s Fable 5 co-authored PR replaces a one-dimensional GEMM split heuristic with cache-aware two-dimensional tiling for the fastest `qai8dxp_qsi4cxp` KleidiAI path. The author measured the 1024×2560×4096 i8mm kernel on a Galaxy S26+, where the main comparison went from 11.62 ms to 10.18 ms, while a focused interleaved run reported 10.99 ms to 10.21 ms with six wins out of six and lower variance. A Qwen3-0.6B model test improved prefill throughput from 571.429 to 620.606 TPS, with build and run tests marked passing.

### [Paperclip cuts multi-minute agent-auth stalls](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/pull/9280) — lacymorrow / Paperclip
Paperclip used Claude Fable 5 through Claude Code and the Agent SDK to diagnose authenticated API routes stalling for 30–150 seconds while `/api/health` stayed fast on busy multi-agent instances. The PR traces the failure to auth middleware doing up to four sequential Postgres pool waits per request, then verifies self-contained agent JWTs before key-table lookups, gates board-key checks by token prefix, throttles `lastUsedAt` writes, and raises the configurable pool default. The report includes 50 server auth tests, database pool parsing tests, TypeScript checks, and a live-instance latency check under a synthetic 12-concurrent-heavy-read load.

### [Ozone fixes masked stream-read failures in HDDS](https://github.com/apache/ozone/pull/10698) — smengcl / Apache Ozone
Apache Ozone has a Claude Code Fable 5-generated patch for two `StreamBlockInputStream` failure paths in the HDDS client. If a datanode ended a gRPC stream while the client still awaited data, `poll()` could return null and trigger a non-IOException NPE that bypassed retry and failover handling; a separate `onNext` error path could throw while building a short-payload hex preview, masking the real failure. The two-file patch changes the stream reader and adds unit coverage in `TestStreamBlockInputStream`.

### [PostHog ties wizard-created PRs back to their task runs](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/69591) — fercgomes / PostHog
PostHog’s Fable 5-assisted PR fixes onboarding wizard runs that opened a GitHub PR but then stayed forever on “Opening a pull request.” The root cause combined bot-authored PRs that failed the sandbox-login identity check with webhook matching against the checkout branch rather than the agent-invented head branch; one production run sat stuck until the sandbox’s six-hour TTL killed it. The patch makes the server generate and persist the PR head branch, templates that branch into the agent prompt, adds a repository-scoped webhook lookup, and reports 80 local tests across the webhook and facade suites.

### [Philomena starts pinning hundreds of controller actions](https://github.com/philomena-dev/philomena/pull/708) — Meow / Philomena
Philomena’s characterization-test PR, generated by Claude Fable 5 and Claude Opus 4.8, sets out to pin the current behavior of roughly 200 Phoenix controller modules and 454 routed actions before future refactors. The plan records status codes, redirects, flash messages, JSON shapes, HTML markers, side effects, and anonymous/user/moderator/admin/banned-user auth matrices, while treating odd existing behavior as something to document rather than silently “fix.” The branch is already large — 262 files and more than 28,000 additions — with fixture modules, role helpers, OpenSearch test strategy, external-call stubs, conventions, field notes, and known oddities committed as test infrastructure.

### [Elixir cross-checks public specs against inferred types](https://github.com/elixir-lang/elixir/pull/15559) — lukaszsamson / Elixir
Elixir has a Fable-assisted PR that turns the compiler’s inferred type contracts into a CI cross-check against public Dialyzer specs. The script translated standard-library specs into Elixir’s `Descr` representation and surfaced enough real mismatches to tighten several specs, while also documenting 21 translation artifacts, 37 intentional API-contract widenings, 54 opacity cases, and machine-dependent or deprecated exceptions. The same branch adds OTP 28 nominal-type handling and trims the checker down to pure static analysis before making it a gate.

### [IceStorm audit fixes backup ordering and replica edge cases](https://github.com/zeroc-ice/ice/pull/5900) — pepone / ZeroC Ice
ZeroC Ice has two IceStorm PRs produced by an automated Claude Fable 5 correctness audit and cross-verified by Codex. The [database fix](https://github.com/zeroc-ice/ice/pull/5900) makes `icestormdb` open subscriber records with the same custom LMDB comparator as the service, preventing restored backups from missing or resurrecting subscriptions when identities differ in length; it also makes truncated exports fail nonzero and extends import/export/restart tests. A companion [correctness batch](https://github.com/zeroc-ice/ice/pull/5903) fixes five lower-severity issues, including node-id stagger underflow, null observer acceptance, a destroy/re-create servant race, `Send.QueueSizeMax=0` queue corruption, and brittle subscriber-admin lookup.

### [QuantEcon.py gets a 2.7x dense Bellman-operator speedup](https://github.com/QuantEcon/QuantEcon.py/pull/857) — oyamad / QuantEcon
A Claude Code run with Fable 5 produced the Python side of QuantEcon’s dynamic-programming performance pass. For product-form dense transition arrays, the PR replaces per-state batched `Q @ v` dispatch with a single two-dimensional GEMV view when the array is C-contiguous, while preserving the old path for non-contiguous inputs and adding regression coverage. The author reports bit-identical solutions and measured `bellman_operator` speedups from 2.79 ms to 1.03 ms on a 500-by-100 case and from 66 µs to 22 µs on a 100-by-50 case, while explicitly rejecting a slower fused Numba max/argmax kernel.

### [PostHog starts routing transformations through its Rust HogVM](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/69605) — jose-sequeira / PostHog
PostHog used Claude Code with Fable 5 to move customer-data-platform transformations from shadow comparison toward real Rust HogVM execution. Behind `CDP_HOG_RUST_VM_EXECUTION_ENABLED`, the branch adds a synchronous N-API `executeSync` path, per-invocation fallback to the Node VM for unsupported programs or missing addons, sensitive-value-redacted print-log capture, timing/cost mapping, and rollout metrics, while avoiding fallback on real execution errors so programs do not run twice. The PR lists local Rust tests, a real N-API build, mocked executor tests, 33 Jest integration tests for routing and fallback, plus TypeScript, ESLint, and Prettier checks.

### [Tab Atelier removes several terminal paint-loop stalls](https://github.com/wdes/tab-atelier/pull/11) — williamdes
A merged Tab Atelier PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 attacks repeated work and lock contention in a terminal UI. The headline commit wraps cached painted rows in `Rc` so every frame no longer deep-clones visible `RawLine` text and shaping segments while holding the parser’s terminal lock, and moves a 16 ms repaint-pump `display_offset` read behind a nonblocking `try_lock_unfair` path after earlier measurement showed parser-held-lock stalls around p99 540 ms. Follow-up commits in the same batch skip viewer wakeups on unwatched tabs, cache websocket inbox/outbox scans, share snapshot grid dumps, reduce per-tab process walks, and stop per-frame subprocess spawns and QR re-encodes.

### [A repo review turns into seeded-data and evaluator fixes](https://github.com/ctrimm/synthetic-gov-data-kit/pull/5) — ctrimm
Synthetic Gov Data Kit used Claude Code with Fable 5 to turn a full repository review into implementation work rather than a checklist. The PR fixes documentation that referenced nonexistent files and classes, adds CI across Python 3.10–3.12, and closes correctness bugs such as non-reproducible `seed=42` case IDs caused by `uuid.uuid4()`, substring scoring where “eligible” matched “ineligible,” shared mutable dicts returned from cached JSON loaders, a Pydantic `validate` name collision, and duplicate JSON keys in a notebook. It also adds a Medicaid generator and Virginia/Texas presets, raises tests from 109 to 263, moves reported coverage from 76% to 95%, and clears 439 ruff and 38 mypy errors.

### [mewlang builds an e-graph language and interactive inspector](https://github.com/kumavis/vibe-dump/pull/9) — kumavis
A merged Fable 5 co-authored `vibe-dump` PR builds `mewlang`, a small functional language evaluated by equality saturation. The core commit includes a lexer and recursive-descent parser with source spans, a union-find/hashcons e-graph engine, monotone lattice joins, congruence repair, arithmetic, conditionals and unfolding rules, BSP rounds, seeded-shuffle confluence mode, fuel limits, and cost-min extraction. Later commits add an interactive webapp and tighten the correctness contract after a six-agent adversarial review, with tests for parser behavior, lattice laws, propagator rules, scheduler behavior, 25-seed confluence on `fib(8)`, golden examples such as `fib(10)=55`, `gcd`, `sum-to(100)`, and source-map round trips.

### [Nethermind extends FlatDB snapshots to long-finality windows](https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/pull/12142) — asdacap / Nethermind
Nethermind has a large Fable co-authored FlatDB PR that adds an opt-in persisted-snapshot tier so the in-memory snapshot ring can stretch from a roughly 256-block window toward configurable long-finality depths around 90,000 blocks. The branch replaces an earlier HSST design with a simpler SortedTable format, mmap-backed arenas, page-residency tracking, and logarithmic compaction, across 116 files and more than 17,000 additions. The public thread also shows Fable used as reviewer: it found a non-reentrant-lock deadlock, cleanup and lease issues, unvalidated corrupt-table length fields, and later separated real concurrency findings from false positives, with the author reporting 777 `Nethermind.State.Flat.Test` cases passing after fixes.

### [A benchmark evaluator starts judging agent diffs first](https://github.com/NoesisVision/nasde-toolkit/pull/75) — Szymon Janikowski / NoesisVision
NoesisVision’s NASDE toolkit PR uses Fable 5 for a full human-in-the-loop calibration round rather than a single generated feature. Starting from 13 sink PRs, 60 inline comments, and a human-approved A/B/C/D reference ranking for a weather-discount task, the branch adds per-task assessment dimensions, deterministic host prechecks, and a diff-first judge prompt so evaluators can see removals and off-task edits before scoring final state. Later Fable co-authored commits record a 10-evaluation Fable subset, measured rubric defects, v2.1 through v2.3 fixes, and live checks where predicted anchor orderings and negative controls matched the revised rubric.

### [Bitwuzla reconstructs signed remainders before bit-blasting](https://github.com/bitwuzla/bitwuzla/pull/212) — Atanazyy / Bitwuzla
A Fable co-authored Bitwuzla PR adds `BV_ADD_SREM`, the signed analogue of an existing unsigned-remainder rewrite. The concrete failure case comes from translation-validation queries such as Alive2: after signed division and remainder are lowered, the solver could miss that `a - (a sdiv b) * b` is a signed remainder and bit-blast two independent signed divisions, turning a trivial remainder-shaped query into a 90-second-plus timeout. The patch registers the new rule at rewrite level 1 and adds unit coverage for matching shapes and near misses.

### [Lunima unifies photonic coupler semantics across UI and simulation](https://github.com/aignermax/Lunima/pull/703) — aignermax / Lunima
Two Lunima PRs show Fable 5 working through a photonics app’s boundary between UI labels and simulation semantics. The first [unifies light-source detection](https://github.com/aignermax/Lunima/pull/699) so suffixed PDK grating and edge coupler names expose wavelength/input-power controls consistently across the properties panel, transient factory, and sweep code, while a maintainer follow-up corrects passive splitter classification. The headline PR then adds per-coupler laser on/off state so disabled couplers become outputs, with canvas controls, persistence, transient/CW wiring, eye-trace selection, help text, and an automated Fable review that found a blocking transient-mode off-icon visibility gap before approval.

### [A public corpus compares Fable and Sonnet on two coding tasks](https://github.com/Unsiloed-AI/cookbook/pull/11) — vikashx10 / Unsiloed AI
Unsiloed AI added a reproducibility-oriented cookbook entry for a Claude Sonnet 5 versus Claude Fable 5 coding comparison. The PR does not just summarize impressions: it checks in the exact prompts and both models’ exported outputs for two tasks, a browser-based Clash Royale-style real-time lane battle game and a RAG Debugger feature added inside the roughly 400,000-line Open WebUI codebase. The import is huge because it includes each model’s full Open WebUI fork and generated app code, giving readers artifacts they can inspect rather than only a leaderboard claim.

### [FLINT gets radix square roots generated from division code patterns](https://github.com/flintlib/flint/pull/2773) — Fredrik Johansson / FLINT
Fredrik Johansson opened a FLINT PR saying the new radix square-root functions were generated with Claude Fable 5 after asking it to follow the strategy used by the library’s division functions. The branch adds `radix_rsqrt_approx`, `radix_sqrt_approx`, `radix_sqrtrem`, integer square-root helpers, tests, and profile programs, while also tightening Hensel square-root code and improving `gr_poly` power-series square roots. Johansson reports radix square roots becoming about 2x faster than `mpn_sqrtrem` asymptotically and approximate square roots up to 3x faster, with a benchmark table covering 19 through 157 million decimal digits.

### [A 133k-prompt DECtalk phoneme gate goes from 46% to 99.96%](https://github.com/pktck/dectalk-python/pull/296) — pktck
dectalk-python merged a Fable-authored parity repair for a corpus phoneme gate that had never actually run in CI and had rotted to a 53.8% failure rate. The commits align dotted-decimal expansion with the C runtime, adjust 96 lexicon entries after corpus and oracle checks, and wire the 133,641-prompt gate into the 16-shard C-oracle lane. The measured result is 133,585 byte-identical prompts, or 99.958%, with the 56 remaining divergences committed as an allowlist and CI reporting lint, test matrix, shellcheck, C-oracle tests, and perf bench all passing.

### [powsybl-core profiles IIDM and CGMES hot paths into a large performance sweep](https://github.com/gautierbureau/powsybl-core/pull/21) — gautierbureau
A Fable 5 co-authored powsybl-core PR starts from profiling IIDM and CGMES import/export hot paths, then applies changes across topology models, variant handling, triple-store queries, serializers, converters, and criteria filters. The commit trail records concrete measurements: a parameterless structural-query cache moved PEGASE 13659 CGMES import from 7,567 ms to 6,850 ms, and replacing per-object stream pipelines in variant clone/remove moved a 50-run PEGASE case from 5,900 ms to 3,223 ms. The branch also documents rejected or within-noise attempts, including a reverted StAX writer experiment, and reports module suites ranging from 905 touched-module tests to 997 IIDM implementation tests passing.

### [Three spec-kit extension bugs get fail-before, pass-after fixes](https://github.com/github/spec-kit/pull/3380) — jawwad-ali / spec-kit
Jawwad Ali submitted a run of Claude Fable 5-assisted spec-kit fixes around extension and bundle edge cases: [preserved config files were wiped on reinstall](https://github.com/github/spec-kit/pull/3380), [prefix-colliding environment variables could crash or silently disable hook conditions](https://github.com/github/spec-kit/pull/3350), and [catalog bundle handling had to reject local `file://` download URLs after an initial Windows path fix was reframed in review](https://github.com/github/spec-kit/pull/3344). Each PR includes a reproducer against main and a new test that fails before the patch and passes after it; the keep-config case also picked up a review fix to preserve the original file mode because the config may contain secrets. The useful signal is the trail from diagnosis to tests to maintainer feedback, not just the small diff sizes.

### [Fable review hardens a robot flashing tool after the initial cleanup](https://github.com/zauberzeug/lizard/pull/227) — evnchn / Zauberzeug
Zauberzeug’s lizard PR began as a cleanup that consolidated stale ESP flashing scripts onto `espresso.py`, then two Fable review passes and follow-up commits turned it into a broader remote-tooling hardening pass. The branch adds `--host user@host[:path]`, folds core dumps into `espresso.py coredump`, makes module import side-effect-free, replaces global config with a frozen dataclass, and derives command behavior from a single registry. Later Fable co-authored fixes catch concrete operational failures: `user@robot:` creating the wrong remote directory, `--baud` being ignored for coredumps, dry-run previews omitting sudo, rsync copying stale build artifacts, lost execute bits on the remote script, and missing ESP-IDF environment notes for remote coredumps.

### [A Fable-backed Lean harness closes an Erdős formalization target on the first attempt](https://github.com/google-deepmind/formal-conjectures/pull/4411) — kukushking / Google DeepMind formal-conjectures
A formal-conjectures PR reports that a custom Lean-kernel-verified theorem-proving harness using Claude Fable 5 closed `Erdos92.possible_f_values_BddAbove` on attempt 1 with no target-specific hints. The proof shows the feasible values defining the Erdős Problem 92 function are bounded above by using any point in `A` and bounding its equidistant classes by `n`, choosing the weaker `n` bound to avoid edge cases around `n - 1`. The author reports `lake build FormalConjectures.ErdosProblems.«92»` completing 8,795 jobs, the theorem statement unchanged, and an axiom check depending only on Lean foundation axioms rather than `sorryAx` or user axioms.

### [Secrets are encrypted value by value without losing diffs](https://github.com/Pranav-Karra-3301/tuck/pull/115) — Pranav-Karra-3301 / tuck
tuck’s Fable 5 co-authored PR adds a SOPS-style mode that encrypts only detected secret values while leaving keys, comments, whitespace, and file structure readable for code review and three-way merges. Each value becomes an independent `ENC[tuck:v1:...]` token carrying its own PBKDF2 parameters, salt, nonce, and AES-GCM tag, so re-encrypting a file can leave existing tokens byte-identical. The public review found two practical failures before merge — unquoted `.env` assignments could encrypt the whole `KEY=value` span, and the quick start could commit the still-plaintext tracked copy without `tuck sync` — and the follow-up commit fixed both, with `pnpm test` reporting 1,538 tests across 148 files plus green typecheck, lint, build, CI, and a sandboxed encrypt/sync/decrypt round trip.

### [A Turkish analytics app turns a Fable UI audit into dozens of fixes](https://github.com/aieasycep/AYAZ/pull/2) — aieasycep / AYAZ
AYAZ’s PR starts with a Fable 5 UI/UX audit of 41 desktop screens, 8 mobile screens, and 4 dark-theme screens using real demo data, checked across visual hierarchy, information architecture, first-use, data visualization, Turkish copy, mobile, dark mode, and accessibility. The branch then moves from report to implementation: target-progress ratios that showed 1% instead of 141%, spend goals being celebrated while over budget, ASCII Turkish, English number formatting, raw UUID/channel-key leakage, contradictory pricing, and clipped mobile tables are all addressed in later commits. The run also records several second-pass review loops, including fixes for budget recommendations telling users to spend faster when forecasts already exceeded budget, accessibility labels on form controls, visible errors for previously silent failures, and reported backend/frontend/build gates such as 2,994 backend tests and 603 frontend tests.

### [Lunima adds a canvas probe for photonic waveguide modes](https://github.com/aignermax/Lunima/pull/706) — aignermax / Lunima
Lunima’s latest Fable 5-assisted photonics PR adds an explicit Probe mode: clicking a waveguide or component opens a flyout with fundamental-mode data such as effective index, group index, polarization, a mode-field image, and estimated mode-field diameter through the project’s existing mode-solver backends. The implementation auto-fills cross-section data from the clicked geometry and active PDK, shows exactly which geometry was assumed when it falls back to manual defaults, and computes Gaussian fiber-overlap efficiency for grating and edge couplers. A physics guard detects MMI, multimode, and star-coupler components and tells users to use FDTD instead of showing a misleading single-mode slice; the PR reports 52 unit tests across overlap math, cross-section provenance, classifiers, MFD estimation, and ViewModel behavior, with an automated Fable review marking the acceptance criteria met and leaving only nits.

### [A smart-collection cap hid almost all matches in production](https://github.com/Silo-Server/silo-server/pull/340) — RXWatcher / Silo Server
Silo Server’s Fable 5-updated PR removes a hidden smart-collection cap that silently limited every collection to 500 items, with unset limits defaulting to 100. On the author’s production server, a French-language smart collection had 10,625 real matches but showed under 1% of them, cut off mid-alphabet; the hotfix image was run in production with all 50 language collections resolving their full membership. A follow-up commit catches the next scaling problem before merge: Jellyfin BoxSet browsing would have materialized tens of thousands of smart-collection rows per request, so the branch adds SQL paging for default smart-query browsing while preserving true total counts, with `go build`, `go vet`, and `go test ./internal/catalog/` reported green.

### [Fable file chips stop tripping a stuck-agent detector](https://github.com/seanssoh/agent-bridge-public/pull/2416) — seanssoh / agent-bridge-public
agent-bridge-public has a concrete field repro from agents running Fable 5: when Fable renders attached files as line-leading `› [file] ... (390.1KB)` chips, two such rows above an idle composer looked like a selector option group to the pane classifier. That made healthy idle agents re-enter `unknown_stuck` and escalate about every five minutes even though the ready prompt was present. The fix excludes file-attachment reference chips at the shared `_caret_has_option_text` gate, anchored on the combination of a leading `[type]` token and trailing byte size, while deliberately keeping the matcher narrow so a genuine stuck picker still escalates.

### [Bun rewrites its 535,000-line Zig runtime in Rust](https://bun.com/blog/bun-in-rust) — Jarred Sumner / Bun
Jarred Sumner says Bun used a pre-release Claude Fable 5 and Claude Code dynamic workflows to mechanically port its JavaScript runtime from 535,496 lines of Zig to Rust. The run kept as many as 64 Claude workers active across four worktrees for 11 days, with one implementer and two adversarial reviewers per task, producing 6,778 commits and a merged [rewrite PR](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/pull/30412) with about 1.0 million additions. Sumner reports that the port reached 100% of Bun’s existing test suite on all six supported platforms with no tests skipped or deleted, after 135 Buildkite test builds and manual checks that the suites were really running. The canary Rust build also records concrete post-merge results: all instrumentable memory leaks fixed, 19 known porting regressions already repaired, roughly 20% smaller Linux and Windows binaries, and several HTTP/app benchmarks landing 2–5% faster than Bun 1.3.14.

### [Sandbox profiles add a host-persistence write floor](https://github.com/tbuckley/escape-clause/pull/5) — tbuckley / escape-clause
escape-clause used Claude Fable 5 on a security-hardening PR for a Claude Code sandbox wrapper. The branch adds default, strict, and paranoid directory-access profiles across both the bash sandbox and an unsandboxed file-tool guard, then places LaunchAgents, shell rc files, XDG autostart, systemd user units, Homebrew and local bin directories, git config, and Claude credential paths under a write-deny floor. The PR includes 41 guard decision tests, drift checks for the generated policy file, and explicit notes on what still needs behavioral verification on real macOS and non-root Linux hosts, including IPC-based persistence through launchctl or systemctl.

### [A game server adds warmed jump gates with five-agent review](https://github.com/komleff/u2/pull/546) — komleff / U2
U2’s server-side “Gates-2” PR uses Claude Fable 5 as both implementer and reviewer for a new gate-readiness protocol and migration guard. The change bumps the protocol to 21, adds per-player `GateReadiness` state, warms nearby open routes through a Cold → Warming → Ready FSM, and blocks sector migration until the gate is ready while keeping world snapshots unchanged. The public thread records 1,300 NUnit tests and 321 Vitest tests passing, then an internal Fable review by five independent subagents that found four fix-now issues — including a fail-open optional gate and protobuf type mismatches — followed by a second review pass approving the fixes.

### [Freelens drives a large Jest-to-Vitest migration green](https://github.com/freelensapp/freelens/pull/2115) — dex4er / Freelens
Freelens reports a Claude Code run with model `claude-fable-5` on phase 6 of its v2 test migration. The PR fixes about 1,450 core test failures by loading source-only workspace injectables through eager `import.meta.glob`, then clears the remaining Vitest tail around multiline `jest.fn` chains, `xdescribe.each`, CSS-module snapshot behavior, worker isolation, async mocks, and build memory. The result is a full Vitest run of 328 files with 3,370 passing and 44 skipped tests, plus a reactivated unit-tests workflow that still needs a maintainer to move the generated workflow file because the CI token cannot edit workflows.

### [TensorDict speeds threaded consolidation and fixes futures](https://github.com/pytorch/tensordict/pull/1740) — vmoens / PyTorch TensorDict
A three-PR TensorDict stack co-authored by Claude Fable 5 rewrites the multithreaded `consolidate` path instead of treating thread count as a simple toggle. The [first PR](https://github.com/pytorch/tensordict/pull/1740) chunks leaves by byte size and uses fused `torch.cat`, reporting an 8×128MB in-memory case moving from 218 ms sequential and 202 ms old-threaded to 20 ms with eight threads, while falling back to sequential file-backed writes when concurrency hurts. The follow-ups [reuse shared thread pools](https://github.com/pytorch/tensordict/pull/1741) to avoid about 0.3 ms of spawn overhead per call and [enable `return_early=True`](https://github.com/pytorch/tensordict/pull/1742) for threaded consolidation except where main-thread buffer or device-sync work is still required.

### [TorchRL parallelizes CPU tests and repairs xdist-only failures](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3969) — vmoens / PyTorch TorchRL
TorchRL has a five-PR Claude Fable 5 stack around CI reliability and parallel test execution. The lower PRs pin a [fresh-interpreter pickle failure](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3969) in `implement_for` wrappers and a [mock-environment key bug](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3970) that only appeared when pytest-xdist scheduled pristine workers; the middle PRs [trim Linux container setup](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3971) and [make coverage collect xdist workers](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3972), changing a local two-worker check from one coverage file and 105 traced lines to seven files and a full 66k-line report. The top [parallelization PR](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3973) sends roughly 24,000 tests through xdist and reports CPU job script times dropping from 73:05 to 48:34 on Python 3.10, 71:56 to 47:57 on 3.11, and 44:37 to 29:06 on 3.14, with the serial quarantine now the next bottleneck.

### [Mastra agents learn to wake from watched Slack threads](https://github.com/mastra-ai/mastra/pull/19079) — CalebBarnes / Mastra
Mastra’s SlackSignals PR is co-authored by Mastra Code using `anthropic/claude-fable-5` and adds a user-token polling signal provider for agent threads. Agents can subscribe to Slack threads, channels, or DMs through `slack_subscribe_thread`, persist cursors and subscriptions on thread metadata, skip the user’s own messages, and wake on new messages without bot invites, webhooks, tunnels, or a public endpoint. The PR reports 16 provider tests for subscribe, poll, cursor advancement, self-message skipping, unsubscribe, and restart behavior, plus 105 package tests, typecheck, and prettier green; live end-to-end Slack verification remains unchecked.

### [PostHog turns a Duckgres audit into failure guards](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/69025) — EDsCODE / PostHog
PostHog has a three-PR Fable 5-assisted stack from an in-session audit of its Duckgres consumer. The lower [backfill PR](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/68785) stops one wedged schema from pinning pageable backlog gauges forever by recording failure streaks, adding capped retry backoff, and splitting durable stuck gauges from healthy blocked work. A second [concurrency PR](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/68808) adds per-organization sink budgets; an adversarial Fable audit found that the first query applied the budget filter after `LIMIT`, letting one saturated org fill the candidate window, and the follow-up commit fixed it with a regression test. The headline divergence PR makes seven permanent apply errors fail fast, flips live incremental schemas to `NEEDS_RESYNC` instead of applying future batches over a gap, and reports local consumer/backfill tests plus Trunk runs showing 4,573, 15,449, and 1,950 tests green across the stack.

### [A crypto strategy adds a backtested drawdown governor](https://github.com/zhaow-de/zcrypto-kraken/pull/77) — zhaow-de / zcrypto-kraken
zcrypto-kraken merged a Claude Fable 5 co-authored risk layer that turns a master-plan drawdown-governance ladder into tested library code and a pre-registered threshold backtest. The new `drawdown_governor` is a pure returns overlay with governed-equity feedback, inclusive drawdown rungs, a renewable daily-loss cooldown, terminal stand-down behavior, and 34 tests covering hand-computed traces, no-look-ahead perturbation, cooldown boundaries, terminal re-arm, validation, and degenerate cases. On the frozen B3+vt-dynamic candidate, the author reports governed Sharpe 1.3300 full-period and 1.3649 for `k≥230`, versus 1.2455 and 1.2781 ungoverned, while max drawdown falls from 25.53% to 14.49% and all 14 one-at-a-time ±25% sensitivity variants stay at or above the ungoverned Sharpe.

### [An AWS demo stack is built around hand-written IAM](https://github.com/dcltdw/annotated-maps-sp/pull/47) — dcltdw / annotated-maps-sp
annotated-maps-sp merged a Milestone 3 infrastructure PR whose brainstorming, spec, and plans were done with Claude Fable 5 before subagent-driven implementation and review. The branch adds Terraform bootstrap and demo stacks for an ephemeral EKS environment, keeps IAM hand-written rather than delegated to modules, uses Identity Center SSO for the laptop, GitHub OIDC for CI, and IRSA only for the AWS load balancer controller, and makes teardown wait for ALB deletion to avoid orphaned resources. A final review caught that public-repo `pull_request` OIDC would also be assumable by fork PRs, so the CI role was pinned to push-to-main; the PR reports static gates green for Terraform fmt/validate, tflint, shellcheck, Helm lint, 21 Helm unit tests, kubeconform on default/prod/demo values, and existing backend/frontend checks.

### [Texera removes a missing PVE gateway rewrite](https://github.com/apache/texera/pull/6130) — xuang7 / Apache Texera
Apache Texera has a Claude Code PR generated with Claude Opus 4.8 and Claude Fable 5 for a Python virtual-environment routing bug in the single-node stack. The frontend asked for `/pve/...`, while the Jersey resource lived under `/api/pve/...`, so the single-node NGINX gateway fell through to the dashboard service and produced a “Could not load Python virtual environments” popup whenever a Python UDF operator was selected. After review, the branch removed the rewrite mismatch instead of adding another gateway special case: the frontend now calls `AppSettings.getApiEndpoint()` directly, the single-node, dev-proxy, and Kubernetes routes all serve `/api/pve`, and the author reports five Angular specs, a live `GET /api/pve/pves?cuid=1` returning 200 JSON on the single-node Docker stack, and a Helm render check with the rewrite filter removed.

### [Slicer-Liver replaces a segmentation stub with real TotalSegmentator runs](https://github.com/ALive-research/Slicer-Liver/pull/576) — RafaelPalomar / ALive Research
ALive Research’s Slicer-Liver work used Claude Fable 5 across a three-PR Stage-2 redesign: the [ADR](https://github.com/ALive-research/Slicer-Liver/pull/575) collapses separate structure tabs into one anatomy-segments table with per-row review states, while the headline PR wires real TotalSegmentator inference behind the Run seam. The implementation exports the Slicer volume to NIfTI, runs TotalSegmentator out of process, imports per-label outputs back as tagged scratch segments, streams backend progress into the card, and adds target mappings for liver, portal vein, hepatic vein, and mass. Seven red-to-green tests cover synthetic output import, command shaping, declined-install handling, busy-state painting, and cursor restoration; a small [docs-lint fix](https://github.com/ALive-research/Slicer-Liver/pull/577) then pinned Lychee on Ubuntu 24.04 after every PR started failing before link checks could run.

### [U2 gate warmup emits sector previews with a five-agent review loop](https://github.com/komleff/u2/pull/547) — komleff / U2
U2’s follow-up Gates-2 PR uses Claude Fable 5 on the server side of warmed jump gates, emitting a `SectorPreview` message with the neighboring room’s config as a gate enters the warmup state. The branch adds a single snapshot builder wrapper, dispatch injection through the message processor, idempotent preview emission on Cold→Warming and Warming→Ready transitions, reliable control-queue delivery, and guard tests around repeated previews and route state. The public thread is unusually explicit about review: an internal Fable pass with five independent subagents approved the architecture/security/quality aspects but found two fix-now issues, and the author reports 1,313 NUnit tests plus an escalation over a 1,383-byte preview exceeding the original MTU target.

### [A Keycloak SMS plugin gets PCI hardening and runtime proof](https://github.com/Modusmundi/keycloak-mfa-plugins/pull/5) — Modusmundi
Modusmundi’s merged Keycloak MFA PR uses Claude Fable 5 on a PCI-oriented SMS OTP hardening pass. Phone numbers in server logs are now masked to last four digits by default, non-HTTPS SMS gateway URLs are refused unless explicitly disabled, and a Trivy SCA workflow gates critical findings while producing SARIF and a CycloneDX SBOM. The PR reports 27 unit tests passing, including 16 new cases for masking, HTTPS predicates, invalid codes, and TTL expiry, plus a local Docker runtime check in the `fwc-kc` realm showing logs changing from `+120****7890` to `••••••7890` with Twilio simulation enabled.

### [kaaroViewer builds a verifiable video-agent pipeline](https://github.com/karx/kaaroViewer/pull/62) — karx
kaaroViewer’s Fable co-authored video-agent PR goes from discovery notes to a concrete media toolchain. The branch adds ffmpeg-backed probe/trim/concat/transcode/audio-mix operations, a timeline schema and compiler, a headless Chromium render harness, deliverable verifiers, and a `kaaro-vid` CLI, with 21 tests including a full end-to-end render. Later commits add story-beat video generation, session traces, a Claude Code skill for the probe-plan-execute-verify loop, golden-frame regression checks, contact sheets, and a 343.4-second 720p sample render with 38 voiceover chunks, 11 crossfades, and six of six verifiers passing.

### [Gota turns personal-finance signals into deterministic affordability checks](https://github.com/fortiz1422/Gota/pull/45) — fortiz1422 / Gota
Gota’s Spanish-language finance app used Claude Fable 5 for a 21-file intelligence slice around extraordinary expenses, goals, recurring income, installments, and safe-to-spend projections. The commits add month snapshots that exclude marked extraordinary spending from baselines, future-installment horizons, goal pace and missing-income facts, deterministic affordability simulation for cash or installment purchases, and mobile home-surface summaries. Review-driven fixes catch two double-counting mistakes where credit-card debt had already been netted out of the available balance, and the branch reports 57 new tests over projections, rules, planner intents, evidence, and hero rendering.

### [A DuckDB endpoint bug exposes fixture-only test coverage](https://github.com/Oltan/java-test-reports/pull/4) — Oltan
java-test-reports used Claude Fable 5 to fix three scenario-history endpoints that returned HTTP 500 against a real on-disk DuckDB database. The handlers opened the connection read-only and then called `init_schema`, whose `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` statements DuckDB rejects; the existing unit suite missed it because fixtures monkeypatched the connection to in-memory databases. The patch switches those paths to writable connections like the rest of the schema callers and adds regression tests that hit the endpoints through the real `db.get_connection` against a temporary database file; later commits in the same PR add browserless Gherkin demo scenarios, Jira matching fixes, and LLM-backed repair-loop infrastructure.

### [BoligData replaces mock housing crawlers with real Danish listing APIs](https://github.com/scottlinddk/BoligData/pull/5) — scottlinddk / BoligData
A merged Claude Fable 5 co-authored PR turns BoligData’s mock-only scrapers into real Boliga and Boligsiden JSON clients without adding a headless browser or new dependencies. The branch adds timeout, retry, `Retry-After`, jitter, JSON-line logging, pagination caps, per-record defensive mapping, in-source dedupe, content hashing, chunked upserts, and `Promise.allSettled` isolation so one failed source does not hide the other. It also hardens read APIs and frontend error states, with mapper tests for malformed and out-of-bounds records plus ingest orchestration tests against a stubbed Supabase client.

### [Reaper gives its audit log a durable sequence key](https://github.com/pgste/reaper/pull/18) — pgste / reaper
Reaper’s Claude Fable 5 co-authored audit PR moves the global decision sequence number out of an in-memory ring tuple and into the durable NDJSON and ClickHouse records themselves. That gives the audit stream an exact ordering key and the position needed for the following tamper-evident hash-chain work; a sequence gap now signals a dropped or deleted record. The PR adds tests for unique contiguous sequence assignment, descending `get_recent` ordering, and NDJSON round trips, and its CI report shows 1,121 tests passing across unit, integration, BDD, volume, memory, eBPF, and scale suites.

### [A PHP CMS closes SVG XXE and SMTP header-injection holes](https://github.com/artstudiouzb-gif/artstudio/pull/3) — artstudiouzb-gif / artstudio
A Russian-language artstudio PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 starts with two concrete security fixes in a PHP CMS. `Uploader::sanitizeSvgString` now strips `DOCTYPE` and stops expanding external entities, closing local-file-read XXE and billion-laughs DoS routes through uploaded SVGs, while `Mailer::send` rejects recipient addresses or names containing CR, LF, or NUL to guard against SMTP command and header injection. The branch adds tests for those cases, then continues into a much larger admin and public-site redesign with government-style page modules, news templates, typography assets, and cache/performance controls.

### [HeisenMICR turns a font spec into reproducible tooling](https://github.com/heisencoder/heisencoder.github.io/pull/2) — heisencoder
In the HeisenMICR PR, Matt Ball says he wrote the `HeisenMICR.md` font spec and Claude Fable 5 created the remaining implementation files. The branch checks in the generated `HeisenMICR.ttf` alongside Python build, preview, specimen, and spec modules, a `uv` project, a specimen image, and font tests, making the typeface reproducible instead of only a static asset. Later human commits tweak the diagonals and font output, which gives the thread a clear split between the Fable-generated toolchain and manual design adjustments.

### [Lunima removes manual Python-env names and restores user PDKs on restart](https://github.com/aignermax/Lunima/pull/714) — aignermax / Lunima
Two Lunima PRs use Claude Fable 5 as an automated reviewer on adjacent Python-environment and PDK workflow fixes. The environment PR removes the manual name field and generates collision-safe names such as `py3.11` from the selected version, with tests for suffixing, invalid versions, and registry collisions; the review confirms legacy environments are untouched and leaves only an orphan-directory nit. The follow-up [startup-restore PR](https://github.com/aignermax/Lunima/pull/715) wires saved user PDK paths back through the normal loader on app initialization, prunes missing files, tolerates editable PDKs without Nazca offsets, and reports new tests for recorded, missing, corrupt, and offset-less PDK files.

### [Hydra keeps catalogue metadata failures out of the global error overlay](https://github.com/GameOneDev/hydra/pull/9) — GameOneDev / hydra
Hydra’s Claude Fable 5 co-authored PR fixes a catalogue page failure mode where four external metadata requests for Steam tags, genres, publishers, and developers had no rejection handler. If the resources host was unreachable or unconfigured, those promise rejections surfaced as global error-overlay cards; `/catalogue/search` had a similar catch-less path through `try/finally`. The patch logs metadata failures and keeps the page usable with empty filter lists, lets failed searches settle into an empty-result state, and adds a follow-up fix so API error logging itself does not crash when an axios error lacks `config`.

### [A virtual-gallery walkthrough finds and fixes a cubemap pole defect](https://github.com/mrcryptooo/virtual-gallery/pull/5) — mrcryptooo / virtual-gallery
virtual-gallery used Claude Fable 5 on an end-to-end production integration test for three real test panoramas, spanning the pipeline, packaged assets, a PSV cubemap-tile engine, DOM hotspots, wayfinding chrome, and the app tour surface. The PR reports 102 tests green across engine, app, and pipeline code, plus desktop and mobile browser verification with zero console errors or failed requests. The most useful field detail is a real defect found during the walkthrough: a naive cubemap net tore at the nadir, so the branch derived the pole-face orientation from Photo Sphere Viewer’s own coordinate code, bumped the pipeline salt, and reverified the straight-down view as pixel-clean.

### [Photonic waveguide routing fixes get a Fable review pass](https://github.com/aignermax/Lunima/pull/717) — aignermax / Lunima
Lunima’s routing PR fixes three concrete photonics-canvas defects: crossings that survived after components moved, A* paths that could self-loop at the start pin, and terminal approaches that cut through neighboring waveguides. The implementation dissolves stale crossing records before rerouting, adds a self-intersection guard to A*, validates smoothed terminal segments against the grid, and marks geometry invalid rather than silently accepting overlap. An automated review using `claude-fable-5` approved the architecture, security, and quality of the fix set, while leaving only narrow nits about stricter staleness validation and test assertions.

### [An enterprise-architecture tool grows signed extension bundles and licensing](https://github.com/vincentmakes/turbo-ea/pull/796) — vincentmakes / turbo-ea
turbo-ea has a large Claude Fable 5 co-authored extension-store branch built around offline-verifiable Ed25519 `.teax` bundles and signed license files. The work adds an admin Extensions page, entitlement checks, content-pack application through the existing workspace-transfer engine, a backend plugin runtime, same-origin UI bundle loading, CLI signing and verification tools, multi-key trust anchors, static catalogue installs, renewal flows, and extension-gated metamodel capabilities. The PR is still open and iterative, but the commit trail is unusually specific about product/security tradeoffs, including removing private commercial runbooks from the public repo, fixing a root-owned Docker volume that broke all uploads, and making content-pack disable/uninstall visibly hide its declared card types without deleting data.

### [Argus adds a chronicle view for scheduled runs, agents, and sessions](https://github.com/Jobaben/Argus/pull/12) — Jobaben / Argus
Argus used Claude Fable 5 to build a Chronicle tab that merges scheduler runs, background agents, and transcript sessions into one timeline endpoint and UI. The API packs spans into swimlanes by schedule, project, and shared-agent lane, reports window totals such as in-flight work, failures, and run spend, and the frontend renders status-colored bars that deep-link back to the relevant view. The same PR adds a reusable segmented control, live polling for resources whose state changes with time, and design-system cards synced from the shared Claude design project; a follow-up commit fixed formatting drift after a Prettier upgrade.

### [Desktop analytics events survive offline runs and consent purges](https://github.com/sillsdev/DesktopAnalytics.net/pull/43) — johnml1135 / SIL DesktopAnalytics.net
DesktopAnalytics.net has a Fable 5-reviewed durability rewrite for its Mixpanel client, replacing immediate best-effort sends with a bounded DiskQueue spool, retry loop, circuit breaker, `$insert_id` dedupe, path scrubbing before disk persistence, and explicit consent purging. The PR reports firewall and Windows Sandbox smoke tests against the live Mixpanel endpoint: blocked runs queued events instead of dropping them, and the next unblocked run delivered both old and new events. Review follow-up found and fixed captive-portal redirects being misclassified as delivery, black-hole shutdown hangs, a circuit breaker that could never open, Mixpanel `200`/`0` rejected payloads, and purge bytes remaining on disk; the final local suite is listed as 81/81 passing.

### [U2 gate windows show the neighboring sector before a jump](https://github.com/komleff/u2/pull/543) — komleff / U2
U2’s K3 gate-window PR uses Claude Fable 5 in the supervisor and review loop for a Unity feature that renders the neighboring sector’s skybox inside an open jump gate before the ship crosses it. The branch adds portal math equivalent to the server migration formula, a preview camera and render texture, a single active gate-window manager, a URP unlit shader, scene wiring, and EditMode tests for math, model state, mesh generation, selection, and boundary hysteresis. The public thread records compile-zero editor runs, 1,170 passing EditMode tests with two known failures, 56 K3 tests, standalone builds, live operator smoke tests, and fixes after smoke found premature window fade-out and low-resolution star rendering.

### [Lunima crossing insertion gets persistence, rollback, and undo safety](https://github.com/aignermax/Lunima/pull/718) — aignermax / Lunima
Lunima’s latest Fable 5-assisted photonic-routing PR fixes six lifecycle blockers before adaptive waveguide crossing insertion can become the default. The branch dissolves crossing records on every delete path, makes undo and redo resolve crossing sub-connections back to originals, persists `IsInsertedCrossing` markers in `.lun` files, rebuilds records after load, rolls back failed insertions structurally, and dissolves stale crossings when anchors move more than 1 µm. The author reports seven new lifecycle tests, three command-level undo/redo tests, and a full suite of 3,449 passing tests with 18 environment-dependent skips.

### [Elixir spec-domain checking turns into three stdlib fixes](https://github.com/elixir-lang/elixir/pull/15571) — lukaszsamson / Elixir
Elixir has a compact three-PR run assisted by Claude Fable 5 after re-running the type checker over stdlib bodies using spec-derived argument domains. The [first PR](https://github.com/elixir-lang/elixir/pull/15571) widens or narrows specs that made real clauses unreachable, including `Logger.Formatter.prune/1` and empty charlists for list parsing; the [second](https://github.com/elixir-lang/elixir/pull/15572) adds the missing `calendar:` key to `Calendar.time()` and shows a map that currently raises in `Time.to_string/1`; the [third](https://github.com/elixir-lang/elixir/pull/15573) normalizes chardata paths before `Mix.Release.make_boot_script/4` concatenates them. The middle PR reports a full stdlib recompile with no new type-checker warnings under the tighter `time()` type and passing calendar suites.

### [LLVM gets a target-independent runtime vector-shuffle intrinsic](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/208537) — Oscar Smith / LLVM
Oscar Smith’s LLVM PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 adds `llvm.dynamicshuffle`, a target-independent intrinsic for shuffling two vectors with a runtime mask rather than a constant `shufflevector` mask. The 3,249-line patch specifies poison for out-of-range selected lanes, then wires lowering for AArch64 `TBL`, x86 `PSHUFB`/`VPERMD`/`VPERMV3`, and RISC-V `vrgather` without target-specific fixup code. The thread is also an example of public AI-use disclosure in a core compiler project: LLVM’s bot asked for confirmation under the project AI policy, and the author replied that AI usage was noted in the PR description.

### [TorchRL chips away at CI wall time with measured shards and opt-ins](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3977) — vmoens / PyTorch TorchRL
TorchRL has a new six-PR Claude Fable 5 co-authored CI stack following the earlier CPU parallelization work. The stack runs expensive CPU quarantine shards only on the oldest and newest Python versions ([3977](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3977)), shards the 84-minute olddeps job after verifying an identical 29,597-test collection with no overlap ([3978](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3978)), contains hard optdeps hangs with thread timeouts and a 100-minute guard ([3979](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3979)), splits SOTA smoke tests into two GPU jobs with an identical 35-test union ([3980](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3980)), demotes the 71-minute Windows suite to nightly except releases and manual runs ([3981](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3981)), and makes benchmark jobs nightly or PR-label opt-in ([3982](https://github.com/pytorch/rl/pull/3982)). The PR bodies are unusually explicit about measured runtimes, collection-equivalence checks, and the tradeoff that some failures now implicate a day of commits rather than a single push.

### [A shallow-proxy library gets explicit watcher wakeups](https://github.com/fork-tongue/observ/pull/188) — Korijn / observ
observ’s Fable 5 co-authored PR adds `trigger_ref(proxy)`, modeled after Vue’s `triggerRef`, so deep mutations inside a shallow proxy can force-notify the proxy dependency and all live key dependencies. The implementation works across every proxy view of the same target because they share the dependency set, and a follow-up commit fixes the public type to accept `Proxy[T] | T` so correctly typed call sites are not rejected. The branch is small — five files and two Fable-attributed commits — but it is a clean example of the model translating a precise reactivity-library gap into API, implementation, and typing changes.

### [An SSH tool blocks destructive agent commands before pod execution](https://github.com/hesedcasa/ssh/pull/7) — allentcm / hesedcasa ssh
hesedcasa’s SSH CLI has a Fable 5 co-authored safety layer that checks `exec`, `artisan`, and `tinker` inputs before they reach a Kubernetes pod. The first pass refuses file/folder removal and destructive database operations through always-on guards rather than optional profile settings; a Greptile review then found bypasses through shell wrappers, keywords, grouping syntax, and narrower SQL patterns. The follow-up Fable-attributed commit handles `bash -c`-style wrappers, subshell and brace groups, and a broader destructive-DDL family, turning the review into concrete parser hardening instead of a policy note.

### [A MySQL profile gets queued concurrency instead of runaway load](https://github.com/hesedcasa/mysql/pull/71) — allentcm / hesedcasa mysql
A second hesedcasa PR co-authored by Claude Fable 5 adds per-profile query semaphores to the MySQL tool so a busy profile waits at a configurable `maxConcurrentQueries` limit instead of piling more work onto the database. Later commits make the cap configurable in `mysql-config.json`, clamp invalid values to avoid deadlocking the queue, and reject pending waiters when `closeAll()` runs so shutdown does not leave callers hanging. The review summary lists tests for queuing, release, shutdown, profile defaults, and per-profile isolation, with no blocking issues found.

### [A browser-design app clears a cluster of interaction bugs](https://github.com/assentorp/design-in-the-browser/pull/29) — assentorp / design-in-the-browser
design-in-the-browser’s Fable 5 branch starts with a shortcut bug where Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Enter was swallowed by the send handler before it could add an annotation to a list. The same session then fixes selector generation using `:nth-of-type`, token mentions that lost their leading marker, Shift+G accidentally invoking ruler guides, VS Code launch paths with spaces, stale IPC handlers after reopening a macOS window, WSL screenshot paths in prompts, duplicate IPC listeners on tab switches, a toolbar Stop button that reloaded instead of stopping, and an over-broad terminal-close shortcut. The PR spans nine Fable-attributed commits across 229 additions and 143 deletions, making it more like a concentrated QA sweep than a single typo fix.

### [Squid-N adds three-dimensional steel-section yield surfaces](https://github.com/hrntsm/Squid-N/pull/33) — hrntsm / Squid-N
Squid-N’s Fable 5 co-authored PR adds a module for N-My-Mz interaction surfaces used in member yield checks. It computes separate surfaces for simple yield springs, multispring models, and multifiber models, builds all-plastic stress-distribution support points, and checks slices against the exact rectangular-steel-section relation `M/Mp = 1 - (N/Npl)^2`. Follow-up commits add a 3D comparison view in the design tab, cache recomputation by section and strength, replace the uncoupled simple-spring box with a coupled double-cone relation, and update `crossbeam-epoch` after CI dependency checks hit RUSTSEC-2026-0204.

### [OpenRadioss gets a local MCP code-index server](https://github.com/laurent-altr/OpenRadioss/pull/162) — laurent-altr / OpenRadioss
OpenRadioss has a Fable 5 co-authored single-file Python MCP server that indexes Fortran, C, C++, header, and include sources into a gitignored SQLite database for Claude Code. The tool exposes symbol lookup, file outlines, callers and callees from CI-maintained headers plus line scans, exact reference search, FTS5 code search with fallback, status checks, and incremental reindexing. The PR reports a full source-tree build of roughly 6,750 files, 14,500 symbols, and 168,000 call-graph edges in about 50 seconds, with PEP 723 metadata and a committed `.mcp.json` for automatic registration.

### [Bracket tournaments can stop multi-set matches at majority win](https://github.com/pspeter/bracket/pull/273) — pspeter / bracket
bracket’s Fable 5 co-authored PR adds a `play_all_sets` ranking option for best-of-n play. When it is off, a multi-set match becomes complete as soon as one side wins the floor-half-plus-one majority and no set is in progress, while existing reconciliation still updates standings, elimination advancement, and completion timestamps. The implementation preserves recorded surplus sets for statistics, reopens matches if scores are edited away from the majority, refuses to start a set on an already decided match, regenerates OpenAPI/frontend clients, and adds English and German UI strings for the new checkbox.

## 2026-07-10

### [Mitos cuts Python sandbox startup latency by reusing TLS connections](https://github.com/mitos-run/mitos/pull/877) — stubbi / Mitos
Mitos used Claude Fable 5 with extended thinking on a Python SDK latency pass for its Firecracker-backed sandbox service. The PR starts from a measured time-to-interactive path: warm engine activation was already about 60 ms P50, but create-to-first-exec from a same-region client was 572 ms P50 because each sandbox creation paid two fresh TLS handshakes of roughly 95 ms apiece. The Fable-authored fix pools keep-alive `httpx.Client` instances, keeps them alive across agent thinking pauses, stops closing them after every `exec`, adds a reproducible `bench/tti-latency.py` harness, and reports a 197 ms P50 improvement before later transport follow-ups.

### [TSwift lifts Foundation coverage by ten points after Fable triage](https://github.com/siuying/tswift/pull/259) — siuying / TSwift
TSwift used Claude Fable 5 as an advisor to triage Foundation gaps into implement-now and documented-Darwin-gap buckets before a seven-slice implementation pass. The result raises implemented Foundation coverage from 59.8% to 70.3% and verified coverage from 54.6% to 65.0%, with concrete additions such as all 58 `URLError.Code` cases, stored `URLRequest` policy properties, nonconforming-float coder strategies, `Calendar` weekend helpers, and `DateComponents.isRepeatedDay`. The PR notes that independent review caught a real interpreter `type_hint` push/pop leak during the URLRequest slice, and `scripts/presubmit` ran green before every commit.

### [MammamiRadio fixes silent OpenAI fallbacks from Fable thinking blocks](https://github.com/florianhorner/mammamiradio/pull/806) — florianhorner / MammamiRadio
MammamiRadio found that reasoning-model responses from `claude-fable-5` broke its Anthropic scriptwriter parser because the code assumed `content[0].text`; thinking blocks raised `AttributeError`, and every creative call silently regenerated through the OpenAI fallback. A Fable co-authored fix joins text blocks past the reasoning content, preserves max-token truncation handling, and adds a guarded model-catalog key that no shipped profile can resolve accidentally. The proof artifacts record before-and-after local ledger rows — `anthropic_AttributeError` fallbacks before, clean Fable generation after — plus 4,376 passing tests, 93% coverage, ruff/format/mypy, and changelog lint.

### [miniextendr fixes vector fields that could not compile through TypedList](https://github.com/A2-ai/miniextendr/pull/1227) — CGMossa / A2-ai
A miniextendr dogfood hiccup turned into a Claude Fable 5-drafted API fix: `TypedList::get::<Vec<_>>()` could not compile because `get` and `get_opt` required `TryFromSexp<Error = SexpError>`, while vector conversions use the narrower `SexpTypeError`. The PR widens the bound for `TypedList` and the sibling `DataFrame::column` accessors, adds an R fixture that retrieves numeric, integer, and character vectors through the exact failing call shape, and updates docs and generated R files. Its verification list includes multiple all-feature `cargo clippy -D warnings` runs, `just check`, R 4.6 package install/documentation passes, and a focused testthat run with 31 passes.

### [Docling stops duplicating authors when PDF text bands overlap](https://github.com/instill-ai/models/pull/112) — pinglin / Instill AI
Instill AI’s Docling service has a small but concrete Claude Fable 5 co-authored PDF parsing fix. On arXiv paper 2411.14199, overlapping author-flow bounding boxes caused the same physical line to be consumed by two candidate bands, duplicating names such as “Pang Wei Koh” and “Hannaneh Hajishirzi” with identical text and boxes. The patch assigns each physical line to the containing band with the tightest fit, adds a regression with overlapping flows sharing a row, and reports the real PDF changing from 26 created author nodes with three duplicates to 23 unique authors.

### [Drop-tracker audit reduces a Discord data scare to six corrections](https://github.com/shearmanb/drop-tracker/pull/5) — shearmanb / drop-tracker
drop-tracker used Claude Fable 5 on a live audit of 783 Discord-sourced drop rows after suspected timestamp problems. The PR says the core data held up: the hour histogram matched store hours, 601 of 633 Discord snowflake times corroborated links within an hour, and the issue narrowed to one shifted cell plus a weekday-numbering convention split. The same pass found four wrong-era links, four shared-link pairs, one exact duplicate, one corrupted URL, 319 double-logged rows, and 30 `NO DROP` strings in the link column, then added a Pages-served tap-through checklist and follow-up correction/delete artifacts.

### [World Cup predictor fixes empty scouting reports from thinking blocks](https://github.com/ZhihaoZhang97/wcpredict/pull/13) — ZhihaoZhang97 / wcpredict
wcpredict found that every `claude-fable-5` trace had empty scout briefings because LangChain streamed Fable’s thinking output as mixed content: thinking dictionaries plus the answer as bare strings. The old extractor only kept dictionary text blocks, so predictions silently ran on tournament data alone; a minimal live API call reproduced the Fable-only failure. Switching to `AIMessage.text` restored 8.6k- and 10.6k-character briefings in an end-to-end Argentina–Egypt backtest, including briefing-only facts such as Egypt’s left-back injuries and Salah’s hamstring, and the regenerated prediction matched the actual 3–2 result.

### [Texera bulk upload stops rerendering eleven hundred rows per tick](https://github.com/apache/texera/pull/6306) — kunwp1 / Apache Texera
Apache Texera’s Fable-generated frontend PR addresses a concrete performance complaint: bulk-uploading about 1,100 dataset files took roughly 12 minutes because Pending and Finished lists fully rerendered on each change-detection pass while uploads shared the same thread. The fix replaces a getter with a cached field updated only when the queue changes, virtualizes both lists with `cdk-virtual-scroll`, and adds `trackBy` so the DOM tracks only visible rows. The PR includes a screen recording of a successful 1,100-file upload after the change, with Codecov showing the patch exercised in frontend tests.

### [A transcriber learns resumable chunk retries and cleanup guardrails](https://github.com/ferynd/Website/pull/151) — ferynd / Website
Ferynd’s transcriber PR, co-authored by Claude Fable 5, makes long audio processing less all-or-nothing. OpenAI transcription chunks now run three at a time, each successful chunk is cached before failures surface, Gemini windows and cleanup chunks resume from saved work, and strict cleanup stops scheduling new chunks after the first failure while allowing in-flight work to finish. The audit pass also adds Gemini response schemas, a preservation-first correction prompt, one automatic re-ask on invalid output, and a length-divergence guardrail that reverts paraphrased or hallucinated segment rewrites; the PR reports 1,190 tests, lint, and build passing.

### [Nearmiss gives contributors export, delete, and retention tools](https://github.com/ChelseaKR/nearmiss/pull/29) — ChelseaKR / nearmiss
Nearmiss used Claude Fable 5 on contributor data-rights tooling for pseudonymous safety reports. A contributor holding their `reporter_token` can now export matching reports, delete them from raw intake, pending moderation, and the approved store, and leave SHA-256 tombstones so later imports or resubmissions do not resurrect deleted IDs; a retention purge removes stale raw records while keeping unparseable timestamps fail-safe. The PR adds CLI commands plus tests for submit→approve→publish→delete→republish, tombstone blocking, retention purging, and CLI coverage, with the author reporting 93% total coverage, ruff, and strict mypy clean; CI was blocked only by a missing DCO signoff on the single commit.

### [Chidori removes O(history²) costs from agent safepoints](https://github.com/ThousandBirdsInc/chidori/pull/99) — kvey / Thousand Birds
Chidori’s Claude Fable 5 co-authored runtime PR attacks several compounding costs in its durable agent scaffold. The first commit stops rewriting `checkpoint.json` and rereading module graphs twice per host call, taking a 300-call debug run from 7.9 seconds to 4.0 seconds while preserving byte-identical artifacts; later commits replace full host-promise table rewrites with per-operation blobs, index replay logs by sequence, pipeline HTTP/S3 mirror writes, and make signal actors wait inline instead of replaying their entire history for each message. The PR reports concrete measurements such as `chidori resume` on a 600-record run falling from 690 ms to 128 ms, a 50-call mirrored run falling from 23.5 seconds to 3.3 seconds, and actor ping-pong at 120 messages going from 2.8 seconds to 177 ms, with the full Chidori suite growing from 383 to 390 passing tests across the stack.

### [Midgard benchmarks its chart module against AG Charts and Highcharts](https://github.com/charliebeckstrand/midgard/pull/946) — charliebeckstrand / Midgard
Midgard used Claude Fable 5 to add a browser-mode competitive benchmark suite for its chart module. The Playwright-backed harness runs real Chromium tests for mount, live-data update, and pointer-sweep hover scenarios across line, bar, and scatter charts, with dashboard-to-heavy data sizes and animations disabled for all contenders. The checked-in README records the first baseline: Midgard is competitive at smaller sizes, but its runtime grows near-linearly with row count while AG Charts and Highcharts stay flatter, giving the optimization work concrete targets instead of anecdotal UI impressions.

### [QuantEcon.py makes DiscreteDP instances picklable](https://github.com/QuantEcon/QuantEcon.py/pull/859) — oyamad / QuantEcon
QuantEcon.py has a small Fable-generated maintenance PR with a clear failure mode: `DiscreteDP.__init__` attached a local `s_wise_max` closure to each instance, so pickling failed with `AttributeError: Can’t get local object ... s_wise_max`, blocking multiprocessing and cache workflows. The patch replaces the two closures with one ordinary method that dispatches on `self._sa_pair`, keeping the public call shape unchanged while making the remaining instance state picklable. A new round-trip test covers all three representations — product, state-action sparse, and state-action dense — and Coveralls reports a small coverage increase on the branch.

### [LibreBooking stops an unbounded date loop from freezing reservations](https://github.com/LibreBooking/librebooking/pull/1534) — JohnVillalovos / LibreBooking
LibreBooking’s Fable-assisted reservation fix starts from a reproduced browser freeze: when a selected day had no reservable periods, `PopulatePeriodDropDown` advanced one day at a time and recursively triggered itself, then settled into a pure CPU spin after weekday-keyed cache entries filled. The one-file patch bounds the skip loop to seven days or the picker’s `maxDate`, jumps directly to the schedule’s `minDate` when appropriate, bails on invalid date fields, fixes a jQuery `.data()` misuse that broke the unchanged-date guard, parses YMD dates in local time, and treats layout-fetch failures as empty days. The author says they reproduced issue #1512 before the patch and that the hang stopped afterward.

### [Apache Fluss fixes a primary-key hash contract bug with a public reproducer](https://github.com/apache/fluss/pull/3629) — NestDream / Apache Fluss
Apache Fluss has a Fable-assisted fix for a value-semantics bug in `Schema.PrimaryKey`: `equals()` compared only column names, while `hashCode()` folded in the object identity hash from `super.hashCode()`. That meant two schemas could be equal after a JSON round trip but unusable as equivalent keys in a `HashSet` or `HashMap`; the PR links a standalone reproducer against the released 0.9.1-incubating artifact. The patch hashes only the compared column names and adds both a unit round-trip test and an end-to-end `Admin.createTable`/`getTableSchema` test, with `mvn verify -pl fluss-common` reporting 1,684 tests and no failures.

### [Data-Analyze-MCP turns a chart redesign into a verified MCP demo flow](https://github.com/chaeminyoon/Data-Analyze-MCP/pull/5) — chaeminyoon / Data-Analyze-MCP
Data-Analyze-MCP’s merged Claude Fable 5 PR adds a chart theme system and then uses it to rebuild the project’s own demo around a real MCP analysis session. The package now has five chart themes, `set_chart_style` and `list_chart_styles` tools that raise the MCP tool count from 38 to 40, and all plotting paths route through the active theme instead of hardcoded matplotlib/seaborn/Plotly defaults. The README demo uses server-returned values from an outlier-cleaning run — 34 outliers detected, `remove_outliers` reducing 545 rows to 511, and a measured area-price correlation moving from 0.33 to 0.80 — while verification covered stdio tool serving, theme switching, dark-chart rendering, bad-theme errors, zero README emoji, and removal of Ollama references.

### [Apple II emulator adds real aux-card variants](https://github.com/st3fan/ewm/pull/244) — st3fan / ewm
The ewm Apple II emulator used Claude Fable 5 to replace a hardwired //e auxiliary-memory model with a swappable `AuxCard` trait covering the Apple 1K 80-column card, the Extended 64K card, and RamWorks III cards up to 8 MB. The PR is specific about the hardware subtlety Fable researched: `$C073` bank switching affects RAMRD/RAMWRT, ALTZP, and aux language-card memory, while 80STORE video pages stay pinned to bank 0 and unpopulated banks float as `0xFF`. Verification includes nine bus-driven tests, a RamWorks ROM self-test reporting “System OK,” byte-identical default golden outputs, `fmt`, `clippy -D warnings`, and a full `cargo test`; the author also posted Fable usage metrics of about 73.5 million total tokens and $83.88 for the session.

### [LAMMPS triages 1,809 Coverity reports into bug fixes and tests](https://github.com/lammps/lammps/pull/5072) — Axel Kohlmeyer / LAMMPS
LAMMPS has a three-PR Claude Fable 5 triage of all 1,809 outstanding reports in a new Coverity Scan project: [bug fixes](https://github.com/lammps/lammps/pull/5072), [hardening](https://github.com/lammps/lammps/pull/5073), and [regression tests](https://github.com/lammps/lammps/pull/5074).
