The repair runs that self-PTY path headlessly outside Claude Code, while a genuinely deferred in-Claude-Code Fable seat now carries its requested model, effort, lens, and artifact reference into JSON output rather than disappearing behind an empty unavailable result. The author reports 2,409 passing tests and 634 documented skips; the public Python, check, secret-scan, clean-room, and bundle checks passed, while a separate documentation-freshness audit failed.

### [A subprocess pipe stops jamming after its first write](https://github.com/saghul/txiki.js/pull/1028) — tarwin
A Claude Fable 5-authored txiki.js repair traces a stalled `stdin: 'pipe'` stream to a native write contract mismatch: the JavaScript sink treated the boolean `true` from a synchronous `uv_try_write` as an asynchronous result and waited for an `onwrite` callback that never arrives. The new regression writes and awaits three chunks to a child process, which reproduces the old hang on the second write and verifies all three are echoed after the one-condition fix. The author says they reviewed the change repeatedly and that the full suite passed locally on macOS arm64; the PR remains open.

### [A game client moves body-layout data out of hard-coded UI paths](https://github.com/Kizunad/Bong/pull/1184) — Kizunad
Claude Fable 5 orchestrated a 40-file Bong migration in which the server supplies silhouette polygons, body-part anchors, meridian paths, and display mappings rather than the Java client carrying fixed humanoid geometry. The PR adds out-of-order and missing-layout cases plus a synthetic six-part non-human body, while a three-Opus adversarial review caught a 4–6-pixel Mini HUD wound-marker drift and led to a second anchor set; the human layout now pins all 16 parts at zero difference. Fable led and coordinated the work, Sonnet implemented it, and Opus reviewed it; the merged change reports 11,374-plus server tests, 848 schema tests, a successful Gradle build, and completed CI checks.

### [A bundle denylist stays after a 125 MiB removal experiment](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/69147) — rafaelaudibert
A PostHog engineer used Claude Fable 5 under human direction to audit whether the toolbar’s remaining build deny rules were obsolete after a modernization stack. Removing them re-inlined 125.58 MiB across 11,365 files; retaining the billing exclusion alone still pushed the eager bundle from 1.5 MB to 8.8 MB, so every deny remained. The PR instead ratchets the graph budget from 15.5 to 14.8 MB around a measured 13.80 MiB result, with build, graph, size, CSP, frontend-test, visual-regression, and Semgrep checks publicly passing.

### [Seventy-one clean macOS stress runs after a USB race fix](https://github.com/libusb/libusb/pull/1872) — Ihor Dutchak / libusb
Ihor Dutchak used Claude Code with Fable 5 to turn an intermittent macOS `stress_mt` hang into an auditable test: the new watchdog cuts a normally sub-15-minute job off at 40 minutes, captures `sample(1)` stacks, and prevents a silent six-hour timeout. The draft branch combines pending Darwin race and use-after-free repairs with that watchdog rather than proposing a direct merge. Fable’s follow-up verification reported 20 clean CI runs, then 50 more with full `stress_mt` logs inspected—71 consecutive clean runs on the combined code.

### [A live-coding lab joins patterns, patches, and a file-tool agent](https://github.com/fdb/research/pull/5) — Frederik De Bleser
Frederik De Bleser’s Fable 5 session produced Spektrum, a 28-file, 7,073-line explorable lab for a live-coding talk. It puts a Tidal-style functional pattern language, a serialized visual node patcher, and a small Claude-Code-like agent with list/read/write/run tools on the same Web Audio, GLSL, and local-storage substrate; the agent can also replay a session offline. The branch includes 40 headless-Chromium browser-test steps covering the basic, scene, and editor interactions, and its Cloudflare Pages preview deployed successfully.

### [Recipe photos become editable imports after client-side OCR](https://github.com/vormadal/Anything/pull/619) — Rúni Egholm Vørmadal
A Claude Fable 5 session added a scan-from-photo flow to the Anything recipe app: Danish and English OCR runs in the browser, the extracted lines land in an editable review screen, and a new API endpoint turns them into the existing import format. The work also found that the generated Kiota client could not serialize a browser `File` in multipart form data, so uploads were failing before a request; passing an `ArrayBuffer` with a filename repairs that path. The open PR adds unit, integration, visual-snapshot, and end-to-end coverage, with the public .NET build/test, Next.js lint/build, test, visual, and E2E checks passing.

### [A zero-concentration branch fabricates treatment-model outflow](https://github.com/gwtransport/gwtransport/issues/312) — bdestombe
A Fable 5 deep-math review found that GWTransport’s nonlinear-sorption front tracker treats a clean-water step with Freundlich `n < 1` as a contact wave when its physics calls for a rarefaction or shock. In the supplied `n=0.7` reproduction, the `c_min=0` branch differs from an independent Godunov finite-volume oracle by 10—the full inlet-concentration scale—and begins emitting a false `cout=2.8512` plateau from day one. The default public API happens to use `c_min=1e-12`, but the documented direct configuration reaches the faulty branch; the proposed repair removes the special case and adds mass and causality checks.

### [Book-view toggles keep stale scroll and highlight state](https://github.com/fsnow/cst/issues/321) — fsnow / CST
A Fable-led review of CST’s per-book display toggles found that hiding footnotes reflowed a document without rebuilding its absolute-position anchor cache, leaving chapter tracking and a later restored scroll position wrong. It also reproduced a timing race in which saved “highlights off” state was silently discarded when a JavaScript bridge injected after the toggle call. The linked merged repair rebuilds the cache while holding the reader’s viewport steady and persists a pending highlight-visibility value for later bridge initialization; the author reports a clean build and defers the combined UI smoke test.

### [A pricing audit finds silent paths and one unresolved boundary](https://github.com/jeroenk22/prijslijst-checker/issues/12) — jeroenk22
A Fable correctness audit of an already merged pricing pipeline found no confirmed incorrect field price in the current subset data, but it identified several ways future data could fail quietly. Unhandled price-bearing components can disappear from a quote, two GPS mechanisms can select one path without warning, and a product containing only an order-cost component can return a bare €35.10. The most consequential open question is whether exactly three hectares belongs in the €25.30 “1–3 ha” tier or the €31.70 “3–5 ha” tier; the parser currently chooses the latter and the maintainer is seeking the supplier’s interpretation before adding boundary tests.

### [A detached implementation process races an orchestrator’s verification](https://github.com/mar3co/fable-orchestrator/issues/4) — j-vshield
During one Fable 5-orchestrated Claude Code session against an Xcode project, the author ran about 15 Grok implementation lanes and recorded five failures to complete their report-and-verification contract. Three lanes made correct edits then exited before testing, while one detached process committed and pushed several minutes after its wrapper had surfaced the task as complete—while the orchestrator was already verifying the working tree. The failures appeared only on multi-step jobs and clustered late in the reused wrapper’s transcript, at reported cumulative contexts of roughly 204,000 to 546,000 tokens; the author now treats a task without a structured report as unsettled until process and Git-state checks are clean.

### [A sealed three-task Fable benchmark releases its raw trial record](https://github.com/BigBirdReturns/tier-bench/pull/74) — BigBirdReturns
Tier-bench sealed a Claude Fable 5 low-effort run on three almanac tasks—exception classification, record binding, and rule boundaries—with three passes in each of the nine trials. The repository includes every candidate, raw solver event stream, SHA-256 packet receipt, hidden-grader receipt, and token ledger; candidates were extracted mechanically from preserved Claude Code subagent transcripts and independently graded twice, yielding 18 deterministic grades. The authors also documented a provenance error from an earlier run—a coordinator-transcribed candidate rather than the raw bytes—and regraded the original response before sealing this one. It is not yet a cross-model result: the comparator deliberately returns “unpaired” until a matching Codex run is resealed.

### [A full Fable quota bucket stops blocking Haiku](https://github.com/andrewmaston1988/claude-plugin-marketplace/pull/176) — andrewmaston1988
A real Fable 5 allocation at 100% exposed a scheduler bug in Claude Plugin Marketplace: it treated the highest of all reported usage limits as account-wide exhaustion, even though the remaining session and weekly-all buckets were only 24% and 54%. The merged repair separates model-scoped limits from account-wide ones, so the Fable leaf remains blocked while a Haiku job with unscoped headroom can run. The author reports a red-to-green 350-test suite and a live endpoint check in which the formerly blocked Haiku dispatch completed; all three public CI jobs passed.

### [A volunteer scheduler blocks self-approval and repairs legacy data handling](https://github.com/HarpElle/volunteercalendar/pull/286) — HarpElle
A merged Claude Fable 5 co-authored hardening pass prevents a resource approver from approving or denying their own reservation request, while retaining an explicit admin/owner override. The work also came out of a production smoke: the People → Teams page had assumed structured prerequisites, but old production records contain string IDs; the compatibility layer now round-trips those records without coercing them. It further reopens affected reviews after role-changing edits, adds a scope-checked service-ministry write route, and reports 982 unit, 127 rules, and 366 integration tests alongside successful typecheck, lint, build, and CodeQL runs.

### [An AVX2 FFT kernel replaces a generic complex128 path](https://github.com/CWBudde/algo-fft/pull/14) — MeKo-Christian
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored change fills the missing size-4096 complex128 AVX2 kernel in algo-fft, where that input had fallen back to a generic six-step Go implementation. The new six-stage radix-4 assembly path is registered ahead of the generic kernels and measured about 36µs versus 61µs for the old selected path, a roughly 1.7x improvement on the author’s Xeon. Forward-versus-reference, round-trip, SIMD-path, race, vet, pure-Go, WASM, cross-architecture, fuzz, lint, and formatting checks were all reported or visibly green when the PR merged.

### [A tarball installer rejects unsafe names before extraction](https://github.com/wienerdog-ai/wienerdog/pull/90) — thegyula
Fable orchestrated a Claude Code implementation that hardens Wienerdog’s tarball install path against three operational failure modes. It lists archive members before extraction and rejects absolute or escaping `..` paths, writes verified bytes into a 0700 random temporary directory through an exclusive 0600 file, and requires a completion marker written before the final atomic rename so a partial install cannot masquerade as complete. The author explicitly leaves tar symlink and hardlink targets for a separate cross-`tar` investigation; the merged patch’s macOS and Ubuntu tests, lint, and boundary checks passed.

### [PostHog stops treating routine toolbar failures as product errors](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/70318) — rafaelaudibert / PostHog
A human-directed Claude Fable 5 audit found that PostHog’s toolbar had a safe request layer but still let ordinary 4xx/5xx responses, network failures, malformed bodies, ad blockers, and CSP restrictions reach error tracking through several higher-level paths. The resulting patch makes transport failures observable through toolbar logs and capture events while reserving exception tracking for actual toolbar bugs, introduces a tagged `ToolbarRequestError` where UI state needs failure handling, and routes remaining raw fetches through the safe layer. It adds three ERROR-level Semgrep rules to lock in that distinction; the PR reports 500 passing tests across 21 affected suites, five Semgrep fixtures, and a clean frontend scan, while frontend typechecking was still running.

### [Delivered medication reminders stop retaining plaintext](https://github.com/korjavin/medicationtrackerbot/pull/626) — korjavin
Two Fable 5 security-review findings led this medication-tracker patch to erase the medication name, dose, and callback payload in the same database update that marks a Telegram reminder delivered. The product also changes the default notification verbosity from detailed to generic, so users must explicitly opt into name-bearing messages; a new store test confirms that delivered rows no longer retain the payload. The author reports 3,545 frontend tests and the focused Go cloudstore tests passing, and the open PR’s lint, secrets-scan, Docker build, server tests, and all four frontend test shards are green.

### [A photo manager blocks directory escapes before deleting files](https://github.com/C-Hipple/camera_rip/pull/10) — C-Hipple
In Camera Rip, a request’s `directory` field was joined to the photo root without checking that the result remained inside it, so `../../Documents` could escape the root; the delete endpoint then made arbitrary host-file deletion possible. A Claude Fable 5 co-authored patch centralizes the containment check and applies it to browse, save, import, export, thumbnail, and delete paths, with extra filename validation at copy boundaries. The new regression test covers the escape cases, and the public full-app, Nix, Go-backend, and React-frontend checks all passed.

### [A Workflow JSON array reaches Fable’s script as a string](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/76906) — akl773
A Claude Code user running Fable 5 found that the Workflow tool accepts a real JSON array in its `args` field but delivers that value to the script as a JSON-encoded string. Scripts that immediately call `args.map` or `args.filter` therefore fail only at runtime, after meta and phase validation have passed; the report includes the exact call, TypeError, and the failure notification’s stringified recovery hint. Until the harness preserves the documented value type, the reporter’s workaround is to parse `args` defensively when it arrives as a string.

### [Feature and audit routines become test-gated Claude Code skills](https://github.com/DanielLourencoFil/agentic-harness/pull/5) — Daniel Lourenço Filho
Claude Fable 5 co-authored an agentic-harness change that packages a feature workflow, a read-only audit workflow, and a kickoff interview as reusable Claude Code skills instead of prompts copied into each session. The feature path starts with a specification interview and negative tests; the audit agent is limited to read/search tools and requires a concrete reproduction for every finding, while the project’s “Layer 0” rule makes plans views over named tests rather than a second source of truth. The pull request’s self-test adds a guard that the skills exist and the auditor remains read-only, and both public self-test variants passed.

## 2026-07-13

### [A 753B mixture model keeps all 256 experts on four GPUs](https://github.com/lukealonso/b12x/pull/31) — MadeBy561
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored b12x change adds an NF3 three-bit format for the low-saliency tail of a W4A16 MoE, so a GLM-5.2 753B deployment can retain all 256 experts per layer instead of pruning them. The Fable-attributed planner correction records 238 registers rather than the packed layout's 255 and accounts for the 12-byte NF3 staging units; the PR also adds a three-case numerical-reference battery. The author reports that the 326GB hybrid on four RTX PRO 6000 GPUs has run in production since July 5, with GPQA-Diamond at 176/198, 100% argmax agreement against a Triton oracle, and 92–104 tokens/second single-stream decode with MTP5.

### [Live fixtures expose a forum API dropping sticky favorites](https://github.com/ForumHFR/redface2/pull/922) — XaaT
A Claude Fable 5 action on the Redface2 Android client reproduced a production REST mismatch: a sticky topic favorited inside a subcategory vanished from the favorites endpoint even though the category-level recent-topics response still carried its flag. The branch preserves both live responses as fixtures, expands the compensating lookup from only no-subcategory groups to every category, and adds regression coverage. It also makes a single-quote reply fail retryably when the forum returns an apparently successful but empty quote prefill, rather than silently sending a reply without the quoted block; the public assemble, test, lint, Detekt, and repository-guard jobs passed.

### [A seven-phase audit repairs an Omeka heritage-site theme](https://github.com/fmadore/IWAC-theme/pull/8) — fmadore
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored audit of the IWAC Omeka theme turned into a 98-file repair across correctness, accessibility, localization, performance, and build hygiene. It fixes fatal null paths and a French pagination-state bug, makes the navigation drawer inert and focus-contained, generates 56 current French UI translations without gettext binaries, and replaces a hierarchy block's potential 80 API reads for a 40-node tree with two batched searches. The performance pass sends a 57KB 768px banner candidate instead of 110–170KB hero assets for a short decorative strip, while the new CI path rebuilds generated artifacts and fails on drift; its public freshness-verification check passed.

### [Twenty-three hidden-test tasks put Fable at 87% across effort levels](https://github.com/morganlinton/VulcanBench/pull/17) — Morgan Linton / VulcanBench
VulcanBench's new three-way run put Claude Fable 5, Grok 4.5, and GPT-5.6 Sol through 23 real post-cutoff PR tasks, graded by deterministic hidden tests in a network-isolated Docker sandbox. Fable solved 20 of 23 at both low and high effort (87.0%), versus 19 at medium, while using 28,000–44,000 tokens per task but costing $0.61–$1.04 per solved task at the benchmark's listed pricing. The result includes an important qualifier: six of Fable's 69 calls were rejected by its cyber classifier and completed by an explicit Opus 4.8 fallback; all six passed. The repository publishes the traces, final patches, replay HTML, task-level failure map, and exact run command.

### [Julia’s Lemke solver reaches zero allocations with caller workspace](https://github.com/QuantEcon/QuantEcon.jl/pull/399) — oyamad / QuantEcon
A Claude Code session using Fable 5 produced an optimization for QuantEcon.jl's preallocating Lemke linear-complementarity solver. Supplying the new `d`, `col_buf`, and `argmins` workspaces changes the reported n=10 path from 350ns and 12 allocations on master to 330ns, zero allocations, and zero bytes on Julia 1.12; default calls still preserve the old API while avoiding several unnecessary temporary allocations. The PR adds a benchmark case and regression tests for the allocation-free and trivial paths, and explains why the goal is GC-tail reduction in repeated solve loops rather than headline single-call speed. Its cross-platform Julia matrix was still running when the report was filed.

### [One record destructure removes a 130x Lean meshing bottleneck](https://github.com/petergpt/lean-3d-game-axiom/pull/2) — spete
In a Lean voxel game, Fable identified that building a new mesh record from projections of a still-live record kept each growing array shared, turning supposedly in-place pushes into full-array copies. Destructuring the record first makes the arrays uniquely referenced; on the author's low-spec notebook, synchronous full-island meshing fell from 12.7–19.3 seconds to 114–127ms, and the retained parallel case from about 17 seconds to 53–59ms. The report adds a three-seed startup microbenchmark, retains exact seam-vertex acceptance checks, and says the final-sha macOS build passed alongside interactive mining and placement QA.

### [TabArena moves one-time setup outside the benchmark clock](https://github.com/autogluon/tabarena/pull/441) — Lennart Purucker / TabArena
A Fable 5 co-authored TabArena change addresses a benchmark-design problem rather than an individual model score: fresh processes charge every fit for imports, JIT/kernel compilation, and CUDA-context startup that a long-lived deployment does not repeat. The new opt-in warm-up hook runs before fitting is timed, may use configuration but not task data, records its duration separately, and falls back to a cold fit if warm-up fails. It adds model-class dispatch for PyTorch, LightGBM, CatBoost, XGBoost, EBM, FastAI, and other heavy paths, plus focused tests; the public build, lint, install, CodeQL, and static-analysis checks passed.

### [A live Trac query restores fifty components](https://github.com/Automattic/mcp-context-wporg/pull/32) — Chris Huber / Automattic
Working under Chris Huber's direction, an OpenCode subagent orchestrated by Claude Fable 5 replaced a grouped CSV scrape that missed WordPress Trac components with parsing of the live `/query` form. An authenticated provider call returned 50 components, including Abilities API, Editor, and REST API; the merged change caches a successful parse for five minutes and retains a static fallback for outages. Fixture tests cover HTML decoding, cache reuse, and network failure, while the author also reports passing tests, typecheck, and build.

### [Twenty-five Fable sessions put verification gaps below an older Opus cohort](https://github.com/Kabilan108/dotfiles/issues/9) — Kabilan108
A local usage-analysis audit segmented 2,317 primary sessions by model and harness, including 25 Claude Fable sessions. It found a check command was absent after writes in 8% of the Fable runs, versus 18% across 529 Claude Opus 4.x sessions; repeated-command loops appeared in 4% of Fable runs. The author treats the Fable sample as too small to justify a new commit-gating hook and plans to revisit it at roughly 150 sessions.

### [Five encounter-state no-ops become state transitions](https://github.com/azerothcore/azerothcore-wotlk/pull/26582) — Nyeriah
A Claude Code session using Fable 5 found that five Algalon encounter-state updates in AzerothCore's Ulduar scripts called a `SetData` path with no boss-ID case, leaving the state at `NOT_STARTED`. The proposed five-line repair uses `SetBossState`, matching TrinityCore's corresponding implementation so achievements, room-door behavior, reset flow, and persisted completion can receive real state changes. Linux build and integration jobs are green, but the author explicitly reports that the change has not yet been tested in-game.

### [Bedrock vision calls fail despite raw Fable requests working](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/62965) — notnonot
A Hermes user isolated a Fable 5 integration failure to the auxiliary-client path rather than their Bedrock account or the model's vision support. On the same account, region, and image, direct `boto3` Messages and Converse calls to `global.anthropic.claude-fable-5` returned correct image descriptions, while Hermes `vision_analyze` returned HTTP 400 about an unsupported `data retention mode`; the same error also broke its compaction summarizer. Plain-text Fable traffic and Claude Code image handling worked, and pinning auxiliary vision to Sonnet restored production use, pointing at an extra request field or header in Hermes rather than a general Fable outage.

### [Fallback leaves Fable subagents labeled as Opus](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/76607) — yscheef
After a Claude Code session fell back from Fable 5 to Opus 4.8, the reporter explicitly launched a background subagent with the Fable override. The spawn confirmation named Fable, and every assistant message in the subagent transcript was stamped `claude-fable-5`, but the task list displayed the parent’s post-fallback model, Opus, beside a 113.5k-token task. Their diagnosis is specific: fan entries store an id, kind, label, and start time but no resolved model, so the renderer inherits mutable parent state and can hide a successful model override.

### [A Fable session exposes a false 1M entitlement error](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/76369) — RubyTeng007
On a Claude Max account, RubyTeng007 tested nine model-selection combinations on Claude Code 2.1.206 across macOS and Linux. A bare Fable session rejected mid-session switches to Opus `[1m]` as unavailable, while startup with the same Opus variants succeeded; an earlier 1M Fable session could switch, and a separate Opus session reached 812k context tokens. A two-day follow-up made the diagnosis more awkward: the same mid-session check now rejected even after a fresh Fable `[1m]` session, without a local client change, while direct startup still worked—evidence that the entitlement gate was serving a server-side answer inconsistent with the API path.

### [An inbound agent blocks DNS and redirect SSRF paths](https://github.com/tiXor-code/icp-agent/pull/1) — tiXor-code
A Fable 5-generated security patch for an inbound lead-enrichment agent starts with a reproducible route from an unauthenticated webhook to an attacker-controlled scrape request and reflected internal response text. It adds a public-address allow policy for IPv4, IPv6, and mapped addresses; validates every redirect; pins the connection to the address it inspected; rejects protocol downgrades; and makes a missing production webhook secret return 503 instead of accepting requests. The PR includes classifier, loopback/link-local, scheme, and pinned-connection tests, and the author reports 23 Vitest cases plus a clean TypeScript check; it remains open, with only the Vercel preview check publicly recorded so far.

### [A 1M Fable session was reported as 106% full](https://github.com/aaronsb/agent-ways/pull/360) — Aaron Bockelie
Aaron Bockelie found that agent-ways had three stale context-window heuristics: a Claude Fable 5 session running past 212,899 tokens was shown as a 200K window at 106%, despite continuing without compaction. That fivefold error also shortened the tool’s window-relative agent refire schedule and distorted a context-threshold trigger. The proposed shared resolver returns both the window and whether it came from an override, model table, or conservative default; the author reports the same Fable session now reads 1M and 23%, and the open PR’s Rust tests plus Linux, macOS, and Windows builds are green.

### [A Windows admin app completes a Native AOT cutover](https://github.com/LuicdLabs/OneMMC/pull/2) — Editird
A migration assisted by Claude Fable 5 and Opus 4.8 made Native AOT the default deployment path for OneMMC, replacing reflection-dependent management, directory, performance-counter, and late-bound COM code. The author documents 175 dynamic-over-COM sites, 121 `System.Management` sites, 467 MMI sites, and about 925 XAML bindings as the starting blockers, then reports a 70.4MB, 13-file native release instead of a 224.4MB, 276-file ReadyToRun package. Debug and Release builds and AOT publishes completed with zero warnings, and a UI automation smoke run reached all seven top-level pages without logged errors; domain-joined ADSI paths remain a declared follow-up.

### [An apartment app closes two redirect paths and adds CI gates](https://github.com/dat31/apartments/pull/58) — dat31
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored Next.js 16 audit found that the app accepted both absolute and protocol-relative `next` targets during sign-in and email confirmation. The repair funnels both through a shared internal-path validator, adds cache invalidation after listing writes, replaces hydration guards that triggered four React Compiler lint errors, and installs a root error boundary. Its public Vercel, GitHub Actions lint/typecheck, and GitGuardian checks passed.

### [A 429 masquerades as a silent Fable advisor hang](https://github.com/ViperJuice/agent-harness/issues/196) — ViperJuice
An agent-harness author ran four small Fable 5 advisor boards where the Fable leg showed no PTY output, almost no CPU use, and no network traffic while other model legs completed. A direct stream-JSON invocation exposed the actual terminal event immediately: an HTTP 429 with `seven_day` limit type, zero input and output tokens, and a rejected overage status. The report locates the failure in the adapter’s handling of that structured provider response, and proposes reporting it as a typed unavailable leg instead of leaving the board to infer a dead process.

### [A quantum simulator turns 16-qubit losses into measured wins](https://github.com/glanzz/simq/pull/78) — glanzz
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored SimQ repair began with a cross-validated benchmark showing the Rust simulator 22–30 times slower than Qiskit Aer at 16 qubits. The branch fixed a sparse-to-dense conversion that never triggered, a qubits-versus-amplitudes threshold error, tiny Rayon tasks, repeated full-state telemetry scans, and precision loss from amplitude truncation; its reproducible harness checks 12 observables against Qiskit to `1e-12` before printing timings. On the author’s four-vCPU Xeon container, the resulting 16-qubit VQE and QAOA workloads measured 1.6 times faster than Aer, though Aer remains 3.4 times faster at 28 qubits; macOS tests and the security/doc checks passed, while the PR’s separate coverage check failed.

### [A real GPU research loop beats its human-tuned baseline](https://github.com/hanifridho62-rgb/Lintester-project/pull/50) — hanifridho62-rgb
A Fable 5-powered autonomous-research setup was exercised on a 6GB RTX 3050 under WSL rather than simulated. Three acceptance runs exposed practical failures: missing C compiler support for Triton, a startup-and-compilation overhead that turned a nominal five-minute run into 432 seconds, an OOM-prone batch setting, and a workspace boundary that initially prevented the research agent from editing `train.py`. The merged repair records the agent halving total batch size, then reports `val_bpb` 1.5858 against a 1.8587 human-tuned baseline; 16 new tests and all four public Ubuntu/Windows test jobs passed, while the author notes that the headless agent still wastes a turn narrating a permission question.

### [A zero-allocation Lemke–Howson solver for repeated game solves](https://github.com/QuantEcon/GameTheory.jl/pull/237) — oyamad / QuantEcon
Claude Code with Fable 5 produced a mutating `lemke_howson!` API for QuantEcon’s Julia game-theory library, letting callers provide result arrays and pivoting workspaces rather than allocating them per solve. The PR adds random-game benchmark fixtures that assert convergence before timing and tests that workspace arrays cannot alias in ways that would silently corrupt a pivot. The author reports 0 bytes per full-workspace call, with n=10 median time falling from 1.67µs in QuantEcon 0.17 to 0.96µs and n=100 from 2.74ms to 2.15ms; it also documents that the p99 tail remains algorithmic rather than GC-driven. The open PR’s Linux, macOS, Windows, nightly Julia, and documentation checks are green.

### [A complete HTTP/1 request body survives a client abort](https://github.com/honojs/node-server/pull/373) — BlankParticle
While investigating a v2 regression in `@hono/node-server`, BlankParticle built a server/client reproduction in which a client sends the full body, disconnects during asynchronous handler work, and then gets a misleading `TypeError: Body is unusable` when the handler reads it. They report that v2.0.8 failed every run while v1.19.14 returned the buffered body, and that a production-shaped Hono plus tRPC load test saw roughly 40 phantom errors per few thousand mutations at about 8% client aborts. Claude Fable 5 (medium) wrote the initial patch and test; after reviewing and manually testing it, the author revised it so a complete HTTP/1 buffer is drained while genuinely truncated input reports an abort error.

### [A Bedrock bearer token reaches Fable through Hermes](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/63650) — vinayshah1998
A Hermes contributor tested Anthropic SDK 0.88 against Bedrock in `us-east-1` with a bearer token and reports successful completions from both Claude Fable 5 and Sonnet 5. Their diagnosis is that 0.87’s Bedrock client only used SigV4 credentials, so the short-lived bearer-token path stopped at credential resolution; the proposed dependency bump keeps Hermes’s three related pins aligned. The same patch raises Fable’s static Bedrock context entry from the 128K fallback to 1M, though the author explicitly has not exercised a prompt beyond 200K tokens.

### [An E2EE journal audit finds two deployment-blocking gaps](https://github.com/plasticparticle/mneme/pull/39) — plasticparticle
Claude Fable audited Mneme’s Go relay, Preact client, crypto, sync, deployment configuration, and design documentation by reading every security-critical file and running two parallel deep dives. The resulting 305-line report rates the architecture B and opens 18 linked issues: it considers the XChaCha20-Poly1305/HKDF core, parameterized owner-scoped queries, and key handling sound, but says an attacker who learns an owner public key can bind a new device without owner-key authorization, and that the absence of CSP/security headers leaves in-memory vault keys exposed to any future script injection. The audit labels those two findings as work to finish before an internet-exposed deployment, then prioritizes link validation, orphaned-upload cleanup, rate limits, quotas, and stronger Argon2 settings.

### [Pixel Park gets a reusable rotation controller and retained drafts](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/ismaelmartinez.me.uk/pull/163) — IsmaelMartinez
A Claude Fable 5 session reworked Pixel Park’s canvas rendering at device-pixel resolution, added rotate-left/right controls and rotation-aware picking, and kept unfinished coaster drafts visible while players switch tools or terraform. A follow-up commit handles clicks during the flip animation, prevents conflicting track writes, fixes resolution scaling after resize, and extracts the controller for a second game rather than duplicating it. The 11-file change adds rotator unit tests, a rotation consistency property, and a terrain-height regression; its public validation, analysis, CodeQL, and Vercel preview checks passed.

### [A TypeScript checker repair clears 150 more conformance cases](https://github.com/tsz-org/tsz/pull/15762) — mohsen1
Using Claude Code with Fable 5 at max effort, mohsen1 traced a family of false TypeScript diagnostics to identity collisions after user interfaces merge into global library types. The merged fix makes four checker paths resolve library declarations consistently and adds regression coverage for script and `declare global` forms, generic and non-generic targets, DOM and core-only libraries, plus negative controls. The author reports 11,169 full-conformance passes—150 more than before—while the six remaining runner failures and 19 checker-library failures match main; the merged PR’s CI summary is green, although several individual matrix jobs were skipped.

### [A SIP warning header triggers an empty-copy stack overflow](https://github.com/irontec/sngrep/issues/550) — Ali-Dogan
After sngrep 1.8.3 repeatedly crashed on legitimate traffic from a Ribbon/Sonus SBC, Ali-Dogan used Claude Fable 5 to work through the failure despite not being a C developer. The report reduces it to `Warning: x`: an empty regex capture passes length zero into a helper that subtracts one, turning the copy length into `SIZE_MAX` and overflowing a 10-byte stack buffer; it includes a backtrace with the overwritten return address. The proposed guard skips empty matches and clamps numeric values, and the reporter says a local rebuild then parsed the offending headers without crashing; the issue remains open.

### [A 25-agent adversarial review reshapes a Next.js toolchain migration](https://github.com/LeeByeongMuk/nextjs-starter-kit/pull/155) — LeeByeongMuk
LeeByeongMuk ran a 25-agent Claude Fable 5 adversarial review over a broad Next.js maintenance branch, confirming 14 findings and incorporating all of them before a separate Codex pass found two more unique issues. The resulting open PR turns `.agents/` into the shared source for Claude and Gemini instructions, adds an FSD lint rule and pre-commit gates, fixes a recurrent Dependabot coverage failure, and updates the React, Next, Jest, and lint stacks. Its public build, lint, coverage, macOS and Ubuntu test, CodeQL, boundary, and title/branch-validation checks are green; the author still flags a post-merge Dependabot run as pending.

### [Queued tool timers begin only at execution](https://github.com/coder/mux/pull/3716) — ThomasK33 / coder
Claude Fable 5 at xhigh effort is attributed to a 22-file Mux repair for a misleading agent UI: tool timers had started when the model emitted parallel calls, even though their handlers were serialized and had not run. The patch records `executionStartedAt` only after the execution lock is acquired, keeps it through stream replay, and leaves queued tools showing a timeout rather than a fictitious elapsed time. Its new tests cover queued siblings, both stream-ordering races, and client aggregation; Linux, VS Code, macOS/Linux E2E, integration, smoke, static, and visual checks were green, while unit and macOS builds were still running.

### [A 2,796-zatoshi deposit disappears from listunspent](https://github.com/zcash/zallet/issues/594) — aphelionz / Zcash
A Zcash operator found that `z_listunspent` was reusing a coin-selection query with a 5,000-zatoshi marginal-fee floor, so it omitted real small UTXOs from RPC enumeration. Their public mainnet example has an unspent 2,796-zatoshi output that remained visible in both balances and `z_viewtransaction`, but not in `z_listunspent`, which can make deposit reconciliation look like missing funds. The reporter says Claude Fable 5 helped analyze and code a downstream repair that queries the small-output candidates separately, then applies the normal unspent, expiry, maturity, and confirmation checks.

### [A 576,000-cell tidal model reaches 23 milliseconds per step](https://github.com/tdamsma/Delft3D/pull/4) — tdamsma
A human-directed, Claude Fable 5-assisted Delft3D performance pass adds a public benchmark harness with warm-up/repeat discipline, verified step counts, RSS and power capture, machine-condition logs, and regression comparison. On the author’s M3 Ultra, its 576,000-cell tidal model measured about 23 ms per step on 24 MPI ranks; the matched Ryzen 3800X comparison took 434.3 seconds to the Mac’s 47.1 seconds for the same 2,054-step run. The report also records a 40-minute thermal soak at roughly 158 W and 3.66 J per timestep, while documenting that a background GPU job had inflated earlier sequential references by 9–24%.

### [Streamed tool calls recover their arguments](https://github.com/jyjeanne/crustly/pull/38) — jyjeanne
Crustly’s terminal agent was executing every streamed Anthropic tool request with `{}` because the service kept the empty `tool_use` block sent at stream start and discarded the later `input_json_delta` argument fragments. A Claude Fable 5 co-authored, merged repair accumulates and parses those fragments at the close of the block (and on a truncated-stream flush), with a regression test assembling a `read_file` call from split JSON. The same PR also makes the Qwen streaming path buffer and parse its complete response so embedded and OpenAI-style tool calls are no longer displayed as plain text; stable and beta Linux, macOS, and Windows builds, Clippy, formatting, and coverage checks are green.

### [OAuth loopback authentication gains PKCE and state checks](https://github.com/wienerdog-ai/wienerdog/pull/102) — thegyula
Wienerdog used a Claude workflow with Fable 5 as orchestrator for a hardening pass on its Google OAuth loopback flow. The merged change adds a random state value, ignores mismatched callbacks instead of letting them win the one-shot listener, sets a five-minute consent timeout, and uses an S256 PKCE verifier at token exchange. Six new tests exercise wrong, missing, matching, and error states plus timeout behavior; the author reports 755 passing tests, clean lint, and green macOS/Linux, boundary, and title checks.

### [A 25-agent asset-pipeline investigation is preserved for selective adoption](https://github.com/KyleMit/Splotch/pull/126) — KyleMit
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored Splotch session sent 25 subagents through every item in an image-asset pipeline’s ideas list, then froze the work as a reviewable experiment archive rather than landing speculative changes. The open PR reports 24 ideas working and one partial result, with per-idea reports, machine-readable metadata, before-and-after images, re-applicable patches, a dashboard, and gold snapshots; the main pipeline tree was restored after each experiment. Its test, E2E, and app-driver-smoke jobs pass, while the Quality job still fails at formatting after successful type and lint steps.

### [Symbolication stops library frames becoming application errors](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/70427) — hpouillot / PostHog
Claude Code running Fable 5 used PostHog’s MCP to inspect real error payloads and trace why native Rust crashes rendered tokio, hyper, and other dependency code as application frames. The proposed Cymbal repair only demotes frames after symbolication when their DWARF paths point into cargo registries, git checkouts, Rust toolchains, or vendor trees, preserving explicit client exclusions and leaving pathless frames unchanged; its regression test covers library, application, Windows, and pathless paths. The author reports 203 Cymbal library tests plus integration suites, clippy, and rustfmt, while the still-open PR has green test-selection and coverage-reporting gates.

### [A 252-line CRM audit turns into a seventeen-commit repair pass](https://github.com/shiinedev/crm-system/pull/1) — shiinedev
A Claude Fable 5 session began with a 252-line review of a multi-tenant CRM, identifying a build failure, an invalid ESLint configuration, missing database migrations, inert background jobs, unconfigured Google sign-in, and unbounded list queries. Over 17 commits and 146 changed files, the branch repaired the deploy blockers, added migrations and blocking CI, wired automation, lead-scoring, and reminder events, enforced per-user AI-chat rate limits and request-size caps, and added audit logging. It also replaces mouse-only task and deal boards with keyboard and touch-capable drag-and-drop, and caps or paginates database and AI-tool queries; the open PR’s public `checks` job is green.

### [A one-character regex repair restores macOS coordinate transforms](https://github.com/Fields2Cover/Fields2Cover/pull/223) — pascalzauberzeug
While porting Fields2Cover to macOS, a Claude Fable 5-authored fix traced every `Transform::transformToUTM` failure to a possessive `\d++` quantifier: libc++ rejects it under the ECMAScript grammar, although Linux’s libstdc++ accepts it. The patch changes it to equivalent `\d+` matching, and the author reports that the full C++ and Python suites passed on macOS 14 with Apple Silicon and AppleClang. The open PR has no GitHub check runs recorded yet.

### [An address-filtered Zcash query was returning every transparent UTXO](https://github.com/zcash/zallet/issues/595) — aphelionz / Zcash
While reconciling wallet addresses, a Zcash operator found that `z_listunspent` decoded its `addresses` argument but never applied it to transparent outputs, so a filtered request returned every transparent UTXO in the wallet. They had initially read the behavior as cross-address data corruption because the RPC documentation promises only outputs received by the supplied addresses. The linked downstream repair, co-authored with Claude Fable 5, collects transparent receivers from transparent, TEX, and unified addresses before querying and retaining matching outputs.

### [A multi-address Zcash filter returned no shielded notes](https://github.com/zcash/zallet/issues/596) — aphelionz / Zcash
The same `z_listunspent` investigation found a second filter bug: Sapling and Orchard code required every requested address to match a note, making any two-address query return zero shielded notes. The reporter reduced the semantic error to `all` versus `any`, while preserving the existing empty-filter behavior. A linked downstream Claude Fable 5 patch changes both pool filters to `is_empty() || any(...)`.

### [Cancelled route segments no longer get scanned](https://github.com/cafein-py/cafein/pull/129) — HTenkanen
Cafein’s McTBTR transit-routing kernel could keep queued segments alive after a newly admitted trip-bag entry had already dominated them. Claude Fable 5 and Codex jointly assisted a merged repair that tracks each segment as pending, scanned, or cancelled, skips only cancelled pending work, and asserts that no cancelled segment appears in a reconstructed destination path. Six focused tests cover the cancellation cases and exhaustive-oracle parity; the author reports 176 core tests, the full pytest suite, and identical output frames on a large profiling workload, while the public macOS, Windows, Ubuntu, and lint checks are green.

### [A check backfill CLI refuses to disturb active applies](https://github.com/block/schemabot/pull/714) — aparajon
Claude Fable 5 wrote a SchemaBot command for finding missing and stuck GitHub check runs across a repository fleet without first crawling an app-wide delivery history. It can bound a sweep to recently updated pull requests, pace itself against the installation’s remaining API budget, and show progress from GitHub’s own open-PR count; absent checks are recreated only when no active apply could own the head. The pull request includes renderer examples for a 214-PR dry run and JSON output for scripting, alongside a 15-file implementation whose visible build, unit, integration, E2E, security, and lint checks are green.

### [A hook installer stops overwriting malformed settings](https://github.com/nangchang/DevIsland/pull/383) — nangchang
A Claude Fable 5-generated DevIsland repair changes the Codex and Gemini hook installers from silently replacing malformed JSON with an empty object to failing while preserving the original file. The same pass makes the app-menu installation path copy and remove its Codex auto-review wrapper just as the shell installer does, prevents a forced unwrap when macOS reports no screens, and documents known limits of literal deny-prefix matching. The author added malformed-settings and localization-golden coverage, reports the full test script and Xcode build succeeding, and the public Build & Test check is green.

### [A racing export test falls from five seconds to half a second](https://github.com/Code-and-Sorts/chirp-ai-note-app/pull/135) — colbytimm
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored Chirp repair traced a concurrent transcript-export test’s slowdown to an unthrottled mutator that made each of 200 exports traverse an ever-growing list and left a daemon thread running after a five-second timeout. It bounds and periodically truncates the real segment fixture, yields the mutator, asserts both worker threads terminate, and checks that callers receive a stable snapshot rather than a mutable internal list. The author reports the full suite falling from 103 to 31 seconds of wall time and the race test from 5.0 to 0.5 seconds; CodeQL and quality checks pass, while the macOS build matrix is still running.

### [A seven-package type migration adds a CI gate](https://github.com/The-Focus-AI/umwelten/pull/256) — wschenk
After `ai@7` renamed `CoreMessage` to `ModelMessage`, Umwelten had roughly 44 TypeScript errors spread across its monorepo even though CI only ran tests and lint. A Claude Fable 5 co-authored migration updates 38 TypeScript files plus Discord and Telegram adapters, raises package targets to ES2022, fixes a few unrelated typing drifts, and runs `tsc --noEmit` across all seven packages before lint. The author reports zero remaining TypeScript errors and 670 affected tests passing; the public test check is green, while two pre-existing flaky session/JSON failures are documented as unchanged.

### [Subagents stop losing their Fable provider](https://github.com/DJRHails/pi-interactive-subagents/pull/9) — DJRHails
A Pi orchestration user found that a parent running through `anthropic-api` could spawn a subagent with either `claude-fable-5` or `anthropic/claude-fable-5` and still hit “No API key found for anthropic.” Pi’s built-in model catalog was resolving both forms to its keyless `anthropic` provider before extensions loaded, rather than the parent’s configured provider. The proposed repair rewrites only bare Claude-family IDs and the built-in `anthropic/` form to the parent provider, leaves explicit correct providers and non-Anthropic parents untouched, and adds seven focused tests; the author reports 136 passing tests in an isolated environment.

### [Bedrock token counting skips a Fable call that always fails](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/70515) — cvolzer3 / PostHog
PostHog’s gateway team analyzed 30 days of production CountTokens traffic and found that AWS Bedrock Runtime rejected Claude Fable 5 and Opus 4.8 on every call with “The provided model doesn't support counting tokens.” The Fable-assisted change replaces an ID-shape heuristic with a narrow, verified denylist, sends those models straight to Bedrock Mantle’s Anthropic endpoint, and adds a live probe that checks the mapping against AWS before it is edited. The report also records an important limitation: under the account’s data-retention mode, the Mantle request still rejects Fable 5, so this removes a wasted round trip and log error rather than restoring token counting.

### [A transit router shadows a rejection cache before turning it on](https://github.com/cafein-py/cafein/pull/130) — HTenkanen
A Claude Fable 5 and Codex-assisted measurement pass on Cafein’s McTBTR transit router instruments the stop-bag dominance checks that dominate closure sweeps, while keeping ordinary runs behaviorally unchanged. It shadows a four-witness rejection cache against the real bag, records scan depth, bag sizes, cache hits, and false positives, and includes a randomized 20,000-operation differential test that requires identical decisions and final bag snapshots. The author reports zero shadow-cache false positives in two-way and four-way diagnostic runs, 182 core tests plus the full pytest suite passing, and green public lint and macOS, Windows, and Ubuntu test matrices.

### [Paperclip’s slowest CI shard drops from 153 to a projected 113 seconds](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/pull/9516) — devinfoley
Using Claude Fable 5 with extended thinking and Claude Code tools, Paperclip replaced round-robin partitioning for its three server-test runners with deterministic longest-processing-time partitioning based on a checked-in manifest of 240 real suite durations. A recent run had split the work into 73-, 153-, and 115-second groups; the new dry-run projects three roughly 113-second groups, cutting about 40 seconds from the critical path without omitting tests. The PR adds coverage for skewed fixtures, determinism, stale manifests, completeness, and real partition balance; its local targeted tests passed while the full CI matrix was still running.

### [Scheduled agents can now run with no history](https://github.com/mindroom-ai/mindroom/pull/1506) — basnijholt
A Claude Fable 5 agent implemented per-schedule context caps in Mindroom, so a recurring task can receive the last N messages, no history at all, or the legacy full thread. The change persists the setting in Matrix state, carries it through natural-language creation and edits, limits both Matrix history and persisted agent replay, and refuses a spoofed cap on an ordinary user message. The author reports 9,757 tests passing and 148 skipped, with focused scheduling and dispatch tests accounting for 125 passes; the public test, build, smoke, scan, and review checks are green.

### [A live Google SSO 404 traced to one missing deploy variable](https://github.com/chase-sets/chase-sets/pull/5159) — todd-skelton
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored diagnosis ruled out DNS and ingress routing for Chase Sets’ DOKS admin host by rendering the Helm chart and curling the live service, then traced the 404 to `ADMIN_GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_HOSTED_DOMAINS` never reaching the Kubernetes `platform-api` process. The repair passes that value through both staging and production workflow deployments and adds regression guards around the exact generated command. The author reproduced the current 404 against the live host, reports 99 targeted tests and a clean typecheck, and notes that a subsequent deployment is still required before the redirected SSO flow is live.

### [One travel-skills plugin works across Claude Code and claude.ai](https://github.com/GruntworkAI/gruntwork-travel-skills/pull/5) — GruntworkAI
GruntworkAI used Claude Fable 5 on Claude Code to turn its flight- and lodging-to-calendar skills into a single marketplace plugin for both Claude Code and the consumer claude.ai app. The implementation keeps one schema but reads configuration through a four-rung ladder—plugin settings, a persistent Claude Code data directory, a pasteable `# ttc-config v1` blob, or an interview—and never writes the plugin cache. The merged 26-file change includes a browser config builder whose emitted blob is round-tripped through the real scripts, tests that pin the instruction-to-schema mapping, 103 reported pytest passes, and a safe zero-finding plugin audit; its author also records a few accepted local-shell and manual-install limitations.

### [A cross-platform co-op game gets a long netplay hardening pass](https://github.com/cradle/newtonia/pull/323) — cradle
Newtonia’s open Fable 5 branch is a 158-file effort to add peer-to-peer co-op across its desktop, web, Android, iOS, Steam, and Xbox targets. The commit record ties implementation to two-device playtests and executable rigs: it fixes touch and soft-keyboard flows, host-loss/rejoin behavior, stale state after network blackouts, remote audio and effects, TURN/signaling abuse limits, and several protocol-versioned simulation errors. The author reports loopback self-tests, room/rejoin/impact and loss-recovery scripts, plus public successful builds for Web, Android, Linux, macOS, Windows, and Xbox; the iOS build was still running when inspected.

### [Finite-element basis transformations derived from dual bases](https://github.com/firedrakeproject/fiat/pull/259) — Peter Brubeck
Firedrake developer Peter Brubeck credits Claude Fable 5 as the AI tool behind an open FIAT change that derives Kirby’s `V = E Vᶜ D` basis transformation from an element’s dual basis instead of maintaining element-specific implementations. The 16-file branch introduces `finat/zany.py` and applies the machinery to Morley, Hermite, Argyris, Bell, Mardal–Tai–Winther, Johnson–Mercier, and Guzmán–Neilan elements, including mapped variants. New automation tests cover functional construction, conditioning scaling, and unsupported scalar and Piola mappings; FIAT’s docs job and Python 3.11–3.14 builds on Ubuntu and macOS are all green.

### [An OpenAPI repair removes phantom pagination from a PostHog MCP tool](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/70566) — danielcarletti / PostHog
PostHog found that a Django action returned a raw JSON array but its OpenAPI schema advertised a paginated envelope, so the generated frontend client expected a nonexistent `.results` field and the MCP tool exposed bogus `limit`, `offset`, and `search` parameters. A Claude Fable 5-assisted patch fixes the declaration at the source, regenerates the types, and deletes the handwritten client that had been masking the mismatch. A fresh-eyes review also caught an initially wrong project-versus-team ID in multi-environment routing; the author reports focused Django, Jest, MCP-snapshot, TypeScript, Ruff, and preflight checks passing locally while the larger CI matrix is still running.

### [Two binary combinators cut Nix metadata-evaluation work](https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/pull/537659) — siraben
In Nixpkgs, every in-tree metadata union and intersection had exactly two members, but its generic implementation allocated a closure and invoked a primop for each verification. A Claude Fable 5-assisted change replaces those six call sites with direct `either` and `both` combinators, reducing function calls by 10.4% and primop calls by 20.9% in a 5,000-attribute CI evaluation; a pinned-core `perf` run measured 2.81% fewer user-space instructions, reproducible to ±0.05%. The PR says output remained byte-identical, 407 library tests passed, and Nixpkgs’ cross-platform evaluation, build, lint, and ownership checks are green.

### [A camera shader stops JPEG blocks from becoming foliage spots](https://github.com/renardoberou/spectral-camera/pull/17) — renardoberou
Field testing of a spectral-camera shader found JPEG chroma blocks were tripping narrow vegetation, sky, skin, and water classifiers, creating hard patchwork in bark and foliage. The Claude Fable 5 co-authored repair feeds those classifiers an eight-tap luma-bilateral average while retaining full-detail tone; on simulated JPEG foliage it reduced spatial noise below the clean-source level and improved classification MAE from 0.038 to 0.034. The same patch caps a saturation boost that had clipped 34% of pixels in a red-garden sample, adds a murky-water case, and makes halation depend on local contrast; assembled GLES 100 shaders passed `glslangValidator`, though device testing remains required.

### [Fixed-point kernels for elementary functions](https://github.com/flintlib/flint/pull/2778) — Fredrik Johansson / FLINT
Fredrik Johansson says he used mainly Claude Fable 5, with some Opus 4.8, to turn rough code from a 2024 paper into FLINT’s new fixed-point elementary-function module: 76 changed files and 29,687 additions covering `exp`, `log1p`, `atan`, trigonometric functions, generated tuning tools, stress tests, and documentation. The public profiles put the new `exp` path at 1.9–4.4× the existing Arb implementation across the listed precision range, while `atan` ranges from 1.3× to 4.1×; Johansson cautions that the comparison favors the new code at low precision and that its rigorous error bounds need another careful audit. The merged PR’s Linux, Windows, macOS/ARM, sanitizer, regression, and build checks are green.

### [Physics-data readers gain constituent and index dimensions](https://github.com/ESCOMP/CAM-SIMA/pull/521) — jimmielin / CAM-SIMA
A CAM-SIMA contributor credits Claude Fable 5 and Opus 4.8 with drafting an open 23-file change for climate-model initialization and physics data. It extends field reading and checking from existing two-dimensional cases to three-dimensional constituent dimensions and index dimensions such as `(ncol, 4)` dust-flux data, with corresponding Fortran and Python fixtures. GCC 12–14, Python 3.8–3.13, source-code, and FlexiMod jobs are green; a GPU job remains queued.

### [Idle teammates drop monitor events in agent teams](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/77300) — genesiscz
A Claude Code 2.1.206 user running a Fable 5 main session with Haiku and Sonnet teammates found that an idle teammate never wakes when its own `Monitor` watch fires or a background task finishes. In a controlled run, a watched line reached disk within one second, but the teammate’s transcript stayed empty for 95 seconds until a `SendMessage` woke it; the missed event was still absent from wake context, and production runs showed multi-ten-minute coordination gaps. Their workaround pairs every durable-channel message with a `SendMessage` nudge and has the awakened teammate poll the underlying file.

### [Concurrent desktop tabs wedge the Filesystem connector](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/77290) — moosepass
A Fable 5 high-effort user reports a reproducible Claude desktop failure when two tabs on different local projects access the Filesystem MCP connector at the same time. One tab proceeds while the other hits a roughly four-minute timeout mid-edit; the edit’s final state is unknown and later connector calls hang. Retrying reproduces the behavior, and the reporter says only restarting the app or toggling the connector restores it.

## 2026-07-14

### [A 350,000-recipient mailing retry stops starting from zero](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/pull/5865) — gumclaw / Gumroad
Gumroad used a Fable 5 agent on a reliability repair for a recent 350,000-recipient blast whose audience query repeatedly hit the database’s five-minute statement cap. The new path snapshots the resolved audience in Redis on the first attempt, then makes a retry re-run the original filters only for those members, so it can begin sending in seconds without mailing someone who has opted out or been refunded in the meantime. An adversarial Fable review found two first-draft P1s — role changes were not being revalidated and a worker death could leave a partial snapshot — and the final branch addresses both with role-aware rechecks, an atomic temporary-key rename, and seven new specs.

### [A dealer’s records become a cited, write-capable chat surface](https://github.com/cameron-cp/cwm-fine-art-app/pull/25) — cameron-cp
A Fable 5 co-authored branch adds a Registrar chat to a fine-art dealer application, built around the dealer’s existing records rather than a generic conversation layer. The agent can search works, ownership, parties, interests, relationships, holdings, and vault notes, then log a collector’s interest through the same schema used by the manual editor. The API is clerk-gated and Zod-validated, returns a record-citation trail alongside each reply, and leaves the chat with exactly one validated write tool.

### [Five investor-system failures repaired in a music-management game](https://github.com/ghostface2232/idolverse/pull/7) — ghostface2232
Idolverse used a Fable 5 co-authored repair pass to fix a collection of economy traps in its investor system: recurring penalties could make recovery impossible, one pressure flag could remain stuck forever, two conditions were permanently unwinnable because their metrics were hard-coded to zero, year-end checks reset at week 52, and a support-card mechanic could create unlimited income. The closed PR adds four-week warning periods and one-time penalties, persists condition state for existing saves, caps the support-card benefit, and uses accumulated weeks for annual deadlines. Follow-up commits add a Vitest baseline, golden weekly snapshots, an effect-key contract test, save round trips, and regression coverage for simultaneous penalty deadlines and pressure expiry.

### [Background deployment polls lose every completion signal](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/77335) — Sunnynah
During a WordPress-to-Next.js migration, a Claude Code user ran eight Fable 5 background Bash polls against Vercel deployments and found that none delivered a completion notification, even when their three-to-ten-minute timeouts elapsed. Each task’s output file remained zero bytes; one still appeared as running around fourteen hours later despite no matching process, while `TaskStop` already said its ID did not exist. The report supplies the command shape, task-file listing, versions, and expected state transitions, making this a concrete limitation for workflows that expect the model to resume after an asynchronous deploy check.

### [Dogfood finds four onboarding faults before users do](https://github.com/coder/mux/pull/3721) — ibetitsmike / Coder
A Fable 5 xhigh session dogfooding Coder’s Mux app on a fresh install found four UI failures: an 818px onboarding dialog that hid its Next button below a 720px viewport, raw Node permission errors shown to users, a 375px non-touch window reduced to roughly 90px of content, and an accessibility warning. The seven-file repair pins the wizard footer while scrolling its body, translates filesystem errors, makes narrow sidebars overlay rather than crush content, and adds integration coverage for error handling. The author re-ran each case in a development sandbox; the open PR’s Linux and macOS builds, Windows tests, integration and E2E jobs, unit tests, visual regression tests, and static checks are green.

### [Email addresses stop swallowing BibTeX imports](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/16245) — koppor / JabRef
Using Claude Code with Fable 5, a JabRef contributor traced a PDF-import failure to the verbatim BibTeX parser treating email-address `@` characters above an actual entry as malformed entries, then consuming the real entry’s opening marker. The fix begins at the first plausible `@type{` or `@type(` marker, and the author demonstrated it with a University of Stuttgart preprint whose first-page citation previously imported nothing. Two new tests cover page text on both sides of an entry and a no-BibTeX control; checkstyle, CodeQL, JabKit smoke, multiple unit suites, and database tests had passed while the larger jablib and installer jobs were still running.

### [A preview cache unpins an audio-plugin GUI](https://github.com/SuzukiDaishi/dispersion_equalizer/pull/10) — SuzukiDaishi
A Fable 5 co-authored fix for the Dispersion Equalizer audio plugin addresses an editor freeze observed in Bitwig on Windows: its per-frame preview compilation could perform roughly 100 million floating-point operations, leaving an idle GUI thread at 100% CPU. The change caches the preview by its curve-affecting inputs and recomputes only when they change, preserving the existing DSP algorithm, latency, and fit output. The two-file PR is open, but its Linux, Windows, and macOS-universal build jobs are all green.

### [A Fable audit finds unsafe installer boundaries](https://github.com/andr-ca/agentharness/pull/31) — malandr
Claude Fable 5 reviewed the full agentharness repository at a fixed commit and scored it 7.2/10, finding that generated client adapters had already drifted enough to fail the project’s offline gate. Its report also identified three concrete safety boundaries: client files overwritten with no backup or force switch, a checkout-based npm install that can copy untracked `.env` files, and forced hook installation that cannot restore a previous `hooksPath`. The filing PR regenerates the drifted adapters, while the visible fixture, integration, Python, shellcheck, manifest, link, and package checks passed; the content-quality job was still failing and hook tests remained in progress when inspected.

### [A five-tool MCP request clears Fable, not the auth stack](https://github.com/samyak-jain/hermes-agent/pull/6) — samyak-jain
While diagnosing a production Claude runtime failure, a Hermes Agent maintainer ran the same Pro OAuth account through Claude Agent SDK 0.3.207 with Fable 5, a one-million-token context window, five host MCP tools, and a 13,000-token appended prompt. Both the minimal and bridge-shaped requests succeeded, narrowing the remaining suspects to inherited provider overrides or a persisted credential copy rather than the model, context tier, SDK, or MCP surface. The resulting patch removes the inherited Anthropic authentication overrides and makes the configuration directory the runtime’s sole credential source; its full visible CI, including eight Python test slices, Docker builds, lint, E2E, and OSV checks, is green.

### [Blank manufacturer IDs match a label-printer PPD](https://github.com/OpenPrinting/system-config-printer/pull/448) — codewiz
A Phomemo PM-241-BT reports a blank manufacturer field, so system-config-printer had discarded its PPD during indexing and fallen back to `textonly.ppd`. A Claude Fable 5-assisted two-line change now indexes blank-manufacturer PPDs under the empty make while still requiring a non-empty model. On Fedora 45, the author verified that system-config-printer, KDE’s printer settings, and GetBestDrivers all select the correct PPD with the `exact-cmd` fit.

### [A Fable compatibility audit finds two production context failures](https://github.com/developerz-ai/ai-task-master/issues/196) — OGtwelve
AI Task Master’s maintainer ran behavioral probes against the real Claude Code Fable 5 harness, audited four ported components, and completed seven `aitm start` sandbox runs after shipping the compatibility work. Although 902 core and 250 compatibility tests were green, the audit found that cumulative AI SDK message lists caused both O(N²) transcript duplication and a compactor that never fired in production. The report records eight earlier runtime-failure classes already repaired and tracks the two P0s alongside gaps in file reads, glob matching, hooks, skills, and background execution.

### [Four hundred randomized Go boards preserve a shared rules engine](https://github.com/Zakkenroller/goxira/pull/93) — Zakkenroller
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored Goxira refactor removed duplicated Go-game logic from two evaluation paths and three prompt-formatting sites, putting group, liberty, move, tactical-fact, and pre-move calculations behind shared modules. The author compared captures, atari, liberties, pre-move context, and problem-role inference across 400 randomized boards, reporting byte-identical outcomes against the old implementations; the only intended difference is the display arrow used in one principal-variation prompt. The merged 27-file change also has a successful redirect-rules check, while its header and changed-pages checks completed neutrally.

### [A service worker stops mixing a stale page with fresh scripts](https://github.com/furious-banana/film_proccessing/pull/23) — furious-banana
While extending a browser-based film-processing tool in a Claude Fable 5 session, its author found that GitHub Pages’ ten-minute HTTP cache could let a service-worker install freeze an old `index.html` beside new JavaScript. That mixed version crashed startup and left the folder browser’s Browse button unusable across restarts. The merged repair fetches installation assets with `cache: 'reload'`, adds an actionable recovery message for a partial startup, and extends the test suite to click the real Browse button.

### [Curtains stop becoming beauty products in FlowKit drafts](https://github.com/MFR-Marketing-Resources/my-flowkit-bosmax/pull/351) — farisdatosheikh
A live owner report found FlowKit pitching a “Skirting Table Top” as a daily beauty product and losing its staged knowledge when a draft was rebuilt. The Claude Fable 5 co-authored repair replaces substring demographic and category checks with word-boundary matching, limits beauty copy to genuine beauty items, and carries staged HUB enrichment into draft creation. The author reports 121 clean-worktree tests, including regressions for `langsir-not-beauty`, `women-not-abang`, retained beauty copy, and the enrichment merge; unrelated regional-model failures were already present on baseline.

### [A Fable reviewer gets an independent read-only lane](https://github.com/KzeroM/evidence-gated-multi-agent-operations/pull/3) — KzeroM
An evidence-gated multi-agent operations project used an independent Claude Fable 5 xhigh reviewer against a fixed commit during a residual-hardening pass. The patch moves medium- and high-risk reviewers into independent read-only execution and updates schemas, templates, threat-model material, and regression tests while replacing a deprecated JSON Schema resolver. The author reports that the independent Fable review and full local gates passed, and the visible GitHub validation check is green.

### [A model picker needs a second Fable selection](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/77374) — adamerose
A Windows user reports that Claude Code 2.1.208’s VS Code extension can show Fable as selected while the running model identifies itself as Opus. Clicking the already-selected Fable option produces a “Switched to claude-fable-5” message, after which the model identifies as Fable. The report calls this a regression and includes a video demonstration, making the selected-model label unreliable until an explicit reselection.

### [A transit router adds measurement without changing its inner loop](https://github.com/cafein-py/cafein/pull/132) — HTenkanen
Using Claude Fable 5 and Codex at xhigh reasoning, Cafein’s McRAPTOR work adds phase timers, label and bag counters, and diagnostic shadows for a planned batch gate while leaving production behavior untouched unless profiling flags are enabled. Its probed insertion path was compared with the original across 20,000 randomized operations under two slack settings, requiring identical decisions and ordered snapshots; an engine-level test also checks counter identities. The author reports 191 core tests, the full pytest suite, Clippy, and formatting passing, with one macOS test job and lint already green while most of the public matrix was still running.

### [Fable-directed subagents solve a Lean benchmark theorem](https://github.com/leanprover/lean-eval-submissions/issues/809) — Ian Klatzco and Rado Kirov
Klatzco and Kirov submitted a clean-room formal proof of the Burnside `p^a q^b` solvability theorem to Lean Eval, saying Claude Fable 5 orchestrated fresh Fable and Sonnet 4.8 implementer and adversarial-review subagents under human direction. The public evaluator fetched the pinned source tree, attempted its one matching benchmark problem, and marked it passed. The submission explicitly distinguishes its work from seven earlier leaderboard solutions.

### [A Fable review finds a build-parity hook was wrong in four ways](https://github.com/performlikemj/nbhd-united/pull/1208) — performlikemj
A maintainer building a development-environment parity hook asked Fable 5 to review the work and applied findings that reversed three misleading dependency-status banners, added a lock around concurrent rebuilds, and fixed a regex that silently skipped one of 174 pins. The report also describes the underlying failure: a local environment on dj-stripe 2.10.3 could not contain the migration-backed tables that CI had failed on, making its green test structurally meaningless. The author exercised the hook in four live worktree states and says the resulting branch has green frontend, backend, and configuration-smoke checks.

### [A trace receiver accepts both OTLP wire formats](https://github.com/otterscope/otterscope/pull/18) — DimitrovK
A merged Claude Fable 5 co-authored Otterscope change replaces a server stub with an OTLP/HTTP trace receiver that accepts protobuf and OTLP/JSON, handles gzip with a decompression cap, and returns protocol-appropriate 400, 413, 415, and 500 responses. It uses OpenTelemetry's `pdata` wire implementation rather than a generic JSON codec, and keeps storage behind a sink interface. A Pydantic AI chat/tool trace fixture and its protobuf twin are round-tripped under a staleness guard; the public Go check is green.

### [A stale second screen can no longer cancel a completed visit](https://github.com/wavespestcontrolfl/waves-customer-portal/pull/2739) — wavespestcontrolfl
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored hardening pass makes completed, cancelled, and skipped service visits one-way states: retries of the same state remain idempotent, while a later flip is rejected with a 409. It also makes the report-creation endpoint return the existing project rather than insert a duplicate, with a partial unique index and a race-safe create path. The author replayed 945 migrations against scratch Postgres and reports 126 tests across the touched routers; the public server and client checks are green.

### [A coverage gate stops ignoring most of the source tree](https://github.com/geminixiang/mikan/pull/86) — geminixiang
Mikan's old coverage check instrumented just 11 selected files, allowing regressions elsewhere in `src` to pass CI. A merged Fable 5 co-authored change expands the gate across eligible TypeScript source and adds tests that raise `read.ts` from 5% to 98% line coverage, `bash.ts` from 6% to 91%, and `store.ts` from 0% to 100%. The new cases include shell-metacharacter paths, real-shell escaping, timeout kills, output spills, concurrent log deduplication, and retry classification; Node 22 and 24 builds are green.

### [A Rust frp implementation catches up with its Go counterpart](https://github.com/viogus/frp-rs/pull/140) — viogus
A Claude Fable 5 co-authored, 15-file frp-rs branch implements Go frp 0.70.0 features including the `tls2raw` PROXY-header path, duplicate proxy and visitor rejection, an eight-endpoint dashboard API, seven-day traffic history, and per-user filtering. It also aligns four operational defaults, including a five-connection pool cap and 1,500-byte UDP packets, and adds safety and lock-ordering documentation around TUN FFI and client removal. The author reports 426 workspace tests and 55 compatibility tests passing with no Clippy warnings; the public build and compatibility matrix was still running when inspected.

### [A model upgrade stops failing on its first tool call](https://github.com/Automattic/studio/pull/4190) — youknowriad / Automattic
While moving Studio Code from GPT-5.5 to GPT-5.6 Sol, its Fable 5 session used a local logging proxy to capture the exact CLI request and bisect a `400 (no body)` failure against the WordPress.com AI proxy. The investigation found that every Studio Code turn attached tools, while the old Chat Completions route rejects function tools when reasoning is active. The resulting seven-file change uses the Responses API instead; the author verified live tool-call turns through the proxy, and the PR's CodeQL checks are green.

### [An OpenRouter route makes Fable agents stop after two tokens](https://github.com/ThirdKeyAI/Symbiont/issues/20) — jaschadub / Symbiont
In an end-to-end `symbi-codered hunt` run, Fable 5 through OpenRouter's Bedrock path made `pattern_scout`, `chain_builder`, and `reflector` each exit after one iteration with two output tokens and zero `store_finding` calls. Gemini 2.5 Pro and Claude Sonnet 4 on the same OpenRouter key emitted tool calls normally, while Fable worked directly through Anthropic. The report traces the failure to a client parser that discards thinking or redacted-thinking blocks and maps `stop_reason: refusal` to an ordinary empty stop, then proposes a distinct refusal result and a tool-aware fallback trigger.

### [A reading-study model plan checks its causal claims in code](https://github.com/dseinternational/language-reading-predictors/pull/303) — frankbuckley / DSE International
A Claude Code Fable 5 session drafted a sign-off-ready plan for time-lagged reverse-coupling analysis in a language-and-reading study, resolving which effects can be pre-specified rather than chosen by judgement. A committed NetworkX script reproduces every valid/invalid d-separation claim against the project DAG and a three-slice crossover model; it finds that the `WR → TE` and `WR → TR` paths can be adjusted, while other proposed reverse edges need roughly 13 covariates at a sample size near 54. The draft creates a primary model and a nested leave-one-out comparator, with Python, lint, spellcheck, and macOS checks all green.
